diff --git a/apps/game-godot/Render3DAdapter.gd b/apps/game-godot/Render3DAdapter.gd
index 4f7429e..1c4c3ac 100644
--- a/apps/game-godot/Render3DAdapter.gd
+++ b/apps/game-godot/Render3DAdapter.gd
@@ -24,17 +24,26 @@ extends RefCounted
 ## verbatim PR1b extraction (same math, same comments); parity is verified
 ## through the Godot harness fixtures (the render path has no unit tests).
 
-## Apex-direction offset for VehicleProxy3D's yaw assignment. Couples
-## DIRECTLY to the +90° X rotation baked into VehicleProxy3D/Mesh.transform
-## in main.tscn (the bake lives on the child mesh post-#301 RigidBody3D
-## migration): PrismMesh's local +Y apex maps to body-local +Z post-bake,
-## and this offset rotates that body-local +Z to world +X (vehicle forward
-## at sim_yaw=0). The lockstep startup assert that enforces the bake
-## precondition lives in `sim_driver._ready` (it reads
-## `_vehicle_3d_mesh.basis.y`). If the bake is ever removed or its sign
-## flipped, BOTH this constant and that assert must be updated in lockstep —
-## they encode the same contract from different sides.
-const APEX_BAKE_OFFSET_Y: float = PI / 2.0
+## Authored-forward yaw offset for the vehicle `.glb` (#567). The Kenney
+## car-kit model (`sedan-sports.glb`) is authored UPRIGHT and lying flat,
+## facing +Z — verified from the glb geometry: the `wheel-front-*` nodes sit
+## at +Z, `wheel-back-*` at -Z. So, unlike the retired PrismMesh wedge, the
+## car needs NO +90° X bake; this offset rotates the model's authored +Z
+## forward to world +X (vehicle forward at sim_yaw=0), so
+## `rotation.y = VEHICLE_BAKE_OFFSET_Y + yaw_world` aligns the car with the
+## sim heading. The value (PI/2) is unchanged from the prism era only by
+## coincidence — the wedge's post-bake forward was also body-local +Z.
+## A MODEL-GEOMETRY constant (the .glb's authored forward), same class as
+## `PLAYER_BAKE_OFFSET_Y` below — NOT an ADR-004 tuning value. If the demo car
+## is swapped for a model with a different authored forward, this is the single
+## place that changes (never ad-hoc signs in the sync loop). Verified on a live
+## drive — the first thing to check if the car faces the wrong way.
+const VEHICLE_BAKE_OFFSET_Y: float = PI / 2.0
+
+## Vehicle render-proxy `.glb` (#567; committed per-pack, see assets/README.md).
+## STATIC mesh (no skeleton/animation) — unlike the player rig, so no headless
+## guard is needed when it instantiates (see `setup_vehicle_proxy`).
+const VEHICLE_GLB_PATH: String = "res://assets/car-kit/sedan-sports.glb"
 
 ## PR1c player-render-proxy `.glb` (committed per-pack, see assets/README.md).
 const PLAYER_GLB_PATH: String = "res://assets/mini-characters/character-female-a.glb"
@@ -51,7 +60,7 @@ const PLAYER_GLB_PATH: String = "res://assets/mini-characters/character-female-a
 
 ## Player model authored-forward bake (fold #7, lc-codex) — a MODEL-GEOMETRY
 ## constant (the `.glb`'s authored forward at yaw 0), NOT a gameplay/feel tunable:
-## same class as `APEX_BAKE_OFFSET_Y` above (also a named const, unflagged), so it
+## same class as `VEHICLE_BAKE_OFFSET_Y` above (also a named const, unflagged), so it
 ## stays in code and does NOT belong in the ADR-004 tuning data. Kept as ONE named
 ## offset so the facing convention lives in a single place, never as ad-hoc signs
 ## in the sync loop or the aim math. Default 0 assumes Godot's -Z forward;
@@ -131,6 +140,49 @@ func setup_player_proxy(is_headless: bool) -> void:
 	_player_3d_proxy.add_child(visual)
 
 
+## Populate the VehicleProxy3D body's visual child once (#567). Mirrors
+## `setup_player_proxy` but with NO headless branch: the car `.glb` is a STATIC
+## mesh (no Skeleton3D / AnimationPlayer), so it carries none of the headless
+## anim-clock / black-frame / leak risk that forces the player to stub — loading
+## it headless is safe and keeps frame captures honest. A failed load (e.g.
+## `--import` not run) falls back to a BoxMesh stub with a push_error rather than
+## a null visual. The body (VehicleProxy3D) stays the sim-write transform target;
+## the Visual is a passive child that inherits the body transform, so the
+## `sync_vehicle` path is identical whether the `.glb` or the stub is mounted.
+## Idempotency guard mirrors `setup_player_proxy` (a defensive no-op today —
+## `_ready` does not re-fire — but removes the double-mount class cheaply).
+func setup_vehicle_proxy() -> void:
+	if _vehicle_3d_proxy.get_node_or_null("Visual") != null:
+		return
+	var visual: Node3D = null
+	var packed := load(VEHICLE_GLB_PATH) as PackedScene
+	if packed != null:
+		visual = packed.instantiate() as Node3D
+		if visual == null:
+			push_error(
+				(
+					(
+						"Render3DAdapter: vehicle .glb at %s instantiated a non-Node3D root; "
+						% VEHICLE_GLB_PATH
+					)
+					+ "falling back to the BoxMesh stub."
+				)
+			)
+	else:
+		push_error(
+			(
+				("Render3DAdapter: failed to load vehicle .glb at %s " % VEHICLE_GLB_PATH)
+				+ "(run a Godot --import?); falling back to the BoxMesh stub."
+			)
+		)
+	if visual == null:
+		var stub := MeshInstance3D.new()
+		stub.mesh = BoxMesh.new()
+		visual = stub
+	visual.name = "Visual"
+	_vehicle_3d_proxy.add_child(visual)
+
+
 ## One-time 3D camera setup (moved from `sim_driver._ready`). Sets the projection
 ## mode, then defers the pose (incl. FOV) to `_apply_camera_pose`. PR1c flips the
 ## projection ortho→PERSPECTIVE (ADR-029 §2.8); the pose math (FOV + distance-from-
@@ -204,22 +256,24 @@ func _apply_camera_pose(
 ## construction.
 ##
 ## Body Y = 0 (ground plane): the RigidBody3D body is the position target and
-## sits at the ground plane; the wedge half-thickness offset (Y=0.25) lives
-## in mesh-local space on VehicleProxy3D/Mesh and is set once at .tscn load.
-## Writes go to `global_position` (not `position`) so the math is robust to a
-## future non-identity transform on Render3D (kinematic RigidBody3D's
-## physics-server sync follows global_transform).
+## sits at the ground plane; the car `.glb` is authored to rest on the ground at
+## its own origin (Kenney kit convention), so the Visual child mounts at the body
+## origin with no extra Y offset (#567; the retired PrismMesh's Y=0.25 half-
+## thickness offset is gone with the wedge). Writes go to `global_position` (not
+## `position`) so the math is robust to a future non-identity transform on
+## Render3D (kinematic RigidBody3D's physics-server sync follows global_transform).
 ##
 ## Yaw: the basis remap sim(X,Y,Z) -> world(X,Z,Y) is an ODD permutation, so
 ## it flips handedness — sim's CCW-around-+Z maps to world's CW-around-+Y,
-## hence the minus sign on `vehicle_xf.w`. `APEX_BAKE_OFFSET_Y` (PI/2)
-## compensates for the .tscn's +90° X bake on the Mesh child. If the basis
-## remap is ever made handedness-preserving, drop the negation in `yaw_world`.
+## hence the minus sign on `vehicle_xf.w`. `VEHICLE_BAKE_OFFSET_Y` (PI/2) rotates
+## the car's authored +Z forward to world +X (vehicle forward at sim_yaw=0) — no
+## +90° X bake, the car lies flat. If the basis remap is ever made handedness-
+## preserving, drop the negation in `yaw_world`.
 func sync_vehicle(vehicle_xf: Vector4) -> void:
 	var planar: Vector2 = _planar_from_3d(Vector3(vehicle_xf.x, vehicle_xf.y, vehicle_xf.z))
 	_vehicle_3d_proxy.global_position = Vector3(planar.x, 0.0, planar.y)
 	var yaw_world: float = -vehicle_xf.w  # handedness flip per sim->world remap
-	_vehicle_3d_proxy.rotation.y = APEX_BAKE_OFFSET_Y + yaw_world
+	_vehicle_3d_proxy.rotation.y = VEHICLE_BAKE_OFFSET_Y + yaw_world
 
 
 ## Per-frame player-proxy transform sync (PR1c; mirrors `sync_vehicle`, also

diff --git a/.totem/specs/p2a-vat-spike.md b/.totem/specs/p2a-vat-spike.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c1e352d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.totem/specs/p2a-vat-spike.md
@@ -0,0 +1,86 @@
+# P2a — VAT / 3D instance-data proof spike (design + 1-page impl)
+
+**Status:** Drafted (lc-claude). Tracking issue mmnto-ai/liquid-city#588 (epic mmnto-ai/liquid-city#565 / umbrella mmnto-ai/liquid-city#568). Cohort blind design-review **folded** (lc-codex / lc-agy / lc-gemini, all returned 2026-06-21) — this round IS the pre-PR design review; the impl PR gets a normal review.
+**Author:** lc-claude.
+**Context:** ADR-029 phase **P2a**. Gates the gore commitment (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#565) and the 3D horde render (P2b). Proves the `bake → import → runtime` vertex-animation-texture (VAT) path on a `MultiMeshInstance3D` horde **before** any gore work depends on it. All behind `use_3d_camera`; the 2D path and the deterministic sim are untouched.
+
+## Goal — what the spike proves
+
+1. An **offline Blender-Python VAT baker** (`tools/bake_vat.*`) bakes 1–2 clips (walk + die) of the committed **skinned mini-character** into a **float-precision** vertex-animation texture + a metadata sidecar.
+2. A `MultiMeshInstance3D` + spatial VAT shader renders **N instances animating independently** — distinct phase and clip — driven from a **per-instance clock + clip index** carried in `CUSTOM_DATA`, fed Godot-side from the existing 2D agent buffer (no new FFI).
+3. Captured **frame-time** at **64 / 128 / 256** (+ a 512 bench) vs a no-horde baseline, plus the **CPU cost of GDScript buffer assembly**.
+
+The output is a go/no-go on VAT as the LOD-1+ horde technology and a measured CPU/GPU budget that P2b's stable ABI + LODManager build on.
+
+## Non-goals (→ P2b+)
+
+`LODManager3D` / the skeletal LOD-0 path; the **stable** instance-data ABI; HitStop; gore / dismemberment; the part-segmented gore rig (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#559); the Rust-side `get_agent_buffer_3d()` graduation; the flip (P5). The permanent baker-home (Blender vs Godot-native) is **also** deferred to P2b — see § Baker.
+
+## Cohort blind round — folded
+
+**Consensus (all 3 — fold as-is):**
+- **VAT-only spike.** No `LODManager3D` / skeletal LOD-0 in P2a (all 3: scope creep that pollutes the measurement).
+- **Rig = the skinned mini-character.** It exists, it's the hardest pipeline case (smooth skinning is what VAT buys us), and the part-segmented gore rig stays LOD-0/P2b.
+- **4-float `CUSTOM_DATA` as the v0 / proof carrier** — explicitly **not** the stable P2b ABI; no wide per-instance data texture in the spike.
+- **Project Godot-side** from the existing `get_agent_buffer` — **no new FFI** for the spike.
+- **Anim clock from `CUSTOM_DATA.x`, never global `TIME`** — a per-slot CPU clock makes HitStop work day-one (ADR-015 per-instance-clock amendment).
+- **Headless guard** — `DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless"` no-ops the VAT setup; headless proves "doesn't load/instantiate/crash," not visual correctness.
+- **Gore boundary** — VAT = LOD-1+ distant; skeletal part-segmented = LOD-0 tactical; mutually exclusive tiers, zero collision.
+
+**Baker pipeline (cohort split; operator-decided 2026-06-21 "go with my recs"):** **Blender-Python float-bake, treated as disposable spike proof data; the permanent baker-home (Blender vs lc-agy's Godot-native `bake_vat.gd`) is deferred to P2b.** The spike proves the *runtime*, not the tool — codex's own framing ("treat the bake as disposable proof data") dissolves the fork for P2a. Blender gives trivial deformed-vertex readback (skinning is GPU-side in Godot — agy's native path carries a real CPU-readback feasibility risk that a spike shouldn't have to clear) and is 2/3 of the cohort.
+
+**Must-fold hazards (attributed):**
+1. **Float texture precision** (agy + gemini) — 8-bit PNG quantizes vertex offsets → vertex-stepping. Bake **EXR / `RGBAF`/`RGBF` float**.
+2. **Root motion stripped** (codex) — the sim owns translation + heading; a clip with root motion double-applies. The baker **strips** it (anchors the root's horizontal position per-frame so the clip plays in-place) and metadata declares `root_motion: stripped`; it **warns** when a clip's stripped travel exceeds `--root-motion-eps` (a sanity signal for an unintended locomotion clip — a death collapse's small drift is expected and silent).
+3. **MultiMesh3D stride reality** (codex; confirmed by Prop 027) — the 3D buffer is **16 floats** (12 `Transform3D` + 4 `CUSTOM_DATA`), unlike the 2D `Transform2D` buffer (§ Grounded facts). The Rust 2D buffer is **not** directly uploadable; measure the GDScript assembly cost.
+4. **Slot-keyed clocks** (codex) — key the per-instance clock by **stable `BufferSlot`**, not compacted draw index; mask dead slots (§ Grounded facts: dead slots zero out). Slot **recycling on respawn** means clocks must expire/rebind at a recycled slot.
+5. **Vertex addressing** (codex) — the VAT shader must address the position texture by a stable per-vertex index. **Default: bake the vertex index into `UV2`** (version-proof); prove `VERTEX_ID` as an optimization later.
+6. **Frozen-subset proof fixture** (codex) — freeze every Nth live slot's clock while the rest animate → makes any accidental global-`TIME` leak a *visible* artifact the harness can inspect.
+7. (deferred) **P2b graduation to a Rust `get_agent_buffer_3d()`** (agy) for the 400-agent scale, and the **altitude/Z-loft** feed (gemini) — both out of P2a (the spike flattens to Y=0, which is fine).
+
+## Grounded current-state facts (recon, file:line)
+
+- **FFI:** `ZomboidSim.get_agent_buffer()` (`lib.rs:412`) → `PackedFloat32Array`, **stride-8** `Transform2D`: `[bx.x, by.x, 0, originX, bx.y, by.y, 0, originY]`. Health-tint variant `get_agent_health_tint_buffer()` (`lib.rs:1266-1303`) = **stride-12** (8 + `[health_frac, 0, 0, 0]`). Consumed `sim_driver.gd:2847-2906`. Heading is reconstructable from the basis row.
+- **2D horde MultiMesh:** `$SimDisplay` `MultiMeshInstance2D` (`sim_driver.gd:468`), `transform_format = TRANSFORM_2D`; `use_custom_data` toggled for tint. **`instance_count` MUST be set before `multimesh.buffer = …`** (Godot validation contract; `count*stride == buffer.size()`).
+- **Liveness:** no explicit alive-bit. `snapshot.rs:134` `buf.fill(0.0)` then live agents rewrite their slot → **dead slots are zero-transforms (invisible at origin)**. `BufferSlot` (Rust) is the stable slot id; `agent_count = buf.len()/stride`. Phase-3 slot recycling exists → clocks must expire/rebind.
+- **`Render3DAdapter.gd`** (`extends RefCounted`): `_init(camera_3d, render_3d_root, vehicle_proxy, player_proxy)`, `setup_player_proxy(is_headless)`, `setup_vehicle_proxy()`, `setup_camera()/apply_camera()`, `sync_vehicle(Vector4)`, `sync_player(Vector2,Vector2)`, `project_cursor_to_sim(Vector2)→Dictionary`, `set_active(bool)`. Constructed `sim_driver.gd:1094-1096` (`_render_adapter`); the adapter **never queries FFI** (sim_driver feeds it). Headless guard `sim_driver.gd:1101`. Planar convention: **sim (X,Y) → Godot (X,Z), Y=0** (`_planar_from_3d` `:346`, `_sim_from_ground_3d` `:356`, `_assert_chirality` `:364`).
+- **VAT tooling / shaders:** **none** — greenfield. The only custom MultiMesh shader is the 2D `canvas_item` health-tint (`sim_driver.gd:691-722`); the VAT horde needs a **`spatial`** shader on a **`MultiMeshInstance3D`** — a different node + shader domain.
+
+## Implementation design (1 page)
+
+**Adapter:** a new, disposable **`VatHordeAdapter.gd`** (`extends RefCounted`, sibling of `Render3DAdapter`, owned by `sim_driver`) — honors the render-ownership split (ADR-029 fold 5); the adapter owns the `MultiMeshInstance3D` + buffer assembly + per-slot clocks, never touches FFI (sim_driver passes it the agent buffer each frame). Keeping it separate (not bolting onto `Render3DAdapter`) keeps the spike disposable.
+
+1. **Baker — `tools/bake_vat.py` + a `pnpm bake:vat` wrapper.** Blender headless Python: load the mini-character `.glb`, for each clip step frames, write deformed world-space vertex positions (root-motion stripped) into a **float EXR** (width = vertex_count, height = Σframes; clips stacked by row-range). Bake the **vertex index into the mesh `UV2.x`** (= `index / vertex_count`) and export the static bind-pose mesh as `.glb`/`.res`. Emit a JSON metadata sidecar: `vertex_count, frame_count, fps, clip_ranges, bounds, source_hash, coord_basis, scale, texture_format, root_motion: stripped`. Fail loud on root motion or a non-float format. **Disposable proof data** — committed under `apps/game-godot/assets/vat-proof/` for the spike.
+2. **VAT spatial shader** — vertex stage samples the position texture at `(UV2.x, clip_row_start + frac(custom.x / clip_len))` and writes `VERTEX`. Reads `INSTANCE_CUSTOM`: `.x` anim-clock, `.y` clip index → row offset, `.z` phase/speed, `.w` proof variation. **No `TIME`.** (Optional baked normal texture if lighting reads poorly — defer unless needed.)
+3. **`CUSTOM_DATA` v0 layout (proof, NOT the stable ABI):** `.x` = accumulated per-slot anim time (so a freeze = stop accumulating); `.y` = clip index; `.z` = per-slot phase offset / playback speed (kills synchronized marching); `.w` = proof variation / reserved.
+4. **Sim feed + buffer assembly (Godot-side):** each frame, read `get_agent_buffer()` (stride-8); for each **live** slot build a `Transform3D` — origin `(originX, 0, originY)` (sim X→Godot X, sim Y→Godot Z), basis yaw from the `Transform2D` basis (matching `Render3DAdapter` chirality) — append the 4 `CUSTOM_DATA` floats → a **16-float** `PackedFloat32Array`; set `instance_count` **before** `multimesh.buffer`. Mask dead (zero-transform) slots out of the draw set. Measure this assembly cost (hazard 3).
+5. **Per-instance clock:** the adapter accumulates `delta` (or physics-tick count) into a per-`BufferSlot` clock array; HitStop-readiness = the adapter can skip a slot's accumulation while others advance. On slot recycle, reset that slot's clock + phase.
+6. **Frozen-subset proof + debug toggle:** a debug mode (panel/CLI) that freezes every Nth live slot's clock → the frozen agents hold pose while the rest animate (catches any global-`TIME` leak visually). Wire the whole VAT path behind `use_3d_camera` + a `vat_horde_proof` flag; **headless → no-op** the setup.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- [ ] `tools/bake_vat.*` produces a deterministic **float** VAT + UV2-indexed mesh + metadata (incl. `root_motion: stripped`); re-running on the same input is byte-stable.
+- [ ] `MultiMeshInstance3D` renders **64 / 128 / 256** instances animating **independently** (distinct phase + clip) from `CUSTOM_DATA`, projected from the live agent buffer; dead slots invisible.
+- [ ] **No global `TIME`** in the VAT shader — the frozen-subset fixture shows per-instance freeze (frozen agents hold pose, others animate).
+- [ ] **Headless** run is no-op/stubbed for the VAT path; 2D + `use_3d_camera` paths unaffected; the harness stays crash-free + bit-stable.
+- [ ] Captured **frame-time at 64/128/256 (+512)** vs baseline + the **GDScript assembly cost**, recorded in the journal / on the issue.
+
+## Verification
+
+- **Headless safety:** `--import` registers `class_name`s; a headless fixture smoke must load without instantiating the VAT path (the guard). No CI Godot gate exists — GDScript/shader correctness is verified **locally via the harness**.
+- **Visual oracle:** non-headless harness **burst PNGs** — read the captures to confirm independent animation + the frozen-subset artifact. This is GDScript/GDShader-only → **no `cargo` rebuild** (no sim/FFI change).
+- Bake is offline + reproducible; commit the proof asset + metadata.
+
+## Freeze note
+
+The ADR-015 **"VAT shader anim-clock must bind to per-instance `CUSTOM_DATA`, never global `TIME`"** AST rule is a **Totem rule to author** — which needs `totem lesson compile`, currently **frozen**. P2a enforces the discipline **in code + via the frozen-subset fixture** now; the Totem rule lands when the freeze lifts (same posture as the deferred `.tres` sensor mmnto-ai/liquid-city#583).
+
+## Open risks
+
+- **Skinned-vertex bake** — Blender handles deformed-vertex readback trivially (de-risks the one place agy's Godot-native path was risky).
+- **Stride-assembly cost** is the real measured unknown — if GDScript assembly dominates at 256, it sharpens the P2b case for the Rust `get_agent_buffer_3d()` graduation (a *finding*, not a spike failure).
+- **`VERTEX_ID` reliability** in 4.6.2 is sidestepped by the UV2-index default.
+
+## Boundary to P2b
+
+P2b inherits: the measured budget, the v0 `CUSTOM_DATA` layout (to harden into the stable ABI), the baker (to either keep in Blender or port to Godot-native), and the LOD-0 skeletal path + `LODManager3D` that decides the VAT/skeletal cutoff from these numbers. HitStop + the Totem `TIME`-rule land in P2b/at-freeze-lift.
diff --git a/apps/game-godot/VatBench.gd b/apps/game-godot/VatBench.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c932519
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/VatBench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,136 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# VAT proof BENCH — ADR-029 P2a (.totem/specs/p2a-vat-spike.md).
+#
+# A controlled, disposable benchmark scene (the cohort's "separate synthetic
+# benchmark scene, same camera, same counts" recommendation). Drives the
+# VatHordeAdapter directly with a SYNTHETIC stride-8 agent buffer (a grid of N
+# instances with varied headings) under a fixed overhead camera — decoupled from
+# the gameplay camera so the proof is framed and the counts are exact.
+#
+# Proves, in one run: independent per-instance animation (varied phase/clip),
+# the frozen-subset (a global-TIME leak would make frozen agents animate), and
+# the perf ceiling at 64/128/256/512. Run:
+#   godot --path apps/game-godot res://vat_bench.tscn
+# Captures PNGs to user://vat_bench/ and prints [vat-bench] perf lines.
+
+const SPACING := 2.0
+# phases: each runs ~PHASE_FRAMES then advances. The last repeats 256 frozen.
+const PHASES: Array[Dictionary] = [
+	{"n": 64, "freeze": 0, "tag": "n64"},
+	{"n": 128, "freeze": 0, "tag": "n128"},
+	{"n": 256, "freeze": 0, "tag": "n256"},
+	{"n": 512, "freeze": 0, "tag": "n512"},
+	{"n": 256, "freeze": 3, "tag": "n256_frozen"},
+]
+const PHASE_FRAMES := 150
+
+var _adapter: VatHordeAdapter
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _phase_idx := 0
+var _frame := 0
+var _accum_fps := 0.0
+var _samples := 0
+var _captured := false
+var _cap_dir := "user://vat_bench/"
+var _buf := PackedFloat32Array()  # reused across frames (no per-frame alloc)
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	_cam = Camera3D.new()
+	_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_PERSPECTIVE
+	_cam.fov = 55.0
+	_cam.current = true
+	add_child(_cam)
+	_adapter = VatHordeAdapter.new(self, DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless")
+	if not _adapter.setup():
+		print("[vat-bench] adapter setup returned false (headless?) — nothing to render, quitting")
+		get_tree().quit(0)
+		return
+	_adapter.set_active(true)
+	if "--colormap" in OS.get_cmdline_user_args():
+		_adapter.set_colormap_enabled(true)  # `-- --colormap` -> real Kenney colors
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(_cap_dir))
+	_apply_phase()
+	print("[vat-bench] start; phases=", PHASES.size())
+
+
+func _apply_phase() -> void:
+	var p: Dictionary = PHASES[_phase_idx]
+	_adapter.frozen_stride = int(p.freeze)
+	_frame_camera(int(p.n))
+	_frame = 0
+	_accum_fps = 0.0
+	_samples = 0
+	_captured = false
+
+
+func _frame_camera(n: int) -> void:
+	var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(n))))
+	var extent := float(side) * SPACING
+	var c := extent * 0.5
+	_cam.position = Vector3(c, extent * 0.95, extent * 1.45)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(c, 0.5, c), Vector3.UP)
+
+
+func _build_buffer(n: int) -> PackedFloat32Array:
+	# Fill the reused member array in place (no per-frame alloc -> honest asm timing).
+	if _buf.size() != n * 8:
+		_buf.resize(n * 8)
+	var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(n))))
+	for i in n:
+		var gx := float(i % side) * SPACING
+		var gy := float(i / side) * SPACING
+		var ang := float(i) * 0.7  # varied heading per instance
+		var cs := cos(ang)
+		var sn := sin(ang)
+		var o := i * 8
+		# Transform2D layout: [bx.x, by.x, 0, ox, bx.y, by.y, 0, oy]
+		_buf[o + 0] = cs
+		_buf[o + 1] = -sn
+		_buf[o + 2] = 0.0
+		_buf[o + 3] = gx
+		_buf[o + 4] = sn
+		_buf[o + 5] = cs
+		_buf[o + 6] = 0.0
+		_buf[o + 7] = gy
+	return _buf
+
+
+func _process(delta: float) -> void:
+	var p: Dictionary = PHASES[_phase_idx]
+	var n: int = int(p.n)
+	_adapter.update(_build_buffer(n), n, delta)
+	_accum_fps += Engine.get_frames_per_second()
+	_samples += 1
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame == PHASE_FRAMES / 2 and not _captured:
+		_captured = true
+		_capture(str(p.tag))
+	if _frame >= PHASE_FRAMES:
+		var avg := _accum_fps / float(maxi(_samples, 1))
+		print(
+			(
+				"[vat-bench] N=%d freeze=%d avg_fps=%.1f asm=%.3fms"
+				% [n, int(p.freeze), avg, _adapter.last_assembly_usec / 1000.0]
+			)
+		)
+		_phase_idx += 1
+		if _phase_idx >= PHASES.size():
+			if "--loop" in OS.get_cmdline_user_args():
+				_phase_idx = 0  # live-view: cycle the counts forever
+				_apply_phase()
+				return
+			print("[vat-bench] DONE")
+			get_tree().quit(0)
+			return
+		_apply_phase()
+
+
+func _capture(tag: String) -> void:
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img == null:
+		return
+	var path := _cap_dir + "vat_%s.png" % tag
+	img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+	print("[vat-bench] shot %s" % path)

new file mode 100644
index 0000000..a1598ae
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/VatHordeAdapter.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,303 @@
+class_name VatHordeAdapter
+extends RefCounted
+
+# VAT horde proof adapter — ADR-029 P2a (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#588).
+#
+# DISPOSABLE proof-spike adapter (sibling of Render3DAdapter; owned by sim_driver).
+# Proves the bake -> import -> runtime VAT path: renders N instances of one rig via
+# a MultiMeshInstance3D, each animating INDEPENDENTLY from a per-slot CPU clock +
+# clip index packed into CUSTOM_DATA. The shader samples a baked float position
+# texture (never global TIME), so HitStop = the adapter declining to advance one
+# slot's clock.
+#
+# Boundaries (spec .totem/specs/p2a-vat-spike.md):
+#   - Owns the MultiMesh + buffer assembly + per-slot clocks. NEVER queries the
+#     sim FFI — sim_driver feeds it the stride-8 agent buffer.
+#   - Headless -> no-op (DisplayServer guard); the proof is a non-headless visual.
+#   - Clocks keyed by stable BufferSlot (not compacted draw index); dead slots are
+#     masked out; a recycled slot resets its anim state.
+#   - 4-float CUSTOM_DATA is the v0 / proof carrier, NOT the stable P2b ABI.
+
+const VAT_DIR := "res://assets/vat-proof/"
+const VAT_STEM := "character-female-a"
+const SHADER_PATH := "res://shaders/vat_horde.gdshader"
+const AGENT_STRIDE := 8  # sim get_agent_buffer() Transform2D stride
+const OUT_STRIDE := 16  # MultiMesh3D: 12 Transform3D + 4 CUSTOM_DATA
+const DIE_CLIP := 1.0
+const DIE_EVERY := 7  # 1-in-N slots play the die clip (proves clip selection)
+
+var _render_root: Node3D
+var _headless := false
+var _enabled := false
+var _ready := false
+var _colormap_enabled := false
+
+var _mmi: MultiMeshInstance3D
+var _mm: MultiMesh
+var _mat: ShaderMaterial
+var _meta: Dictionary = {}
+
+# per-slot state (indexed by stable BufferSlot)
+var _clock := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _phase := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _speed := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _clip := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _tint := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _was_live := PackedByteArray()
+var _out := PackedFloat32Array()
+
+# tunables (calibrated from a burst frame)
+var character_scale := 1.0
+var y_offset := 0.19  # lift feet (VAT min y ~= -0.19) to ground
+var facing_offset := 0.0  # radians; correct model-forward vs sim-heading
+var frozen_stride := 0  # 0 = none; freeze every Nth slot's clock (the proof)
+
+# perf instrumentation
+var last_assembly_usec := 0
+var last_live := 0
+
+
+func _init(render_root: Node3D, headless: bool) -> void:
+	_render_root = render_root
+	_headless = headless
+
+
+# Loads the VAT asset + builds the MultiMesh. Returns false (no-op) when headless
+# or on a load failure. Idempotent.
+func setup() -> bool:
+	if _ready:
+		return true
+	if _headless:
+		return false  # headless proves "doesn't crash", not the visual
+	# Runtime-load the source EXR (NOT load()/the imported .ctex) to keep FULL
+	# RGBAF precision: the texture importer VRAM-compresses it (detect_3d) and may
+	# store half-float — both quantize VAT vertex positions into visible stepping.
+	# Caveat (GCA): load_from_file reads the physical FS, so this works in-editor /
+	# the harness (where this spike lives) but NOT a packaged export (assets pack
+	# into the .pck); it fails loud below in that case. Production asset-load is a
+	# P2b decision (e.g. a custom data-texture importer or bundling the loose .exr).
+	var img := Image.load_from_file(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + ".vat.exr"))
+	if img == null:
+		push_error("VatHordeAdapter: failed to load VAT EXR")
+		return false
+	if img.get_format() != Image.FORMAT_RGBAF:
+		img.convert(Image.FORMAT_RGBAF)  # keep full float precision
+	var tex := ImageTexture.create_from_image(img)
+
+	var meta_str := FileAccess.get_file_as_string(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + ".vat.json")
+	_meta = JSON.parse_string(meta_str) if meta_str != "" else {}
+	if _meta.is_empty():
+		push_error("VatHordeAdapter: failed to load VAT metadata")
+		return false
+
+	var mesh := _load_mesh()
+	if mesh == null:
+		push_error("VatHordeAdapter: failed to load VAT mesh")
+		return false
+
+	var shader := load(SHADER_PATH) as Shader
+	if shader == null:
+		push_error("VatHordeAdapter: failed to load shader")
+		return false
+	_mat = ShaderMaterial.new()
+	_mat.shader = shader
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("vat_tex", tex)
+	# Optional Kenney colormap (off by default — flat tint keeps the perf path clean).
+	# A normal color texture, so the import pipeline (sRGB) is fine here, unlike the
+	# precision-critical VAT data. Sampled via UV (the bake left UV intact).
+	var cm := load(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + "_colormap.png") as Texture2D
+	if cm != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("colormap", cm)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", _colormap_enabled)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("total_rows", int(_meta.get("total_rows", 1)))
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("fps", float(_meta.get("fps", 24.0)))
+	var clips: Array = _meta.get("clips", [])
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("clip_count", mini(clips.size(), 8))  # shader arrays are [8]
+	var starts := PackedFloat32Array()
+	var counts := PackedFloat32Array()
+	starts.resize(8)
+	counts.resize(8)
+	for i in clips.size():
+		if i >= 8:
+			break
+		starts[i] = float(clips[i].get("row_start", 0))
+		counts[i] = float(clips[i].get("frame_count", 1))
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("clip_row_start", starts)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("clip_frame_count", counts)
+
+	_mm = MultiMesh.new()
+	_mm.transform_format = MultiMesh.TRANSFORM_3D
+	_mm.use_custom_data = true
+	_mm.mesh = mesh
+	# the shader displaces VERTEX, so the bind-pose AABB understates extent — give
+	# the mesh a generous custom AABB so instances don't get culled mid-animation.
+	mesh.custom_aabb = AABB(Vector3(-1.5, -1.5, -1.5), Vector3(3.0, 3.0, 3.0))
+
+	_mmi = MultiMeshInstance3D.new()
+	_mmi.name = "VatHordeProof"
+	_mmi.multimesh = _mm
+	_mmi.material_override = _mat
+	_mmi.visible = false
+	# _mmi is owned by the tree (parented to _render_root) -> freed with the scene,
+	# not leaked despite the adapter being RefCounted: it's created once in _ready and
+	# never recreated, so there is no re-instantiation path that could orphan it.
+	_render_root.add_child(_mmi)
+
+	_ready = true
+	return true
+
+
+func set_active(enabled: bool) -> void:
+	_enabled = enabled
+	if _mmi != null:
+		_mmi.visible = enabled and not _headless
+
+
+func is_active() -> bool:
+	return _enabled and _ready and not _headless
+
+
+# Toggle the real Kenney colormap (vs the flat per-instance proof tint).
+func set_colormap_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_colormap_enabled = on
+	if _mat != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", on)
+
+
+func is_colormap_enabled() -> bool:
+	return _colormap_enabled
+
+
+# Per-frame: assemble the MultiMesh buffer from the stride-8 agent buffer.
+func update(agent_buf: PackedFloat32Array, count: int, delta: float) -> void:
+	if not is_active():
+		return
+	var t0 := Time.get_ticks_usec()
+	_ensure_slots(count)
+	var needed := count * OUT_STRIDE
+	if _out.size() < needed:
+		_out.resize(needed)
+
+	var live := 0
+	for s in count:
+		var bo := s * AGENT_STRIDE
+		var bxx := agent_buf[bo + 0]
+		var byx := agent_buf[bo + 1]
+		var ox := agent_buf[bo + 3]
+		var bxy := agent_buf[bo + 4]
+		var byy := agent_buf[bo + 5]
+		var oy := agent_buf[bo + 7]
+		# dead slots are zeroed by the snapshot writer -> all-zero basis
+		if absf(bxx) < 1e-9 and absf(bxy) < 1e-9 and absf(byx) < 1e-9 and absf(byy) < 1e-9:
+			_was_live[s] = 0
+			continue
+		if _was_live[s] == 0:
+			_init_slot(s)  # (re)born / recycled slot -> fresh anim state
+			_was_live[s] = 1
+		var frozen := frozen_stride > 0 and (s % frozen_stride) == 0
+		if not frozen:
+			_clock[s] += delta * _speed[s]
+
+		# sim heading (sim plane) -> Godot yaw about +Y; sim (X,Y) -> Godot (X,Z)
+		var heading := atan2(bxy, bxx)
+		var b := Basis(Vector3.UP, -heading + facing_offset)
+		if character_scale != 1.0:
+			b = b.scaled(Vector3(character_scale, character_scale, character_scale))
+		var po := live * OUT_STRIDE
+		# MultiMesh TRANSFORM_3D: 3x4 row-major; b.x/b.y/b.z are the basis columns
+		_out[po + 0] = b.x.x
+		_out[po + 1] = b.y.x
+		_out[po + 2] = b.z.x
+		_out[po + 3] = ox
+		_out[po + 4] = b.x.y
+		_out[po + 5] = b.y.y
+		_out[po + 6] = b.z.y
+		_out[po + 7] = y_offset
+		_out[po + 8] = b.x.z
+		_out[po + 9] = b.y.z
+		_out[po + 10] = b.z.z
+		_out[po + 11] = oy
+		# CUSTOM_DATA v0: x=clock y=clip z=phase w=tint
+		_out[po + 12] = _clock[s]
+		_out[po + 13] = _clip[s]
+		_out[po + 14] = _phase[s]
+		_out[po + 15] = _tint[s]
+		live += 1
+
+	_mm.instance_count = live
+	if live > 0:
+		# exact length, no per-frame slice copy (keeps the assembly timing honest)
+		_out.resize(live * OUT_STRIDE)
+		_mm.buffer = _out
+	last_assembly_usec = Time.get_ticks_usec() - t0
+	last_live = live
+
+
+# Toggle / cycle the frozen-subset proof (freezes every Nth slot's clock so a
+# global-TIME leak would be instantly visible: frozen agents must hold pose).
+func cycle_frozen_stride() -> int:
+	match frozen_stride:
+		0:
+			frozen_stride = 2
+		2:
+			frozen_stride = 3
+		_:
+			frozen_stride = 0
+	return frozen_stride
+
+
+func debug_status() -> String:
+	if not is_active():
+		return "VAT: off"
+	return (
+		"VAT: %d inst, asm %.2fms, freeze=%d"
+		% [last_live, last_assembly_usec / 1000.0, frozen_stride]
+	)
+
+
+func _ensure_slots(count: int) -> void:
+	if _clock.size() >= count:
+		return
+	var n := count
+	_clock.resize(n)
+	_phase.resize(n)
+	_speed.resize(n)
+	_clip.resize(n)
+	_tint.resize(n)
+	_was_live.resize(n)
+
+
+func _init_slot(s: int) -> void:
+	var hp := float((s * 2654435761) % 997) / 997.0
+	var hs := float((s * 40503) % 251) / 251.0
+	var ht := float((s * 2246822519) % 211) / 211.0
+	_phase[s] = hp
+	_speed[s] = 0.85 + 0.30 * hs
+	_clip[s] = DIE_CLIP if (s % DIE_EVERY == 0) else 0.0
+	_tint[s] = ht
+	_clock[s] = hp * 2.0  # stagger start so instances don't march in lockstep
+
+
+func _load_mesh() -> Mesh:
+	var scene := load(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + ".vat.glb") as PackedScene
+	if scene == null:
+		return null
+	var root := scene.instantiate()
+	if root == null:
+		return null
+	var mi := _find_mesh(root)
+	var mesh: Mesh = null
+	if mi != null:
+		mesh = mi.mesh
+	root.queue_free()
+	return mesh
+
+
+func _find_mesh(n: Node) -> MeshInstance3D:
+	if n is MeshInstance3D:
+		return n
+	for c in n.get_children():
+		var r := _find_mesh(c)
+		if r != null:
+			return r
+	return null

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/shaders/vat_horde.gdshader b/apps/game-godot/shaders/vat_horde.gdshader
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..19c9f72
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/shaders/vat_horde.gdshader
@@ -0,0 +1,55 @@
+shader_type spatial;
+render_mode unshaded;
+
+// VAT proof shader — ADR-029 P2a (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#588).
+//
+// Vertex positions come ENTIRELY from a baked float position texture:
+//   - column = the ORIGINAL vertex index, carried in UV2.x as (index+0.5)/N
+//     (survives Godot vertex-splitting; VERTEX_ID would not).
+//   - row    = the animation frame, derived from a PER-INSTANCE clock in
+//     INSTANCE_CUSTOM.x. There is NO global TIME here, so HitStop is just the
+//     CPU-side adapter declining to advance one slot's clock (the rest animate).
+//
+// Unshaded: the proof measures the instance-data path + perf, not lighting
+// (art-directed lighting is the deferred pass, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#586). Normals
+// stay in bind pose, which unshaded ignores.
+//
+// INSTANCE_CUSTOM layout (v0 / proof carrier, NOT the stable P2b ABI):
+//   x = accumulated anim clock (seconds)   y = clip index
+//   z = phase offset / playback speed       w = per-instance tint variation
+
+uniform sampler2D vat_tex : filter_nearest, repeat_disable;
+uniform int total_rows = 1;
+uniform float fps = 24.0;
+uniform int clip_count = 1;
+uniform float clip_row_start[8];
+uniform float clip_frame_count[8];
+uniform vec3 base_color : source_color = vec3(0.30, 0.55, 0.30);
+uniform vec3 accent_color : source_color = vec3(0.78, 0.72, 0.38);
+uniform bool use_colormap = false; // OFF = flat per-instance tint (clean perf path)
+uniform sampler2D colormap : filter_nearest, source_color; // Kenney palette (UV lookup)
+
+varying float v_tint;
+
+void vertex() {
+	int clip = clamp(int(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.y + 0.5), 0, clip_count - 1);
+	float cstart = clip_row_start[clip];
+	float ccount = max(clip_frame_count[clip], 1.0);
+	float dur = ccount / max(fps, 0.001); // guard div-by-zero on the fps uniform
+	float local = fract(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.x / dur); // [0,1) looped per-instance clock
+	float frame = min(floor(local * ccount), ccount - 1.0);
+	float v = (cstart + frame + 0.5) / float(total_rows);
+	VERTEX = textureLod(vat_tex, vec2(UV2.x, v), 0.0).xyz;
+	v_tint = INSTANCE_CUSTOM.w;
+}
+
+void fragment() {
+	if (use_colormap) {
+		// Real Kenney colormap (palette lookup via UV; UV2 carries the VAT index,
+		// UV is untouched by the bake). Keep a subtle per-instance tint so the
+		// instance-data variation still reads. Unshaded -> no lighting cost.
+		ALBEDO = texture(colormap, UV).rgb * (0.85 + 0.3 * clamp(v_tint, 0.0, 1.0));
+	} else {
+		ALBEDO = mix(base_color, accent_color, clamp(v_tint, 0.0, 1.0));
+	}
+}

diff --git a/tools/bake_vat.py b/tools/bake_vat.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e7af295
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tools/bake_vat.py
@@ -0,0 +1,265 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python
+"""Offline VAT (vertex-animation-texture) baker — Blender headless.
+
+ADR-029 P2a proof spike (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#588). Bakes one or more skeletal
+clips of a skinned glb into a float-precision position texture + a UV2-vertex-
+indexed bind-pose mesh + a JSON metadata sidecar, for a MultiMeshInstance3D VAT
+horde. DISPOSABLE proof data — the permanent baker-home (Blender vs Godot-native)
+is a P2b decision (see .totem/specs/p2a-vat-spike.md).
+
+Run via the pnpm wrapper:  pnpm bake:vat
+or directly:
+  "<blender>" --background --factory-startup --python tools/bake_vat.py -- \
+      --glb apps/game-godot/assets/mini-characters/character-female-a.glb \
+      --out apps/game-godot/assets/vat-proof \
+      --clips walk,die
+
+Output (under --out):
+  <stem>.vat.exr   RGBA float position texture (width=vertex_count, height=Σframes)
+  <stem>.vat.glb   bind-pose mesh; UV2 (TEXCOORD_1) carries (vertex_index+0.5)/N
+  <stem>.vat.json  metadata (vertex/frame counts, fps, clip rows, bounds, hash, axes)
+
+Contracts (cohort folds, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#588):
+  - FLOAT texture (EXR) — no 8-bit quantization → no vertex-stepping.
+  - Root motion: clips are in-place; we bake in object-LOCAL space (excludes the
+    object transform). We DETECT root-bone horizontal travel and FAIL LOUD if a
+    clip exceeds --root-motion-eps (the sim owns translation; a moving root would
+    double-apply).
+  - Axis convert Blender Z-up -> Godot Y-up: (x, y, z) -> (x, z, -y). The texture
+    stores positions already in Godot space (the shader overwrites VERTEX with the
+    sample, so the exported mesh geometry is irrelevant; only UV2 + topology matter).
+  - Vertex addressing via UV2 (survives Godot vertex-splitting — the index travels
+    with the vertex as an attribute, unlike VERTEX_ID which indexes the post-split
+    array).
+"""
+
+import bpy
+import sys
+import os
+import json
+import math
+import hashlib
+
+
+def _argv():
+    return sys.argv[sys.argv.index("--") + 1:] if "--" in sys.argv else []
+
+
+def _parse(argv):
+    opts = {
+        "glb": "apps/game-godot/assets/mini-characters/character-female-a.glb",
+        "out": "apps/game-godot/assets/vat-proof",
+        "clips": "walk,die",
+        "root_motion_eps": 0.5,  # WARN threshold for stripped root travel (not fatal)
+    }
+    i = 0
+    while i < len(argv):
+        a = argv[i]
+        if a == "--glb":
+            opts["glb"] = argv[i + 1]; i += 2
+        elif a == "--out":
+            opts["out"] = argv[i + 1]; i += 2
+        elif a == "--clips":
+            opts["clips"] = argv[i + 1]; i += 2
+        elif a == "--root-motion-eps":
+            opts["root_motion_eps"] = float(argv[i + 1]); i += 2
+        else:
+            i += 1
+    return opts
+
+
+def _die(msg):
+    print(f"[bake_vat] ERROR: {msg}")
+    sys.exit(1)
+
+
+def main():
+    opts = _parse(_argv())
+    glb = os.path.abspath(opts["glb"])
+    out_dir = os.path.abspath(opts["out"])
+    clip_names = [c.strip() for c in opts["clips"].split(",") if c.strip()]
+    if not os.path.isfile(glb):
+        _die(f"glb not found: {glb}")
+    os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)
+    stem = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(glb))[0]
+
+    print(f"[bake_vat] importing {glb}")
+    bpy.ops.wm.read_factory_settings(use_empty=True)
+    bpy.ops.import_scene.gltf(filepath=glb)
+    scene = bpy.context.scene
+    fps = scene.render.fps
+
+    # --- collect the skinned meshes (have an ARMATURE modifier); drop junk ---
+    arm = next((o for o in bpy.data.objects if o.type == "ARMATURE"), None)
+    if arm is None:
+        _die("no armature in glb")
+    skinned = [o for o in bpy.data.objects
+               if o.type == "MESH"
+               and any(m.type == "ARMATURE" for m in o.modifiers)]
+    if not skinned:
+        _die("no skinned meshes (armature-modified) found")
+    # deterministic order so the bake is reproducible
+    skinned.sort(key=lambda o: o.name)
+    print(f"[bake_vat] skinned meshes: {[o.name for o in skinned]}")
+
+    # --- join into one mesh (a MultiMesh renders ONE mesh N times) ---
+    bpy.ops.object.select_all(action="DESELECT")
+    for o in skinned:
+        o.select_set(True)
+    joined = skinned[0]
+    bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = joined
+    if len(skinned) > 1:
+        bpy.ops.object.join()
+    me = joined.data
+    vcount = len(me.vertices)
+    print(f"[bake_vat] joined vertex_count={vcount}")
+
+    # --- UV2 (second layer / TEXCOORD_1) carries the vertex index ---
+    while len(me.uv_layers) < 2:
+        me.uv_layers.new(name="vat_index")
+    uv2 = me.uv_layers[1]
+    for loop in me.loops:
+        u = (loop.vertex_index + 0.5) / vcount
+        uv2.data[loop.index].uv = (u, 0.5)
+    # keep only 2 UV layers (Godot caps at UV/UV2)
+    while len(me.uv_layers) > 2:
+        me.uv_layers.remove(me.uv_layers[2])
+
+    # --- resolve actions + count rows ---
+    clips = []
+    row = 0
+    for name in clip_names:
+        act = bpy.data.actions.get(name)
+        if act is None:
+            _die(f"action {name!r} not found; available: {[a.name for a in bpy.data.actions]}")
+        f0, f1 = int(act.frame_range[0]), int(act.frame_range[1])
+        nframes = f1 - f0 + 1
+        clips.append({"name": name, "f0": f0, "f1": f1, "row_start": row,
+                      "frame_count": nframes, "action": act})
+        row += nframes
+    total_rows = row
+    print(f"[bake_vat] clips={[(c['name'], c['frame_count']) for c in clips]} total_rows={total_rows}")
+
+    arm.animation_data_create()
+    depsgraph = bpy.context.evaluated_depsgraph_get()
+
+    # --- bake: pixels are RGBA, row-major from image BOTTOM (Blender native).
+    # We write frame-row r at image-y r; the shader samples v=(r+0.5)/total_rows.
+    px = [0.0] * (vcount * total_rows * 4)
+    bmin = [1e9, 1e9, 1e9]
+    bmax = [-1e9, -1e9, -1e9]
+    # root = the parentless bone (pose.bones order isn't guaranteed root-first)
+    if not arm.pose.bones:
+        _die("armature has no bones")
+    root_bone = next((b for b in arm.pose.bones if b.parent is None), arm.pose.bones[0])
+
+    # Root motion is STRIPPED: each frame we anchor the root bone's HORIZONTAL
+    # world position (Blender X/Y) at origin, so the clip plays in-place at the
+    # agent's sim position. Vertical (Z, up) is kept (bob, fall, collapse). This
+    # honors "the sim owns translation"; locomotion drift would double-apply.
+    for c in clips:
+        arm.animation_data.action = c["action"]
+        stripped = 0.0  # pre-strip horizontal travel, reported for info
+        rest = None
+        for fi in range(c["frame_count"]):
+            f = c["f0"] + fi
+            scene.frame_set(f)
+            depsgraph = bpy.context.evaluated_depsgraph_get()
+            rb = arm.matrix_world @ root_bone.head  # world-space root head
+            if rest is None:
+                rest = rb.copy()
+            else:
+                stripped = max(stripped, math.hypot(rb.x - rest.x, rb.y - rest.y))
+            obj_eval = joined.evaluated_get(depsgraph)
+            mw = obj_eval.matrix_world
+            mesh_eval = obj_eval.to_mesh()
+            r = c["row_start"] + fi
+            base = r * vcount * 4
+            for i, v in enumerate(mesh_eval.vertices):
+                w = mw @ v.co  # world-space deformed vertex (Blender Z-up)
+                # strip root: re-express horizontal relative to the root each frame
+                bx = w.x - rb.x
+                by = w.y - rb.y
+                bz = w.z
+                # Blender Z-up -> Godot Y-up
+                gx, gy, gz = bx, bz, -by
+                o = base + i * 4
+                px[o + 0] = gx
+                px[o + 1] = gy
+                px[o + 2] = gz
+                px[o + 3] = 1.0
+                if gx < bmin[0]: bmin[0] = gx
+                if gy < bmin[1]: bmin[1] = gy
+                if gz < bmin[2]: bmin[2] = gz
+                if gx > bmax[0]: bmax[0] = gx
+                if gy > bmax[1]: bmax[1] = gy
+                if gz > bmax[2]: bmax[2] = gz
+            obj_eval.to_mesh_clear()
+        print(f"[bake_vat]   {c['name']}: rows [{c['row_start']},{c['row_start']+c['frame_count']}) "
+              f"stripped_root_travel={stripped:.4f}")
+        if stripped > opts["root_motion_eps"]:
+            print(f"[bake_vat]   WARNING: {c['name']} stripped {stripped:.3f} of root travel "
+                  f"(> --root-motion-eps {opts['root_motion_eps']}) — confirm the clip is in-place")
+
+    # --- write the EXR (float) ---
+    exr_path = os.path.join(out_dir, f"{stem}.vat.exr")
+    img = bpy.data.images.new(f"{stem}_vat", width=vcount, height=total_rows,
+                              float_buffer=True, alpha=True)
+    img.colorspace_settings.name = "Non-Color"
+    img.pixels = px
+    img.file_format = "OPEN_EXR"
+    img.filepath_raw = exr_path
+    scene.render.image_settings.file_format = "OPEN_EXR"
+    scene.render.image_settings.color_depth = "32"
+    scene.render.image_settings.exr_codec = "ZIP"
+    img.save()
+    print(f"[bake_vat] wrote {exr_path} ({vcount}x{total_rows} float)")
+
+    # --- export the bind-pose mesh (UV2 = index) ---
+    bpy.ops.object.select_all(action="DESELECT")
+    joined.select_set(True)
+    bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = joined
+    # neutralize deformation for the exported geometry (positions are overwritten
+    # by the shader anyway; this keeps the bind-pose tidy)
+    scene.frame_set(0)
+    glb_path = os.path.join(out_dir, f"{stem}.vat.glb")
+    bpy.ops.export_scene.gltf(
+        filepath=glb_path,
+        use_selection=True,
+        export_format="GLB",
+        export_yup=True,
+        export_animations=False,
+        export_skins=False,
+        export_apply=False,
+    )
+    print(f"[bake_vat] wrote {glb_path}")
+
+    # --- metadata sidecar ---
+    with open(glb, "rb") as fh:
+        src_hash = hashlib.md5(fh.read()).hexdigest()
+    meta = {
+        "schema": "vat-proof-v0",
+        "source_glb": os.path.relpath(glb, os.getcwd()).replace("\\", "/"),
+        "source_md5": src_hash,
+        "vertex_count": vcount,
+        "total_rows": total_rows,
+        "fps": fps,
+        "clips": [{"name": c["name"], "row_start": c["row_start"],
+                   "frame_count": c["frame_count"]} for c in clips],
+        "bounds": {"min": bmin, "max": bmax},
+        "coord_basis": "godot-y-up",
+        "axis_convert": "blender(x,y,z)->godot(x,z,-y)",
+        "root_motion": "stripped (horizontal root anchored per-frame; vertical kept)",
+        "uv2": "(vertex_index+0.5)/vertex_count in TEXCOORD_1",
+        "texture_format": "OPEN_EXR RGBA 32f, Non-Color",
+        "row_origin": "row r at image-y r (bottom-up); shader v=(r+0.5)/total_rows",
+    }
+    json_path = os.path.join(out_dir, f"{stem}.vat.json")
+    with open(json_path, "w") as fh:
+        json.dump(meta, fh, indent=2)
+    print(f"[bake_vat] wrote {json_path}")
+    print("[bake_vat] DONE")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

diff --git a/adr/adr-029-3d-render-migration.md b/adr/adr-029-3d-render-migration.md
index 351ed60..bb29349 100644
--- a/adr/adr-029-3d-render-migration.md
+++ b/adr/adr-029-3d-render-migration.md
@@ -56,6 +56,10 @@ The migration is **broad-surface but low-core-risk**: rendering is downstream of
 - **Migration-window length — DECIDED (satur8d 2026-06-19): no 2D fallback tail.** 2D is retained ONLY as a Tenet-7 build scaffold to keep `main` green across the migration PRs (P1–P4), and is **deleted at the flip (P5)** — no post-flip fallback flag is retained. This overrides the cohort's "keep a 2D fallback flag ~1 minor before deletion" recommendation; the migration window collapses to "as fast as the work lands," gated only by fixture-backed visual parity (§2 contract 9).
 - **Cheap-wins re-sequencing (`mmnto-ai/liquid-city#555` / `mmnto-ai/liquid-city#556` / `mmnto-ai/liquid-city#557`) — DECIDED (satur8d 2026-06-19):** re-homed by visibility/durability, not run as a phase-0 batch. **`mmnto-ai/liquid-city#557` (speed) shipped first** (standalone sim-side, merged `7ff0d26`). **`mmnto-ai/liquid-city#555` (player-visual) folds into `mmnto-ai/liquid-city#558` / P1** — the rigged 3D player IS the legibility fix; no throwaway 2D. **`mmnto-ai/liquid-city#556` (loft) defers to phase 2** with the 3D horde (z-loft only reads once the perspective Camera3D + 3D horde land; tuning under top-down = blind calibration).
 
+## Amendments
+
+- **3D became the working default at P2b PR3 (satur8d 2026-06-22) — ahead of the planned P5 flip.** PR `mmnto-ai/liquid-city#597` set `use_3d_camera = true` by default. The §1 phased sequence flips the default only at **P5** on fixture-backed visual parity (§2 contract 9), with 2D as the working default through P1–P4; this moves the boot default forward. Rationale: by P2b PR3 the 3D path renders the player + the production VAT horde, and the operator chose 3D as the default working view ("2D only for debugging") rather than continue booting into 2D. **This does NOT collapse the P5 gate.** 2D is still retained as the Tenet-7 build scaffold (NOT deleted — the no-2D-fallback deletion still waits for P5's fixture-backed visual-parity gate, §Resolved Operator Calls), and `use_3d_camera` remains an operator toggle. Only the boot DEFAULT changed (3D-first); the parity gate and the 2D-deletion trigger are unchanged. P5 still stands: prove fixture-backed visual parity → delete 2D.
+
 ## Non-goals
 
 Touching the deterministic sim; multi-floor camera altitude tracking (Proposal 030 defers it); a big-bang rewrite.
diff --git a/apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd b/apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ca8856
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,415 @@
+class_name HordeRenderAdapter
+extends RefCounted
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR3 — PRODUCTION 3D horde render adapter (Surface A consumer +
+# CUSTOM_DATA v1). Successor to the disposable proof `VatHordeAdapter`.
+#
+# Reads TWO FFI buffers each frame and assembles one stride-16 MultiMesh3D
+# buffer behind `use_3d_camera`:
+#   - get_agent_buffer()             — stride-8 Transform2D (position + heading)
+#   - get_agent_render_state_buffer()— stride-4 render-state record per slot
+#       [0] flags (LIVE/BALLISTIC + reserved gore), [1] health, [2] hitstop,
+#       [3] AnimIntent.
+#
+# Per slot it derives CUSTOM_DATA v1: x = per-slot anim clock (FROZEN while
+# hitstop > 0 — HitStop is the adapter not advancing the clock; the shader
+# never reads global TIME), y = clip index (AnimIntent → clip name → numeric
+# index from the bake table), z = health fraction, w = the packed flags uint
+# (floatBitsToUint round-trip proven bit-exact by spike 1).
+#
+# Boundaries (spec .totem/specs/p2b-3d-horde.md):
+#   - Owns the MultiMesh + buffer assembly + per-slot clocks. NEVER queries
+#     the sim FFI — sim_driver feeds it both buffers each frame.
+#   - Headless -> no-op (DisplayServer guard); rendering needs a real GPU.
+#   - Clocks keyed by stable BufferSlot; dead slots (zeroed transform) masked.
+
+const SHADER_PATH := "res://shaders/horde_render.gdshader"
+const VAT_DIR := "res://assets/vat-proof/"
+const VAT_STEM := "character-female-a"
+const AGENT_STRIDE := 8  # sim get_agent_buffer() Transform2D stride
+const OUT_STRIDE := 16  # MultiMesh3D: 12 Transform3D + 4 CUSTOM_DATA
+
+# AnimIntent — MIRROR of `density_feedback.rs` `AnimIntent` (the wire contract).
+# Locked against the Rust discriminants by tests/anim_intent_parity.rs.
+const ANIM_IDLE := 0
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const ANIM_STAGGER := 2
+const ANIM_BALLISTIC := 3
+const ANIM_DEATH := 4
+const ANIM_ATTACK := 5
+const ANIM_INTENT_COUNT := 6
+
+# Render-state record layout — MIRROR of `density_feedback.rs`
+# RENDER_STATE_STRIDE + RS_* offsets.
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const RS_FLAGS := 0
+const RS_HEALTH := 1
+const RS_HITSTOP := 2
+const RS_INTENT := 3
+
+var _render_root: Node3D
+var _headless := false
+var _enabled := false
+var _ready := false
+## Production horde shows the real Kenney colormap texture by default (ADR-029
+## P2b PR3). The disposable proof defaulted this OFF for a clean perf baseline;
+## the production look wants the material. One UV texture sample — negligible.
+var _colormap_enabled := true
+## Dmg-based red health tint. OFF by default (clean Kenney textures for the
+## artist preview); enabled via `set_health_tint_enabled` (the `--health-tint`
+## launch flag wires it on for an A/B look). ADR-029 P2b PR3.
+var _health_tint_enabled := false
+
+var _mmi: MultiMeshInstance3D
+var _mm: MultiMesh
+var _mat: ShaderMaterial
+var _meta: Dictionary = {}
+
+# per-slot anim clock (indexed by stable BufferSlot)
+var _clock := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _was_live := PackedByteArray()
+var _out := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _scratch := PackedByteArray()  # 4-byte bitcast scratch for the w pack
+
+# AnimIntent -> clip row index (built from the bake clip table at setup).
+var _intent_clip := PackedInt32Array()
+
+# tunables (calibrated from the proof asset; shared with VatHordeAdapter)
+var character_scale := 1.0
+var y_offset := 0.19
+var facing_offset := 0.0
+
+# perf instrumentation
+var last_assembly_usec := 0
+var last_live := 0
+
+
+func _init(render_root: Node3D, headless: bool) -> void:
+	_render_root = render_root
+	_headless = headless
+	_scratch.resize(4)
+
+
+# Loads the VAT asset + builds the MultiMesh + the intent→clip table. Returns
+# false (no-op) when headless or on a load failure. Idempotent.
+func setup() -> bool:
+	if _ready:
+		return true
+	if _headless:
+		return false
+	# Runtime-load the source EXR at full RGBAF precision (NOT the imported
+	# .ctex, which VRAM-compresses / may half-float and step the vertices).
+	# load_from_file accepts res:// directly (FileAccess maps it to the project
+	# dir in editor/harness — probe-verified RGBAF, identical to a globalized
+	# path), so do NOT globalize_path: that yields an absolute OS path that can
+	# never resolve inside an exported .pck. NOTE: neither form is export-safe
+	# yet — the raw .exr is not packed into an export (imported assets ship as
+	# .ctex); the export-safe path is the deferred baked .res ImageTexture
+	# (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#599). This adapter is editor/harness-only today.
+	var img := Image.load_from_file(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + ".vat.exr")
+	if img == null:
+		push_error("HordeRenderAdapter: failed to load VAT EXR")
+		return false
+	if img.get_format() != Image.FORMAT_RGBAF:
+		img.convert(Image.FORMAT_RGBAF)
+	var tex := ImageTexture.create_from_image(img)
+
+	var meta_str := FileAccess.get_file_as_string(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + ".vat.json")
+	# JSON.parse_string returns null on malformed input; assigning null to the
+	# typed `_meta: Dictionary` would crash. Type-check before the assign so a
+	# corrupt sidecar fails loud via the is_empty() guard, not a Nil call.
+	var parsed: Variant = JSON.parse_string(meta_str) if meta_str != "" else {}
+	_meta = parsed if parsed is Dictionary else {}
+	if _meta.is_empty():
+		push_error("HordeRenderAdapter: failed to load VAT metadata (missing or malformed JSON)")
+		return false
+
+	var mesh := _load_mesh()
+	if mesh == null:
+		push_error("HordeRenderAdapter: failed to load VAT mesh")
+		return false
+
+	var shader := load(SHADER_PATH) as Shader
+	if shader == null:
+		push_error("HordeRenderAdapter: failed to load shader")
+		return false
+	_mat = ShaderMaterial.new()
+	_mat.shader = shader
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("vat_tex", tex)
+	var cm := load(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + "_colormap.png") as Texture2D
+	if cm != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("colormap", cm)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", _colormap_enabled)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_health_tint", _health_tint_enabled)
+	# The .get default only fires when the key is ABSENT; a literal
+	# "total_rows": 0 from a corrupt bake would otherwise reach the shader and
+	# project every vertex to infinity → silently invisible horde (CR, #597).
+	# Clamp to >= 1 and warn loudly so a bad sidecar is diagnosable, not silent.
+	var raw_rows := int(_meta.get("total_rows", 1))
+	if raw_rows < 1:
+		push_warning(
+			(
+				"HordeRenderAdapter: total_rows=%d (<1) in bake meta — clamping to 1; check the sidecar for a corrupt bake."
+				% raw_rows
+			)
+		)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("total_rows", maxi(raw_rows, 1))
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("fps", float(_meta.get("fps", 24.0)))
+	# A malformed sidecar could carry a non-Array under "clips"; the typed
+	# assignment would then crash, so coerce to [] and let the guards below run.
+	var raw_clips: Variant = _meta.get("clips", [])
+	var clips: Array = raw_clips if raw_clips is Array else []
+	# clip_count guards both ends: mini(...,8) because the shader arrays are [8],
+	# and maxi(...,1) because an empty/absent clip table would otherwise be 0 →
+	# the shader's `clamp(x, 0, clip_count-1)` underflows to -1 and indexes
+	# clip_row_start[-1] (same class as the total_rows guard above, CR #597).
+	var clip_count := maxi(mini(clips.size(), 8), 1)
+	if clips.is_empty():
+		push_warning(
+			"HordeRenderAdapter: bake meta has no clips — clamping clip_count to 1; check the sidecar."
+		)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("clip_count", clip_count)
+	var starts := PackedFloat32Array()
+	var counts := PackedFloat32Array()
+	starts.resize(8)
+	counts.resize(8)
+	# Unused rows default to a safe single-frame count, not 0 — a zero frame
+	# count would make the shader's clip duration collapse (CR #597).
+	counts.fill(1.0)
+	for i in clips.size():
+		if i >= 8:
+			break
+		# Guard non-Dictionary elements (corrupt sidecar): .get() on a non-Dict
+		# crashes; default such rows to clip 0 / single-frame.
+		var clip_data: Variant = clips[i]
+		if clip_data is Dictionary:
+			starts[i] = float(clip_data.get("row_start", 0))
+			# Clamp frame_count >= 1 so an explicit "frame_count": 0 can't reach
+			# the shader as a zero-length clip (CR #597).
+			counts[i] = maxf(float(clip_data.get("frame_count", 1)), 1.0)
+		else:
+			starts[i] = 0.0
+			counts[i] = 1.0
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("clip_row_start", starts)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("clip_frame_count", counts)
+	_build_intent_clip_table(clips)
+
+	_mm = MultiMesh.new()
+	_mm.transform_format = MultiMesh.TRANSFORM_3D
+	_mm.use_custom_data = true
+	_mm.mesh = mesh
+	# the shader displaces VERTEX, so the bind-pose AABB understates extent —
+	# give a generous custom AABB so instances don't cull mid-animation.
+	mesh.custom_aabb = AABB(Vector3(-1.5, -1.5, -1.5), Vector3(3.0, 3.0, 3.0))
+
+	_mmi = MultiMeshInstance3D.new()
+	_mmi.name = "HordeRender"
+	_mmi.multimesh = _mm
+	_mmi.material_override = _mat
+	_mmi.visible = false
+	_render_root.add_child(_mmi)
+
+	_ready = true
+	return true
+
+
+# Map each AnimIntent to a clip row index by NAME preference, falling back to
+# clip 0 (the spec's "Godot maps anim_intent → clip name → numeric index from
+# the bake metadata"). Unknown names resolve to 0 so a sparse proof asset
+# (walk + die) still renders every intent without an out-of-range clip.
+func _build_intent_clip_table(clips: Array) -> void:
+	_intent_clip.resize(ANIM_INTENT_COUNT)
+	_intent_clip.fill(0)
+	var by_name := {}
+	for i in clips.size():
+		var clip_data: Variant = clips[i]
+		if not (clip_data is Dictionary):
+			continue
+		var nm := String(clip_data.get("name", ""))
+		if nm != "":
+			by_name[nm] = i
+	# preference lists per intent (first present name wins; else clip 0)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_IDLE] = _first_clip(by_name, ["idle", "walk"])
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_LOCOMOTION] = _first_clip(by_name, ["walk", "run", "locomotion"])
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_STAGGER] = _first_clip(by_name, ["hit", "stagger", "walk"])
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_BALLISTIC] = _first_clip(by_name, ["fall", "flail", "walk"])
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_DEATH] = _first_clip(by_name, ["die", "death", "walk"])
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_ATTACK] = _first_clip(by_name, ["attack", "walk"])
+
+
+func _first_clip(by_name: Dictionary, names: Array) -> int:
+	for nm in names:
+		if by_name.has(nm):
+			return int(by_name[nm])
+	return 0
+
+
+func set_active(enabled: bool) -> void:
+	_enabled = enabled
+	if _mmi != null:
+		_mmi.visible = enabled and not _headless
+
+
+func is_active() -> bool:
+	return _enabled and _ready and not _headless
+
+
+func set_colormap_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_colormap_enabled = on
+	if _mat != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", on)
+
+
+func is_colormap_enabled() -> bool:
+	return _colormap_enabled
+
+
+# Toggle the dmg-based red health tint (off by default — clean textures).
+func set_health_tint_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_health_tint_enabled = on
+	if _mat != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_health_tint", on)
+
+
+# Per-frame: assemble the MultiMesh buffer from the stride-8 transform buffer +
+# the stride-4 render-state buffer. `render_state` may be empty/short (init
+# race / lock-poison) — missing records default to live-locomotion full-health.
+func update(
+	agent_buf: PackedFloat32Array, render_state: PackedByteArray, count: int, delta: float
+) -> void:
+	if not is_active():
+		return
+	# Defensive clamp: the loop reads agent_buf[s * AGENT_STRIDE ..]; if a
+	# desync ever hands us count > the transform buffer's capacity we'd index
+	# out of bounds and halt the script. Mirrors the short-render_state defense
+	# below. Normal path: count == agent_buf.size() / AGENT_STRIDE (no clamp).
+	var max_agents := agent_buf.size() / AGENT_STRIDE
+	if count > max_agents:
+		push_error(
+			(
+				"HordeRenderAdapter: count %d exceeds agent_buf capacity %d — clamping (FFI desync?)"
+				% [count, max_agents]
+			)
+		)
+		count = max_agents
+	var t0 := Time.get_ticks_usec()
+	_ensure_slots(count)
+	var needed := count * OUT_STRIDE
+	if _out.size() < needed:
+		_out.resize(needed)
+	var rs_len := render_state.size()
+
+	var live := 0
+	for s in count:
+		var bo := s * AGENT_STRIDE
+		var bxx := agent_buf[bo + 0]
+		var byx := agent_buf[bo + 1]
+		var ox := agent_buf[bo + 3]
+		var bxy := agent_buf[bo + 4]
+		var byy := agent_buf[bo + 5]
+		var oy := agent_buf[bo + 7]
+		# dead slots are zeroed by the snapshot writer -> all-zero basis
+		if absf(bxx) < 1e-9 and absf(bxy) < 1e-9 and absf(byx) < 1e-9 and absf(byy) < 1e-9:
+			_was_live[s] = 0
+			continue
+		if _was_live[s] == 0:
+			_clock[s] = 0.0  # (re)born / recycled slot -> fresh clock
+			_was_live[s] = 1
+
+		# render-state record (default to live-locomotion full-health if absent)
+		var ro := s * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		var flags := 0
+		var health := 255
+		var hitstop := 0
+		var intent := ANIM_LOCOMOTION
+		if ro + RENDER_STATE_STRIDE <= rs_len:
+			flags = render_state[ro + RS_FLAGS]
+			health = render_state[ro + RS_HEALTH]
+			hitstop = render_state[ro + RS_HITSTOP]
+			intent = render_state[ro + RS_INTENT]
+
+		# HitStop = freeze this slot's clock; the rest keep advancing.
+		if hitstop == 0:
+			_clock[s] += delta
+
+		# sim heading (sim plane) -> Godot yaw about +Y; sim (X,Y) -> Godot (X,Z)
+		var heading := atan2(bxy, bxx)
+		var b := Basis(Vector3.UP, -heading + facing_offset)
+		if character_scale != 1.0:
+			b = b.scaled(Vector3(character_scale, character_scale, character_scale))
+		var po := live * OUT_STRIDE
+		_out[po + 0] = b.x.x
+		_out[po + 1] = b.y.x
+		_out[po + 2] = b.z.x
+		_out[po + 3] = ox
+		_out[po + 4] = b.x.y
+		_out[po + 5] = b.y.y
+		_out[po + 6] = b.z.y
+		_out[po + 7] = y_offset
+		_out[po + 8] = b.x.z
+		_out[po + 9] = b.y.z
+		_out[po + 10] = b.z.z
+		_out[po + 11] = oy
+		# CUSTOM_DATA v1: x=clock y=clip z=health_frac w=packed-flags-uint
+		_out[po + 12] = _clock[s]
+		_out[po + 13] = float(_intent_clip[clampi(intent, 0, ANIM_INTENT_COUNT - 1)])
+		_out[po + 14] = float(health) / 255.0
+		_out[po + 15] = _pack_flags(flags)
+		live += 1
+
+	_mm.instance_count = live
+	if live > 0:
+		_out.resize(live * OUT_STRIDE)
+		_mm.buffer = _out
+	last_assembly_usec = Time.get_ticks_usec() - t0
+	last_live = live
+
+
+# Bit-reinterpret the flags byte into CUSTOM_DATA.w (the shader decodes it with
+# floatBitsToUint). The value lives in the denormal float range, proven exact
+# by the floatBitsToUint round-trip spike. Higher bytes reserved (gore mask).
+func _pack_flags(flags: int) -> float:
+	_scratch.encode_u32(0, flags & 0xFFFFFFFF)
+	return _scratch.decode_float(0)
+
+
+func debug_status() -> String:
+	if not is_active():
+		return "Horde3D: off"
+	return "Horde3D: %d inst, asm %.2fms" % [last_live, last_assembly_usec / 1000.0]
+
+
+func _ensure_slots(count: int) -> void:
+	if _clock.size() >= count:
+		return
+	_clock.resize(count)
+	_was_live.resize(count)
+
+
+func _load_mesh() -> Mesh:
+	var scene := load(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + ".vat.glb") as PackedScene
+	if scene == null:
+		return null
+	var root := scene.instantiate()
+	if root == null:
+		return null
+	var mi := _find_mesh(root)
+	var mesh: Mesh = null
+	if mi != null:
+		mesh = mi.mesh
+	# `root` was instantiated but never add_child'd — it has no SceneTree, so
+	# queue_free() (which defers to the tree's end-of-frame sweep) would never
+	# run and the scene would leak. free() deallocates the orphan immediately.
+	# `mesh` is a Resource ref and survives the node teardown.
+	root.free()
+	return mesh
+
+
+func _find_mesh(n: Node) -> MeshInstance3D:
+	if n is MeshInstance3D:
+		return n
+	for c in n.get_children():
+		var r := _find_mesh(c)
+		if r != null:
+			return r
+	return null

@@ -138,6 +150,33 @@ func setup_player_proxy(is_headless: bool) -> void:
 		visual = stub

 	_player_3d_proxy.add_child(visual)
+	# TEMP placeholder weapon (ADR-029 P2b PR3 — 3D-artist preview). A STATIC

+	if not is_headless and _player_3d_proxy.get_node_or_null("Weapon") == null:

+				weapon.rotation.y = deg_to_rad(WEAPON_YAW_DEG)
+				_player_3d_proxy.add_child(weapon)
+			elif instance != null:
+				# instantiate() succeeded but the root isn't a Node3D — free the
+				# orphan so the failed cast doesn't leak it (GCA #597).
+				instance.free()
+		else:
+			push_warning(
+				(
+					"Render3DAdapter: weapon .glb at %s failed to load (run --import?)"
+					% WEAPON_GLB_PATH
+				)
+			)
 
 
 ## Populate the VehicleProxy3D body's visual child once (#567). Mirrors
@@ -193,10 +232,13 @@ func setup_camera(
 	camera_zoom: float,
 	viewport_h: float,
 	fov_deg: float,
-	pitch_deg: float
+	pitch_deg: float,
+	distance_scale: float = 1.0

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/VatAssemblyBench.gd b/apps/game-godot/VatAssemblyBench.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c99326d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/VatAssemblyBench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,202 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR3 — SPIKE 2: production VAT assembly cost (measurement gate).
+#
+# Decides the "Rust transform graduation" fork: do we keep the Godot-side buffer
+# assembly (P2a measured 0.26ms/512 for a bare transform copy) or graduate it to a
+# Rust get_agent_buffer_3d()? That call is justified ONLY if the *production* path —
+# 400 agents, full render-state eval + clip mapping + 16-float pack — shows cost.
+# P2a only measured a bare transform copy; this measures the real per-slot work.
+#
+# PURE CPU: this times the GDScript assembly, NOT rendering or GPU upload — so it
+# runs HEADLESS (no real FFI / GPU needed). The render-state buffer is SYNTHETIC
+# (the get_agent_render_state_buffer() FFI lands in PR3 proper); the per-slot WORK
+# is faithful to what that adapter will do (decode bits -> anim_intent -> clip,
+# basis-from-heading, bitcast-pack CUSTOM_DATA.w). Honest upper bound: the number
+# here is >= production (synthetic input is dense/all-live; no early-out on culled).
+#
+# Run (headless OK):
+#   <godot> --headless --path apps/game-godot res://vat_assembly_bench.tscn
+# Prints [asm-bench] lines (min/avg/p99 usec per assembly) and quits.
+
+const AGENT_STRIDE := 8  # sim get_agent_buffer() Transform2D stride
+const OUT_STRIDE := 16  # MultiMesh3D: 12 Transform3D + 4 CUSTOM_DATA
+const ITERS := 600  # assembly iterations timed per N (drop warm-up)
+const WARMUP := 60
+const COUNTS: Array[int] = [256, 400, 512, 1000]
+
+# horde-state bit protocol (mirror of density_feedback.rs — read-only here)
+const LIVE_BIT := 0x04
+const BALLISTIC_BIT := 0x10
+const STUCK_BIT := 0x80
+const HORDE_STATE_MASK := 0x03
+
+# anim_intent enum — values mirror the canonical AnimIntent (density_feedback.rs).
+# This bench DERIVES a representative intent from synthetic flags as a
+# conservative upper bound on per-slot work; the production adapter reads the
+# precomputed RS_INTENT byte (cheaper), so the measurement over-counts if
+# anything. Values kept identical to the wire enum so a future diff against the
+# adapter stays honest (CR / Greptile P2, #597). ATTACK is reserved (unused).
+const INTENT_IDLE := 0
+const INTENT_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const INTENT_STAGGER := 2
+const INTENT_BALLISTIC := 3
+const INTENT_DEATH := 4
+const INTENT_ATTACK := 5
+
+var _agent := PackedFloat32Array()  # synthetic stride-8 transforms
+var _state := PackedByteArray()  # synthetic render-state bytes (1/slot)
+var _health := PackedByteArray()  # synthetic health bytes (0..255)
+var _hitstop := PackedByteArray()  # synthetic hitstop remaining frames
+var _out := PackedFloat32Array()  # assembly target (reused)
+var _scratch := PackedByteArray()  # 4-byte bitcast scratch for the w pack
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	_scratch.resize(4)
+	print("[asm-bench] start; headless=", DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless")
+	for n in COUNTS:
+		_run_count(n)
+	print("[asm-bench] DONE")
+	get_tree().quit(0)
+
+
+func _run_count(n: int) -> void:
+	_seed(n)
+	var samples := PackedFloat64Array()
+	samples.resize(ITERS)
+	for it in ITERS + WARMUP:
+		# vary the per-slot clock by iteration so nothing constant-folds (and the
+		# pack input changes), faithful to a live frame where clocks advance.
+		var t0 := Time.get_ticks_usec()
+		var live := _assemble(n, float(it) * 0.016)
+		var dt := Time.get_ticks_usec() - t0
+		if it >= WARMUP:
+			samples[it - WARMUP] = float(dt)
+		if it == WARMUP:
+			# sanity: report live count once (synthetic is all-live by construction)
+			_last_live = live
+	var sorted := Array(samples)
+	sorted.sort()
+	var mn: float = sorted[0]
+	var p99: float = sorted[int(floor(float(sorted.size() - 1) * 0.99))]
+	var sum := 0.0
+	for v in sorted:
+		sum += v
+	var avg := sum / float(sorted.size())
+	print(
+		(
+			"[asm-bench] N=%d live=%d min=%.1fus avg=%.1fus p99=%.1fus (%.3fms avg)"
+			% [n, _last_live, mn, avg, p99, avg / 1000.0]
+		)
+	)
+
+
+var _last_live := 0
+
+
+# One frame of production-representative assembly. Returns the live count.
+func _assemble(n: int, clock_base: float) -> int:
+	var needed := n * OUT_STRIDE
+	if _out.size() < needed:
+		_out.resize(needed)
+	var live := 0
+	for s in n:
+		var bo := s * AGENT_STRIDE
+		var bxx := _agent[bo + 0]
+		var byx := _agent[bo + 1]
+		var ox := _agent[bo + 3]
+		var bxy := _agent[bo + 4]
+		var byy := _agent[bo + 5]
+		var oy := _agent[bo + 7]
+		# dead-slot mask (zeroed basis) — same predicate the adapter uses
+		if absf(bxx) < 1e-9 and absf(bxy) < 1e-9 and absf(byx) < 1e-9 and absf(byy) < 1e-9:
+			continue
+
+		# --- render-state eval (the new per-slot work PR3 adds) ---
+		var st := _state[s]
+		var health_frac := float(_health[s]) / 255.0
+		var hitstop := _hitstop[s]
+		# Canonical priority is Death > Stagger > Ballistic > Idle
+		# (derive_anim_intent). The synthetic state byte has no Staggered bit, so
+		# hitstop > 0 proxies for stagger here — and the proxy is checked AFTER
+		# ballistic, a deliberate deviation from the canonical order. It only
+		# shifts which synthetic slots pick which clip; the per-slot work this
+		# bench measures is identical either way (CR #597).
+		var intent := INTENT_LOCOMOTION
+		if (st & LIVE_BIT) == 0:
+			intent = INTENT_DEATH
+		elif (st & BALLISTIC_BIT) != 0:
+			intent = INTENT_BALLISTIC
+		elif hitstop > 0:
+			intent = INTENT_STAGGER
+		elif (st & STUCK_BIT) != 0:
+			intent = INTENT_IDLE
+		var clip := float(intent)
+
+		# --- bitcast pack: CUSTOM_DATA.w = flags|intent|seg via floatBitsToUint round-trip ---
+		var flags := st & (LIVE_BIT | BALLISTIC_BIT | STUCK_BIT | HORDE_STATE_MASK)
+		var seg := 0  # reserved gore segment mask (0 in PR3)
+		var packed := (flags & 0xFF) | ((intent & 0xFF) << 8) | ((seg & 0xFFFF) << 16)
+		_scratch.encode_u32(0, packed)
+		var w := _scratch.decode_float(0)
+
+		# --- transform build (basis from heading; sim (X,Y) -> Godot (X,Z)) ---
+		var heading := atan2(bxy, bxx)
+		var b := Basis(Vector3.UP, -heading)
+		var po := live * OUT_STRIDE
+		_out[po + 0] = b.x.x
+		_out[po + 1] = b.y.x
+		_out[po + 2] = b.z.x
+		_out[po + 3] = ox
+		_out[po + 4] = b.x.y
+		_out[po + 5] = b.y.y
+		_out[po + 6] = b.z.y
+		_out[po + 7] = 0.19
+		_out[po + 8] = b.x.z
+		_out[po + 9] = b.y.z
+		_out[po + 10] = b.z.z
+		_out[po + 11] = oy
+		# CUSTOM_DATA v1: x=clock y=clip z=health_frac w=packed-flags
+		_out[po + 12] = clock_base + float(s) * 0.013
+		_out[po + 13] = clip
+		_out[po + 14] = health_frac
+		_out[po + 15] = w
+		live += 1
+	return live
+
+
+# Synthetic but representative inputs: all-live dense horde (worst case for asm),
+# varied heading/state/health so the per-slot branches actually diverge.
+func _seed(n: int) -> void:
+	_agent.resize(n * AGENT_STRIDE)
+	_state.resize(n)
+	_health.resize(n)
+	_hitstop.resize(n)
+	var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(n))))
+	for i in n:
+		var gx := float(i % side) * 2.0
+		var gy := float(i / side) * 2.0
+		var ang := float(i) * 0.7
+		var cs := cos(ang)
+		var sn := sin(ang)
+		var o := i * AGENT_STRIDE
+		_agent[o + 0] = cs
+		_agent[o + 1] = -sn
+		_agent[o + 2] = 0.0
+		_agent[o + 3] = gx
+		_agent[o + 4] = sn
+		_agent[o + 5] = cs
+		_agent[o + 6] = 0.0
+		_agent[o + 7] = gy
+		# state: mostly live-locomotion, a sprinkling of ballistic/stuck/dead
+		var st := LIVE_BIT | (i % 3)  # HordeState 0..2 in low bits
+		if i % 11 == 0:
+			st |= BALLISTIC_BIT
+		if i % 7 == 0:
+			st |= STUCK_BIT
+		if i % 23 == 0:
+			st &= ~LIVE_BIT  # a few dead -> death intent (still rendered this frame)
+		_state[i] = st
+		_health[i] = (i * 37) % 256
+		_hitstop[i] = 3 if (i % 13 == 0) else 0
