diff --git a/apps/game-godot/VatBitpackProbe.gd b/apps/game-godot/VatBitpackProbe.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..b2c9e55
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/VatBitpackProbe.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,221 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR3 — SPIKE 1: floatBitsToUint round-trip on INSTANCE_CUSTOM.
+#
+# Gates the CUSTOM_DATA.w packing decision (spec .totem/specs/p2b-3d-horde.md,
+# measurement gate 1). Packs a battery of known 32-bit patterns into the w channel
+# of a stride-16 MultiMesh buffer (the SAME storage path the production adapter
+# uses), and a self-checking shader emits GREEN per instance iff floatBitsToUint(w)
+# returns the exact bits (ground truth via a clean uniform, not the channel under
+# test). The CPU reads back one pixel per instance and reports PASS/FAIL per class.
+#
+# WHY it matters: the realistic pack (flags|intent|seg in the low 24 bits, high byte
+# zero) lands in the *denormal / zero-exponent* float range — exactly where a fp16
+# custom-data channel or a flush-to-zero driver would corrupt it. The denormal +
+# bitfield patterns below are the load-bearing cases; NaN/Inf test canonicalisation.
+#
+# Needs a REAL GPU (the shader must rasterise) — headless -> no-op + quit, like the
+# VAT path's headless guard. Run:
+#   <godot> --path apps/game-godot res://vat_bitpack_probe.tscn
+# Prints [bitpack] PASS/FAIL lines + a verdict, saves a capture, then quits.
+
+const SHADER_PATH := "res://shaders/vat_bitpack_probe.gdshader"
+const OUT_STRIDE := 16
+const SPACING := 2.0
+const SETTLE_FRAMES := 12  # let the GPU rasterise before readback
+const CAP_DIR := "user://vat_bitpack_probe/"
+
+# [pattern_u32, label, class] — class groups the verdict (finite / denormal / inf / nan).
+const PATTERNS: Array = [
+	[0x00000000, "zero", "finite"],
+	[0x80000000, "neg_zero", "finite"],
+	[0x3F800000, "one_1.0", "finite"],
+	[0xBF800000, "neg_one", "finite"],
+	[0x40490FDB, "pi_low_mantissa", "finite"],
+	[0xDEADBEEF, "arb_DEADBEEF", "finite"],
+	[0x12345678, "arb_12345678", "finite"],
+	[0xAAAAAAAA, "alt_AAAA", "finite"],
+	[0x55555555, "alt_5555", "finite"],
+	[0x3F800003, "normal_low_bits", "finite"],
+	[0x7F7FFFFF, "max_normal", "finite"],
+	[0x00800000, "min_normal", "finite"],
+	[0x00000001, "min_denormal", "denormal"],
+	[0x007FFFFF, "max_denormal", "denormal"],
+	[0x0000FFFF, "low16_denormal", "denormal"],
+	[0x0003A5C7, "pack_clip_flags_seg", "denormal"],  # realistic w-pack (low 24 bits)
+	[0x00A50003, "pack_alt", "denormal"],  # realistic w-pack
+	[0x00FF00FF, "pack_bytes", "denormal"],  # realistic w-pack
+	[0x7F800000, "pos_inf", "inf"],
+	[0xFF800000, "neg_inf", "inf"],
+	[0x7FC00000, "qNaN", "nan"],
+	[0x7FA00000, "sNaN", "nan"],
+	[0xFFFFFFFF, "all_ones", "nan"],
+]
+
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _mmi: MultiMeshInstance3D
+var _frame := 0
+var _done := false
+var _scratch := PackedByteArray()
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	_scratch.resize(4)
+	if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless":
+		print("[bitpack] headless — cannot rasterise the shader; no-op + quit")
+		get_tree().quit(0)
+		return
+
+	var n := PATTERNS.size()
+	var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(n))))
+
+	# camera: orthographic, on +Z looking at the XY grid (quads face +Z -> full-on)
+	_cam = Camera3D.new()
+	_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_ORTHOGONAL
+	var extent := float(side) * SPACING
+	_cam.size = extent + SPACING
+	var cx := float(side - 1) * SPACING * 0.5
+	var cy := -float(int(ceil(float(n) / float(side))) - 1) * SPACING * 0.5
+	_cam.position = Vector3(cx, cy, 20.0)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(cx, cy, 0.0), Vector3.UP)
+	_cam.current = true
+	add_child(_cam)
+
+	# MultiMesh — SAME storage path as the production adapter (TRANSFORM_3D + custom data)
+	var mm := MultiMesh.new()
+	mm.transform_format = MultiMesh.TRANSFORM_3D
+	mm.use_custom_data = true
+	var quad := QuadMesh.new()
+	quad.size = Vector2(1.6, 1.6)
+	mm.mesh = quad
+
+	var mat := ShaderMaterial.new()
+	mat.shader = load(SHADER_PATH) as Shader
+
+	# ground truth: each u32 as its two's-complement int32 (clean uniform, full int32)
+	var expected := PackedInt32Array()
+	expected.resize(32)  # matches the shader's expected[32]; padding never indexed
+	for i in n:
+		var p: int = PATTERNS[i][0]
+		expected[i] = p if p < 0x80000000 else p - 0x100000000
+	mat.set_shader_parameter("expected", expected)
+
+	# buffer: 16 floats/instance; transform = identity at grid pos; w = bitcast(pattern)
+	var buf := PackedFloat32Array()
+	buf.resize(n * OUT_STRIDE)
+	for i in n:
+		var gx := float(i % side) * SPACING
+		var gy := -float(i / side) * SPACING
+		var po := i * OUT_STRIDE
+		# identity basis, translation (gx, gy, 0)
+		buf[po + 0] = 1.0
+		buf[po + 3] = gx
+		buf[po + 5] = 1.0
+		buf[po + 7] = gy
+		buf[po + 10] = 1.0
+		buf[po + 11] = 0.0
+		# CUSTOM_DATA: x/y/z unused, w = the bit pattern under test
+		buf[po + 15] = _bitcast(PATTERNS[i][0])
+	mm.instance_count = n
+	mm.buffer = buf
+
+	_mmi = MultiMeshInstance3D.new()
+	_mmi.multimesh = mm
+	_mmi.material_override = mat
+	add_child(_mmi)
+
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(CAP_DIR))
+	print("[bitpack] start; patterns=", n)
+
+
+func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
+	if _done:
+		return
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame < SETTLE_FRAMES:
+		return
+	_done = true
+	_verify()
+	get_tree().quit(0)
+
+
+func _verify() -> void:
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img == null:
+		print("[bitpack] FAIL: no viewport image (cannot read back)")
+		return
+	var w := img.get_width()
+	var h := img.get_height()
+	var n := PATTERNS.size()
+	var fail_by_class := {"finite": 0, "denormal": 0, "inf": 0, "nan": 0}
+	var total_by_class := {"finite": 0, "denormal": 0, "inf": 0, "nan": 0}
+	var fails := 0
+
+	for i in n:
+		var p: int = PATTERNS[i][0]
+		var label: String = PATTERNS[i][1]
+		var cls: String = PATTERNS[i][2]
+		total_by_class[cls] += 1
+		# grid pos exactly as built in _ready()
+		var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(n))))
+		var world := Vector3(float(i % side) * SPACING, -float(i / side) * SPACING, 0.0)
+		var sp := _cam.unproject_position(world)
+		var ok := _sample_pass(img, w, h, sp)
+		if not ok:
+			fails += 1
+			fail_by_class[cls] += 1
+		print("[bitpack] %s 0x%08X %s (%s)" % ["PASS" if ok else "FAIL", p, label, cls])
+
+	var shot := CAP_DIR + "bitpack.png"
+	img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(shot))
+	print("[bitpack] capture %s" % shot)
+
+	print(
+		(
+			"[bitpack] by-class fails: finite=%d/%d denormal=%d/%d inf=%d/%d nan=%d/%d"
+			% [
+				fail_by_class["finite"],
+				total_by_class["finite"],
+				fail_by_class["denormal"],
+				total_by_class["denormal"],
+				fail_by_class["inf"],
+				total_by_class["inf"],
+				fail_by_class["nan"],
+				total_by_class["nan"],
+			]
+		)
+	)
+	# VERDICT: the production w-pack lives in the finite + denormal classes.
+	var pack_ok: bool = fail_by_class["finite"] == 0 and fail_by_class["denormal"] == 0
+	if fails == 0:
+		print("[bitpack] VERDICT: GO — full 32-bit round-trip exact (all classes incl. NaN/Inf)")
+	elif pack_ok:
+		print(
+			"[bitpack] VERDICT: GO (constrained) — finite+denormal exact; only NaN/Inf differ (pack avoids those)"
+		)
+	else:
+		print(
+			"[bitpack] VERDICT: NO-GO — finite/denormal corruption; w falls back to flags-only finite-normal subset"
+		)
+
+
+# argmax(green vs red) over a 3x3 neighbourhood — robust to sRGB/tonemap.
+func _sample_pass(img: Image, w: int, h: int, sp: Vector2) -> bool:
+	var votes := 0
+	var samples := 0
+	for dy in range(-1, 2):
+		for dx in range(-1, 2):
+			var x := int(round(sp.x)) + dx
+			var y := int(round(sp.y)) + dy
+			if x < 0 or y < 0 or x >= w or y >= h:
+				continue
+			var c := img.get_pixel(x, y)
+			samples += 1
+			if c.g > c.r:
+				votes += 1
+	return samples > 0 and votes * 2 > samples
+
+
+func _bitcast(u: int) -> float:
+	_scratch.encode_u32(0, u)
+	return _scratch.decode_float(0)

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/horde_render_bench.gd b/apps/game-godot/horde_render_bench.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..bbd016c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/horde_render_bench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,218 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR3 — production HordeRenderAdapter verification bench.
+#
+# Drives the production adapter from a SYNTHETIC stride-8 transform buffer + a
+# SYNTHETIC stride-4 render-state buffer (the get_agent_render_state_buffer()
+# FFI shape), exercising the NEW behaviors the disposable proof never did:
+#   - intent → clip selection (varied AnimIntent per slot),
+#   - HitStop clock-freeze (a slot with hitstop > 0 holds while the rest run),
+#   - health tint (z channel) + the packed-flags w channel (ballistic accent),
+#   - dead-slot masking (zeroed transform → not drawn).
+#
+# Asserts the CPU-side logic programmatically (clip table + clock freeze +
+# live count), then captures a frame for eyeballing. Needs a real GPU — run:
+#   <godot> --path apps/game-godot res://horde_render_bench.tscn
+# Prints [horde-bench] PASS/FAIL lines + a verdict, then quits.
+
+const N := 64
+const SPACING := 2.0
+const DELTA := 0.1
+const FRAMES := 4  # advance the clock a few frames before asserting
+const CAP_DIR := "user://horde_render_bench/"
+
+# mirror of HordeRenderAdapter's AnimIntent / render-state layout
+const ANIM_IDLE := 0
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const ANIM_STAGGER := 2
+const ANIM_BALLISTIC := 3
+const ANIM_DEATH := 4
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const LIVE_BIT := 0x04
+const BALLISTIC_BIT := 0x10
+
+var _adapter: HordeRenderAdapter
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _xf := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _rs := PackedByteArray()
+var _frame := 0
+var _done := false
+# slots reserved for the hitstop-freeze assertion
+var _frozen_slot := 5
+var _running_slot := 6
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless":
+		print("[horde-bench] headless — cannot rasterise; no-op + quit")
+		get_tree().quit(0)
+		return
+	_cam = Camera3D.new()
+	_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_PERSPECTIVE
+	_cam.fov = 55.0
+	_cam.current = true
+	add_child(_cam)
+	_frame_camera(N)
+
+	_adapter = HordeRenderAdapter.new(self, false)
+	if not _adapter.setup():
+		print("[horde-bench] FAIL: adapter setup returned false")
+		get_tree().quit(1)
+		return
+	_adapter.set_active(true)
+	_build_buffers()
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(CAP_DIR))
+	print(
+		(
+			"[horde-bench] start; N=%d clips=%s"
+			% [N, str(_adapter._meta.get("clips", []).map(func(c): return c.get("name", "?")))]
+		)
+	)
+
+
+func _frame_camera(n: int) -> void:
+	var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(n))))
+	var extent := float(side) * SPACING
+	var c := extent * 0.5
+	_cam.position = Vector3(c, extent * 0.95, extent * 1.45)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(c, 0.5, c), Vector3.UP)
+
+
+func _build_buffers() -> void:
+	_xf.resize(N * 8)
+	_rs.resize(N * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE)
+	var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(N))))
+	for i in N:
+		var gx := float(i % side) * SPACING
+		var gy := float(i / side) * SPACING
+		var ang := float(i) * 0.7
+		var cs := cos(ang)
+		var sn := sin(ang)
+		var o := i * 8
+		# Transform2D: [bx.x, by.x, 0, ox, bx.y, by.y, 0, oy]
+		_xf[o + 0] = cs
+		_xf[o + 1] = -sn
+		_xf[o + 2] = 0.0
+		_xf[o + 3] = gx
+		_xf[o + 4] = sn
+		_xf[o + 5] = cs
+		_xf[o + 6] = 0.0
+		_xf[o + 7] = gy
+		# render-state record
+		var intent := i % 5  # cycle Idle/Locomotion/Stagger/Ballistic/Death
+		var health := (i * 37) % 256
+		var hitstop := 0
+		if i == _frozen_slot:
+			hitstop = 6  # this slot must NOT advance its clock
+			intent = ANIM_LOCOMOTION
+		if i == _running_slot:
+			intent = ANIM_LOCOMOTION  # control: must advance
+		# Compute flags from the FINAL intent, mirroring the production ABI:
+		# LIVE_BIT is clear for Death (CR #597) so the synthetic dataset never
+		# ships a contradictory LIVE_BIT + ANIM_DEATH record.
+		var flags := 0
+		if intent != ANIM_DEATH:
+			flags |= LIVE_BIT
+		if intent == ANIM_BALLISTIC:
+			flags |= BALLISTIC_BIT
+		var ro := i * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		_rs[ro + 0] = flags
+		_rs[ro + 1] = health
+		_rs[ro + 2] = hitstop
+		_rs[ro + 3] = intent
+	# slot 0 = a dead slot (zeroed transform) → must be masked out of the draw
+	for k in 8:
+		_xf[k] = 0.0
+
+
+func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
+	if _done:
+		return
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, N, DELTA)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame < FRAMES:
+		return
+	_done = true
+	var fails := _verify()
+	# Exit non-zero on any assertion failure so the bench can gate automation
+	# instead of always reporting success (CR #597).
+	get_tree().quit(0 if fails == 0 else 1)
+
+
+func _verify() -> int:
+	var fails := 0
+
+	# 1. intent → clip table well-formed + differentiates when ≥2 named clips.
+	var clips: Array = _adapter._meta.get("clips", [])
+	var clip_count := clips.size()
+	var loco_clip: int = _adapter._intent_clip[ANIM_LOCOMOTION]
+	var death_clip: int = _adapter._intent_clip[ANIM_DEATH]
+	if loco_clip < 0 or loco_clip >= clip_count or death_clip < 0 or death_clip >= clip_count:
+		fails += 1
+		print(
+			(
+				"[horde-bench] FAIL: clip out of range (Locomotion=%d, Death=%d, valid=0..%d)"
+				% [loco_clip, death_clip, clip_count - 1]
+			)
+		)
+	else:
+		print("[horde-bench] PASS: Locomotion → clip %d, Death → clip %d" % [loco_clip, death_clip])
+	if clip_count >= 2 and loco_clip == death_clip:
+		fails += 1
+		print(
+			"[horde-bench] FAIL: Locomotion and Death map to the same clip despite ≥2 named clips"
+		)
+	elif clip_count >= 2:
+		print("[horde-bench] PASS: intents differentiate (Locomotion ≠ Death clip)")
+
+	# 2. HitStop clock-freeze: the frozen slot held; the control advanced.
+	var frozen_clock: float = _adapter._clock[_frozen_slot]
+	var running_clock: float = _adapter._clock[_running_slot]
+	if frozen_clock != 0.0:
+		fails += 1
+		print(
+			"[horde-bench] FAIL: hitstop slot clock advanced to %.3f (must stay 0)" % frozen_clock
+		)
+	else:
+		print("[horde-bench] PASS: hitstop slot clock frozen at 0")
+	var expected_running := float(FRAMES) * DELTA
+	if absf(running_clock - expected_running) > 1e-4:
+		fails += 1
+		print(
+			(
+				"[horde-bench] FAIL: running slot clock %.3f, expected %.3f"
+				% [running_clock, expected_running]
+			)
+		)
+	else:
+		print("[horde-bench] PASS: running slot clock advanced to %.3f" % running_clock)
+
+	# 3. dead-slot masking: slot 0 zeroed → live count is N-1.
+	if _adapter.last_live != N - 1:
+		fails += 1
+		print(
+			(
+				"[horde-bench] FAIL: live=%d, expected %d (one dead slot masked)"
+				% [_adapter.last_live, N - 1]
+			)
+		)
+	else:
+		print("[horde-bench] PASS: dead slot masked (live=%d)" % _adapter.last_live)
+
+	# capture for eyeballing (health gradient + ballistic accents + animation)
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img != null:
+		var path := CAP_DIR + "horde_render.png"
+		img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+		print("[horde-bench] capture %s" % path)
+
+	if fails == 0:
+		print(
+			(
+				"[horde-bench] VERDICT: GO — production adapter logic verified (asm %.3fms @ %d)"
+				% [_adapter.last_assembly_usec / 1000.0, _adapter.last_live]
+			)
+		)
+	else:
+		print("[horde-bench] VERDICT: FAIL — %d assertion(s) failed" % fails)
+	return fails

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/shaders/horde_render.gdshader b/apps/game-godot/shaders/horde_render.gdshader
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..0c5e26a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/shaders/horde_render.gdshader
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+shader_type spatial;
+render_mode unshaded;
+
+// ADR-029 P2b PR3 — production VAT horde shader (CUSTOM_DATA v1, the stable
+// P2b ABI). Successor to the disposable proof shader `vat_horde.gdshader`.
+//
+// Vertex positions come ENTIRELY from the baked float VAT texture (UV2.x =
+// the original vertex index as (index+0.5)/N — survives Godot vertex
+// splitting; row = animation frame from a PER-INSTANCE clock). There is NO
+// global TIME, so HitStop is the CPU-side adapter declining to advance a
+// slot's clock while the rest animate.
+//
+// INSTANCE_CUSTOM v1 (proven bit-exact through the channel by the
+// floatBitsToUint round-trip spike — finite + denormal classes all exact):
+//   x = anim clock (seconds, CPU-owned, stoppable for HitStop)
+//   y = clip index (numeric, from the bake clip table)
+//   z = health fraction [0,1] (unquantized — gore/dissolve math wants it raw)
+//   w = packed flags uint: floatBitsToUint(w) yields the Rust render-state
+//       flags byte (LIVE / BALLISTIC + reserved gore mask). The realistic
+//       pack lives in the denormal range, which the spike proved exact.
+
+uniform sampler2D vat_tex : filter_nearest, repeat_disable;
+uniform int total_rows = 1;
+uniform float fps = 24.0;
+uniform int clip_count = 1;
+uniform float clip_row_start[8];
+uniform float clip_frame_count[8];
+uniform vec3 base_color : source_color = vec3(0.30, 0.55, 0.30);
+uniform vec3 hurt_color : source_color = vec3(1.0, 0.05, 0.05);
+uniform vec3 ballistic_color : source_color = vec3(0.86, 0.66, 0.22);
+uniform bool use_colormap = false; // OFF = flat tint (clean perf path)
+uniform sampler2D colormap : filter_nearest, source_color; // Kenney palette (UV lookup)
+// ADR-029 P2b PR3: the dmg-based red health tint is OFF by default so the clean
+// Kenney textures show for the 3D-artist preview; flip on to re-enable health viz.
+uniform bool use_health_tint = false;
+
+// Flag bits — mirror of the Rust render-state flags byte (density_feedback.rs
+// BALLISTIC_BIT). Decoded from the packed `w` channel to prove the
+// floatBitsToUint ABI end-to-end; the gore mask (high bytes) is reserved.
+const uint BALLISTIC_BIT = 0x10u;
+
+varying float v_health;
+varying flat float v_ballistic;
+
+void vertex() {
+	// Self-safe clip index: setup() guards clip_count into [1, 8], but the
+	// shader must not trust the uniform — clamp into [0, 7] so neither a 0
+	// (underflow to -1) nor a clip_count/index > 8 can read past the
+	// 8-element clip_row_start / clip_frame_count uniforms (CR #597).
+	int max_clip = max(min(clip_count, 8) - 1, 0);
+	int clip = clamp(int(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.y + 0.5), 0, max_clip);
+	float cstart = clip_row_start[clip];
+	float ccount = max(clip_frame_count[clip], 1.0);
+	float dur = ccount / max(fps, 0.001); // guard div-by-zero on the fps uniform
+	float local = fract(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.x / dur); // [0,1) looped per-instance clock
+	float frame = min(floor(local * ccount), ccount - 1.0);
+	float v = (cstart + frame + 0.5) / float(total_rows);
+	VERTEX = textureLod(vat_tex, vec2(UV2.x, v), 0.0).xyz;
+	v_health = clamp(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.z, 0.0, 1.0);
+	uint flags = floatBitsToUint(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.w); // proven bit-exact (spike 1)
+	v_ballistic = ((flags & BALLISTIC_BIT) != 0u) ? 1.0 : 0.0;
+}
+
+void fragment() {
+	vec3 body = use_colormap ? texture(colormap, UV).rgb : base_color;
+	// Health tint: lerp toward hurt as health drops (z = health fraction). Gated
+	// OFF by default (use_health_tint) — clean Kenney textures for the preview.
+	if (use_health_tint) {
+		// Squared curve so damage reads EARLY (ADR-029 P2b PR3 showcase): a single
+		// pistol hit (70% health) already shows clearly red — `v_health*v_health`
+		// maps 0.7 → 0.49 (≈half red) instead of the faint 0.7 a linear lerp gives.
+		body = mix(hurt_color, body, v_health * v_health);
+	}
+	// Ballistic accent, decoded from the packed flags uint (exercises the
+	// CUSTOM_DATA.w ABI; gore-driven dissolve lands on the reserved mask).
+	body = mix(body, ballistic_color, 0.5 * v_ballistic);
+	ALBEDO = body;
+}

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/shaders/vat_bitpack_probe.gdshader b/apps/game-godot/shaders/vat_bitpack_probe.gdshader
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..04d291b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/shaders/vat_bitpack_probe.gdshader
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+shader_type spatial;
+render_mode unshaded;
+
+// ADR-029 P2b PR3 — SPIKE 1: floatBitsToUint round-trip on INSTANCE_CUSTOM.
+//
+// Gates CUSTOM_DATA.w packing: can a full 32-bit uint survive the
+// GDScript -> MultiMesh stride-16 buffer -> vertex shader -> floatBitsToUint()
+// path BIT-EXACT in Godot 4.6 (i.e. is the per-instance custom-data channel real
+// fp32, not fp16 / NaN-canonicalised / denormal-flushed)? If yes, w can carry a
+// packed uint; if not, w falls back to a flags-only finite-float subset.
+//
+// Self-checking: the EXPECTED uint arrives via a clean `expected[]` uniform (full
+// int32, NOT through the custom-data channel under test). The shader decodes the
+// channel-under-test with floatBitsToUint and emits GREEN iff the bits match the
+// ground truth, RED otherwise. CPU reads back one pixel per instance and classifies
+// by argmax(channel) — robust to sRGB/tonemap (a primary green stays > red).
+
+const int MAX_PATTERNS = 32;
+uniform int expected[MAX_PATTERNS]; // ground truth, two's-complement reinterpret of the u32
+
+varying flat float v_ok;
+
+void vertex() {
+	uint got = floatBitsToUint(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.w);      // the channel under test
+	uint want = uint(expected[INSTANCE_ID]);            // int->uint = bit reinterpret (GLSL)
+	v_ok = (got == want) ? 1.0 : 0.0;
+}
+
+void fragment() {
+	ALBEDO = (v_ok > 0.5) ? vec3(0.0, 1.0, 0.0) : vec3(1.0, 0.0, 0.0);
+}

+/// Semantic animation intent for an agent's render-LOD-0 / VAT clip
+/// selection (ADR-029 P2b). NET-NEW: the sim has no animation-state enum
+/// today — [`HordeState`] is *density* classification, not animation.
+///
+/// The sim publishes *intent* (what the agent is doing), NOT a clip id or
+/// a raw velocity; the Godot render adapter maps intent → clip name →
+/// numeric clip index from the bake metadata (the render-ownership split,
+/// ADR-029 fold). A pure function of the agent's components, so the byte is
+/// determinism-clean (ADR-013): same seed + input stream → same intent.
+///
+/// `#[repr(u8)]` values are the wire contract the GDScript adapter mirrors
+/// (the mirror + its parity test land with the production adapter in the
+/// next P2b slice). `Attack` is RESERVED — published only when the sim
+/// grows an attack component to drive it (none exists in P2b PR3), per the
+/// cohort fold "the attack clip lands only if the sim has an attack intent
+/// to publish."
+#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Default, Eq, PartialEq)]
+#[repr(u8)]
+pub enum AnimIntent {
+    /// Live, at rest — per-tick translation below the idle cutoff.
+    #[default]
+    Idle = 0,
+    /// Live, moving under its own locomotion.
+    Locomotion = 1,
+    /// Poise-broken: carries [`Staggered`].
+    Stagger = 2,
+    /// In-flight: carries [`BallisticMode`] (catapulted / tossed / launched).
+    Ballistic = 3,
+    /// Dying/dead this frame ([`DeadTag`] or `Health::is_dead`). Published
+    /// for ABI completeness; true death PLAYBACK is deferred (the snapshot
+    /// zeroes despawned slots, so render-death needs the linger /
+    /// last-transform contract — an ADR-029 P2b non-goal). The adapter
+    /// holds-pose / idles on Death until that contract lands.
+    Death = 4,
+    /// RESERVED — published only once an attack component exists to drive it.
+    Attack = 5,
+}
+
+/// Per-slot record stride for [`AgentRenderStateBuffer`] (ADR-029 P2b PR3,
+/// Surface A). Bytes per slot:
+///   `[0]` flags  — bit 2 [`LIVE_BIT`] + bit 4 [`BALLISTIC_BIT`] (reused from
+///                  the horde-state bit protocol). Bits 0/1/7 are unused in
+///                  render-state records today; bits 3/5/6 are reserved for
+///                  coarse gore flags.
+///   `[1]` health — [`quantize_health_fraction`] (255 = full, 0 = dead).
+///   `[2]` hitstop— [`HitStop::remaining_frames`] (0 = none); the render
+///                  adapter freezes that instance's anim clock while > 0.
+///   `[3]` intent — [`AnimIntent`] discriminant → clip (Godot-side map).
+///
+/// Surface A of the P2b ABI: the wide CPU-authoritative render state,
+/// consumed render-side to assemble `CUSTOM_DATA` — NOT uploaded to the GPU
+/// as a lookup texture. It supersedes the production path's need to read
+/// `get_agent_horde_states` + `get_agent_health_buffer` separately.
+/// Extending the record (e.g. a gore-segment byte) is a coordinated
+/// Rust+GDScript stride bump guarded by the buffer's parity test.
+pub const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE: usize = 4;
+
+/// `[0]` flags byte offset within a [`RENDER_STATE_STRIDE`] record.
+pub const RS_FLAGS: usize = 0;
+/// `[1]` health-fraction byte offset.
+pub const RS_HEALTH: usize = 1;
+/// `[2]` hit-stop-remaining-frames byte offset.
+pub const RS_HITSTOP: usize = 2;
+/// `[3]` [`AnimIntent`] discriminant byte offset.
+pub const RS_INTENT: usize = 3;
+
+/// Per-agent render-state FFI buffer (ADR-029 P2b PR3 — Surface A).
+///
+/// Mirrors [`AgentHordeStateBuffer`] / [`AgentHealthBuffer`] but carries a
+/// [`RENDER_STATE_STRIDE`]-byte record per slot (flags / health / hitstop /
+/// intent), indexed by [`BufferSlot`] so slot `i`'s record lives at
+/// `buf[i * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE ..]` and lines up with the agent whose
+/// transform is at `snapshot[i * SNAPSHOT_STRIDE]`. Written each tick by
+/// [`agent_render_state_publish_system`]; read by
+/// `ZomboidSim::get_agent_render_state_buffer` via `try_read` so a contended
+/// or poisoned lock never panics the Godot main thread. Pure function of
+/// components → determinism-clean (ADR-013).
+///
+/// Length parity with the snapshot slot count (× the stride) is locked by
+/// `agent_render_state_buffer_length_matches_snapshot_slot_count`.
+#[derive(Resource, Clone, Default)]
+pub struct AgentRenderStateBuffer(pub Arc<RwLock<Vec<u8>>>);
+
+impl AgentRenderStateBuffer {
+    pub fn new() -> Self {
+        Self::default()
+    }
+
+    pub fn handle(&self) -> Arc<RwLock<Vec<u8>>> {
+        Arc::clone(&self.0)
+    }
+}
+
 /// Quantize a `Health` component to the 0–255 fraction byte the FFI ships.
 ///
 /// `round((current / max) * 255)` clamped to `[0, 255]`. Pure function —
@@ -741,6 +836,130 @@ pub fn agent_horde_state_publish_system(
     }
 }
 
+/// Derive the semantic [`AnimIntent`] for one agent. Pure function of the
+/// agent's components — no RNG, no wall-clock, no cross-agent state — so the
+/// published byte is determinism-clean (ADR-013) and each branch of the
+/// priority ladder is trivially unit-testable.
+///
+/// Priority (first match wins): **Death > Stagger > Ballistic > Idle /
+/// Locomotion**. HitStop is deliberately NOT an intent — it freezes the
+/// *current* clip and ships in its own record byte, so a hit-stopped walking
+/// agent keeps `Locomotion` intent (the adapter just stops advancing its
+/// clock). The idle cutoff reuses the diagnostic stuck velocity-magnitude
+/// threshold ([`STUCK_VEL_MAG_SQ_THRESHOLD`]) as the PR3 starting heuristic;
+/// it can graduate to its own tunable if the idle band wants to differ from
+/// the stuck-diagnostic band.
+#[inline]
+pub fn derive_anim_intent(
+    health: Option<&Health>,
+    staggered: Option<&Staggered>,
+    ballistic: Option<&BallisticMode>,
+    dead: bool,
+    velocity: &Velocity,
+) -> AnimIntent {
+    if dead || health.is_some_and(|h| h.is_dead()) {
+        AnimIntent::Death
+    } else if staggered.is_some() {
+        AnimIntent::Stagger
+    } else if ballistic.is_some() {
+        AnimIntent::Ballistic
+    } else {
+        let mag_sq = velocity.dx * velocity.dx + velocity.dy * velocity.dy;
+        if mag_sq.is_finite() && mag_sq < STUCK_VEL_MAG_SQ_THRESHOLD {
+            AnimIntent::Idle
+        } else {
+            AnimIntent::Locomotion
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+/// Publish the per-agent render-state record buffer parallel to the snapshot
+/// (ADR-029 P2b PR3 — Surface A). Sibling of [`agent_health_publish_system`]
+/// / [`agent_horde_state_publish_system`]: pure component → bytes, indexed
+/// by [`BufferSlot`], FFI-only (no in-sim consumer). Sizing semantics mirror
+/// the siblings — re-use the allocation when the slot count is stable;
+/// resize + unconditional `fill(0)` so a despawned higher slot can't leave
+/// stale bytes in a reused lower record.
+///
+/// Schedule position (`systems.rs`): `.after(snapshot_write_system)
+/// .before(hit_stop_decay_system)` — the SAME pre-decay window the snapshot
+/// occupies, so the published `HitStop` / `Staggered` reflect the active
+/// frame Godot consumes (the decay systems run the tick after), and the
+/// read/write order against every component writer is fully pinned.
+///
+/// Determinism (ADR-013): each slot is written independently from a pure
+/// function of that agent's components, so archetype iteration order cannot
+/// perturb the output.
+// Clippy's `type_complexity` flags the 7-element Query tuple. Same rationale
+// as `vehicle_input_arbitration_system` / `damage_resolution_system`: a
+// dedicated `QueryData` derive isn't worth it at this slice's scope; the
+// annotation is targeted.
+#[allow(clippy::type_complexity)]
+pub fn agent_render_state_publish_system(
+    buffer: Res<AgentRenderStateBuffer>,
+    q: Query<(
+        &BufferSlot,
+        &Velocity,
+        Option<&Health>,
+        Option<&HitStop>,
+        Option<&Staggered>,
+        Option<&BallisticMode>,
+        Option<&DeadTag>,
+    )>,
+) {
+    // max_slot before the write guard so the O(N) scan stays out of the lock
+    // window (GCA R1 finding on `snapshot_write_system`, PR #87).
+    let max_slot = q.iter().map(|(slot, ..)| slot.0).max();
+    let Ok(mut buf) = buffer.0.write() else {
+        return;
+    };
+    let Some(max_slot) = max_slot else {
+        buf.clear();
+        return;
+    };
+    let required_len = (max_slot as usize + 1) * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE;
+    if buf.len() != required_len {
+        buf.resize(required_len, 0);
+    }
+    buf.fill(0);
+    for (slot, velocity, health, hitstop, staggered, ballistic, dead) in q.iter() {
+        let base = slot.0 as usize * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE;
+        let intent = derive_anim_intent(health, staggered, ballistic, dead.is_some(), velocity);
+        // LIVE_BIT mirrors the horde-state bit protocol: it must be CLEAR for a
+        // dead agent (`DeadTag` or `Health::is_dead` — exactly the predicate
+        // `derive_anim_intent` collapses into `AnimIntent::Death`), so the flags
+        // byte never contradicts the intent byte it ships alongside. A dead
+        // ballistic corpse is therefore `BALLISTIC_BIT` alone, not
+        // `LIVE_BIT | BALLISTIC_BIT`. No current consumer masks on LIVE_BIT, but
+        // the ABI is documented to be extended with gore-segment flags — a
+        // future `flags & LIVE_BIT == 0` skip must not read a dying corpse as
+        // live (Greptile P1 / GCA, #597).
+        let mut flags = if intent == AnimIntent::Death {
+            0
+        } else {
+            LIVE_BIT
+        };
+        if ballistic.is_some() {
+            flags |= BALLISTIC_BIT;
+        }
+        // Death always publishes health 0 so the record's fields never
+        // contradict each other — a `DeadTag` agent with no `Health` would
+        // otherwise ship intent=Death + flags=0 + health=255 (CR #597). Live
+        // agents missing `Health` → full (255), matching
+        // `get_agent_health_tint_buffer`'s "absent health = full/green" fallback.
+        let health_byte = if intent == AnimIntent::Death {
+            0
+        } else {
+            health.map_or(255, quantize_health_fraction)
+        };
+        let hitstop_frames = hitstop.map_or(0, |h| h.remaining_frames);
+        buf[base + RS_FLAGS] = flags;
+        buf[base + RS_HEALTH] = health_byte;
+        buf[base + RS_HITSTOP] = hitstop_frames;
+        buf[base + RS_INTENT] = intent as u8;
+    }
+}
+
 /// Clamp-and-floor a world-space position to its grid cell. Returns
 /// `None` for positions outside the grid (negative, non-finite, or
 /// beyond `grid_w * cell_size` / `grid_h * cell_size`).

         assert_eq!(buf[2], 255, "live slot 2 carries its health byte");
     }
 
+    fn run_render_state_publish(world: &mut World) {
+        let mut sys = IntoSystem::into_system(agent_render_state_publish_system);
+        sys.initialize(world);
+        let _ = sys.run((), world);
+        sys.apply_deferred(world);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn derive_anim_intent_priority_ladder() {
+        let moving = Velocity {
+            dx: 5.0,
+            dy: 0.0,
+            dz: 0.0,
+        };
+        let still = Velocity {
+            dx: 0.0,
+            dy: 0.0,
+            dz: 0.0,
+        };
+        let alive = Health {
+            current: 100.0,
+            max: 100.0,
+        };
+        let dead = Health {
+            current: 0.0,
+            max: 100.0,
+        };
+        let stag = Staggered {
+            remaining_duration: 0.2,
+        };
+        let ball = BallisticMode {
+            velocity: [1.0, 0.0, 2.0],
+            spawned_at_tick: 0,
+        };
+
+        // Death dominates everything (even with stagger + ballistic present).
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&alive), Some(&stag), Some(&ball), true, &moving),
+            AnimIntent::Death
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&dead), None, None, false, &moving),
+            AnimIntent::Death,
+            "is_dead health → Death even without DeadTag"
+        );
+        // Stagger over ballistic + locomotion.
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&alive), Some(&stag), Some(&ball), false, &moving),
+            AnimIntent::Stagger
+        );
+        // Ballistic over locomotion.
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&alive), None, Some(&ball), false, &moving),
+            AnimIntent::Ballistic
+        );
+        // Moving live agent → Locomotion; still agent → Idle.
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&alive), None, None, false, &moving),
+            AnimIntent::Locomotion
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&alive), None, None, false, &still),
+            AnimIntent::Idle
+        );
+        // Missing Health still classifies on motion (the publish system
+        // supplies the default health byte, not the derive).
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(None, None, None, false, &still),
+            AnimIntent::Idle
+        );
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn render_state_publish_writes_record_per_slot() {
+        let mut world = World::new();
+        let buffer = AgentRenderStateBuffer::default();
+        let handle = buffer.handle();
+        world.insert_resource(buffer);
+        // slot 0: moving, full health, no hitstop → Locomotion
+        world.spawn((
+            BufferSlot(0),
+            Velocity {
+                dx: 5.0,
+                dy: 0.0,
+                dz: 0.0,
+            },
+            Health {
+                current: 100.0,
+                max: 100.0,
+            },
+        ));
+        // slot 1: hit-stopped, half health, still → Idle + hitstop byte
+        world.spawn((
+            BufferSlot(1),
+            Velocity {
+                dx: 0.0,
+                dy: 0.0,
+                dz: 0.0,
+            },
+            Health {
+                current: 50.0,
+                max: 100.0,
+            },
+            HitStop {
+                remaining_frames: 6,
+            },
+        ));
+        // slot 2: ballistic → Ballistic intent + BALLISTIC_BIT flag
+        world.spawn((
+            BufferSlot(2),
+            Velocity {
+                dx: 1.0,
+                dy: 0.0,
+                dz: 0.0,
+            },
+            Health {
+                current: 80.0,
+                max: 100.0,
+            },
+            BallisticMode {
+                velocity: [1.0, 0.0, 3.0],
+                spawned_at_tick: 0,
+            },
+        ));
+        run_render_state_publish(&mut world);
+        let buf = handle.read().unwrap();
+        assert_eq!(buf.len(), 3 * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE);
+
+        assert_eq!(buf[RS_FLAGS], LIVE_BIT);
+        assert_eq!(buf[RS_HEALTH], 255);
+        assert_eq!(buf[RS_HITSTOP], 0);
+        assert_eq!(buf[RS_INTENT], AnimIntent::Locomotion as u8);
+
+        let b1 = RENDER_STATE_STRIDE;
+        assert_eq!(buf[b1 + RS_FLAGS], LIVE_BIT);
+        assert_eq!(buf[b1 + RS_HEALTH], 128, "half health → 128");
+        assert_eq!(buf[b1 + RS_HITSTOP], 6);
+        assert_eq!(buf[b1 + RS_INTENT], AnimIntent::Idle as u8);
+
+        let b2 = 2 * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE;
+        assert_eq!(buf[b2 + RS_FLAGS], LIVE_BIT | BALLISTIC_BIT);
+        assert_eq!(buf[b2 + RS_HEALTH], 204, "round(0.8 * 255) = 204");
+        assert_eq!(buf[b2 + RS_HITSTOP], 0);
+        assert_eq!(buf[b2 + RS_INTENT], AnimIntent::Ballistic as u8);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn render_state_publish_clears_live_bit_for_dead_agents() {
+        // The flags byte reuses the horde-state bit protocol where LIVE_BIT is
+        // CLEAR for a dead agent. The publish query iterates dead agents too —
+        // a `DeadTag` corpse rides the ballistic arc until it lands, and a
+        // lethally-damaged agent is `is_dead` the tick before despawn — so the
+        // byte must agree with the `AnimIntent::Death` it ships, or a future
+        // `flags & LIVE_BIT` consumer reads a corpse as live (Greptile P1 /
+        // GCA, #597). Note slot 2 of `render_state_publish_writes_record_per_slot`
+        // is a *live* ballistic agent (health 80, no DeadTag) and KEEPS LIVE_BIT;
+        // this test pins the dead side of that boundary.
+        let mut world = World::new();
+        let buffer = AgentRenderStateBuffer::default();
+        let handle = buffer.handle();
+        world.insert_resource(buffer);
+        // slot 0: DeadTag corpse mid-ballistic-arc → BALLISTIC_BIT only, Death.
+        world.spawn((
+            BufferSlot(0),
+            Velocity {
+                dx: 2.0,
+                dy: 0.0,
+                dz: 0.0,
+            },
+            Health {
+                current: 0.0,
+                max: 100.0,
+            },
+            BallisticMode {
+                velocity: [2.0, 0.0, 4.0],
+                spawned_at_tick: 0,
+            },
+            DeadTag,
+        ));
+        // slot 1: dead via zero health, NO DeadTag and NOT ballistic → flags 0.
+        world.spawn((
+            BufferSlot(1),
+            Velocity {
+                dx: 0.0,
+                dy: 0.0,
+                dz: 0.0,
+            },
+            Health {
+                current: 0.0,
+                max: 100.0,
+            },
+        ));
+        // slot 2: DeadTag with NO Health component — intent=Death, flags=0, and
+        // health must publish 0 (not the absent-Health 255 default), or the
+        // record contradicts itself (CR #597).
+        world.spawn((
+            BufferSlot(2),
+            Velocity {
+                dx: 0.0,
+                dy: 0.0,
+                dz: 0.0,
+            },
+            DeadTag,
+        ));
+        run_render_state_publish(&mut world);
+        let buf = handle.read().unwrap();
+
+        // Dead ballistic corpse: LIVE_BIT clear, BALLISTIC_BIT kept.
+        assert_eq!(
+            buf[RS_FLAGS], BALLISTIC_BIT,
+            "dead ballistic corpse must clear LIVE_BIT and keep BALLISTIC_BIT"
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            buf[RS_FLAGS] & LIVE_BIT,
+            0,
+            "LIVE_BIT must be clear for a dead agent"
+        );
+        assert_eq!(buf[RS_INTENT], AnimIntent::Death as u8);
+
+        // is_dead-via-health agent (no DeadTag, not ballistic): flags fully 0.
+        let b1 = RENDER_STATE_STRIDE;
+        assert_eq!(
+            buf[b1 + RS_FLAGS],
+            0,
+            "is_dead agent (no DeadTag) clears LIVE_BIT → flags 0"
+        );
+        assert_eq!(buf[b1 + RS_HEALTH], 0, "zero health → 0");
+        assert_eq!(buf[b1 + RS_INTENT], AnimIntent::Death as u8);
+
+        // DeadTag with no Health: health byte forced to 0, not the 255 default.
+        let b2 = 2 * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE;
+        assert_eq!(buf[b2 + RS_FLAGS], 0, "DeadTag (no Health) clears LIVE_BIT");
+        assert_eq!(
+            buf[b2 + RS_HEALTH],
+            0,
+            "Death forces health 0 even with no Health component"
+        );
+        assert_eq!(buf[b2 + RS_INTENT], AnimIntent::Death as u8);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn render_state_publish_zeros_despawned_prefix_record() {
+        // Non-recycling slots: a live slot 2 with absent 0/1 leaves their
+        // whole records zeroed by the unconditional `fill(0)`.
+        let mut world = World::new();
+        let buffer = AgentRenderStateBuffer::default();
+        let handle = buffer.handle();
+        world.insert_resource(buffer);
+        world.spawn((
+            BufferSlot(2),
+            Velocity {
+                dx: 5.0,
+                dy: 0.0,
+                dz: 0.0,
+            },
+            Health {
+                current: 100.0,
+                max: 100.0,
+            },
+        ));
+        run_render_state_publish(&mut world);
+        let buf = handle.read().unwrap();
+        assert_eq!(
+            buf.len(),
+            3 * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE,
+            "sized to max_slot (2) + 1"
+        );
+        assert!(
+            buf[0..2 * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE].iter().all(|&b| b == 0),
+            "absent slots 0 and 1 stay fully zeroed"
+        );
+        assert_eq!(buf[2 * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE + RS_FLAGS], LIVE_BIT);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn render_state_publish_empty_world_clears_buffer() {
+        let mut world = World::new();
+        let buffer = AgentRenderStateBuffer::default();
+        let handle = buffer.handle();
+        world.insert_resource(buffer);
+        handle.write().unwrap().extend_from_slice(&[1, 2, 3, 4]);
+        run_render_state_publish(&mut world);
+        assert!(handle.read().unwrap().is_empty());
+    }

+    /// Length parity with the snapshot slot count (× stride) — sibling of
+    /// `agent_health_buffer_length_matches_snapshot_slot_count`. FFI consumers
+    /// index the snapshot and render-state buffers by the same `BufferSlot`,
+    /// so the record count must equal the snapshot slot count.
+    #[test]
+    fn agent_render_state_buffer_length_matches_snapshot_slot_count() {
+        use crate::systems::SNAPSHOT_STRIDE;
+        use bevy_tasks::{ComputeTaskPool, TaskPool};
+
+        ComputeTaskPool::get_or_init(TaskPool::default);
+        let snapshot = SnapshotBuffer::new();
+        let snapshot_handle = snapshot.handle();
+        let render_buffer = AgentRenderStateBuffer::default();
+        let render_handle = render_buffer.handle();
+        let mut world = crate::systems::build_world(snapshot).unwrap();
+        world.insert_resource(AgentRenderStateBuffer(render_handle.clone()));
+        let mut schedule = build_sim_schedule();
+        schedule.run(&mut world);
+        let snapshot_floats = snapshot_handle.read().unwrap().len();
+        let render_bytes = render_handle.read().unwrap().len();
+        assert_eq!(snapshot_floats % SNAPSHOT_STRIDE, 0);
+        assert_eq!(
+            render_bytes,
+            (snapshot_floats / SNAPSHOT_STRIDE) * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE,
+            "render-state buffer length ({}) must equal snapshot slot count ({}) × stride ({})",
+            render_bytes,
+            snapshot_floats / SNAPSHOT_STRIDE,
+            RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+        );
+    }

diff --git a/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/anim_intent_parity.rs b/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/anim_intent_parity.rs
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..ceffb39
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/anim_intent_parity.rs
@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@
+//! Cross-language parity for the `AnimIntent` enum (ADR-029 P2b PR3).
+//!
+//! `AnimIntent` is canonical in `density_feedback.rs` as a `#[repr(u8)]` enum.
+//! The Godot production render adapter `HordeRenderAdapter.gd` mirrors the
+//! discriminants as `const ANIM_* := N`. This test reads the GDScript via
+//! `include_str!` and asserts the mirror matches the Rust discriminants,
+//! catching silent desync if a future change moves only one side — the same
+//! discipline `bit_protocol_parity.rs` applies to the horde-state bit protocol.
+//!
+//! Adding/moving an intent:
+//!   1. Edit the `AnimIntent` enum in `density_feedback.rs`.
+//!   2. Mirror the `const ANIM_* := N` in `HordeRenderAdapter.gd`.
+//!   3. Extend the mirror table below.
+
+use zomboid_sim::density_feedback::AnimIntent;
+
+const HORDE_RENDER_ADAPTER_GD: &str =
+    include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd");
+
+/// Scan a GDScript file for `const NAME := <decimal>` (or `const NAME: int =
+/// <decimal>`) and return the parsed `u8`. Returns `None` if absent. Matches
+/// at word boundaries so `const ANIM_IDLE` is not matched by a query for a
+/// shorter prefix.
+fn extract_const_int(content: &str, name: &str) -> Option<u8> {
+    let prefix = format!("const {name}");
+    for line in content.lines() {
+        let trimmed = line.trim_start();
+        if !trimmed.starts_with(&prefix) {
+            continue;
+        }
+        // Word-boundary: the char after the name must not extend an identifier
+        // (so `ANIM_DEATH` is not matched by a query for `ANIM_DEAT`, and a
+        // query for `ANIM_IDLE` does not match `const ANIM_IDLE_FOO`).
+        let after_name = &trimmed[prefix.len()..];
+        match after_name.chars().next() {
+            Some(c) if c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_' => continue,
+            _ => {}
+        }
+        // Works for both `:=` (GDScript inference) and `: int =` forms — the
+        // value token is the first whitespace-delimited run after the `=`.
+        let Some(eq_pos) = trimmed.find('=') else {
+            continue;
+        };
+        let after_eq = trimmed[eq_pos + 1..].trim_start();
+        let Some(token) = after_eq.split_whitespace().next() else {
+            continue;
+        };
+        let token = token.trim_end_matches(',');
+        if let Ok(val) = token.parse::<u8>() {
+            return Some(val);
+        }
+    }
+    None
+}
+
+struct Mirror {
+    local_name: &'static str,
+    canonical: u8,
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn horde_render_adapter_gd_anim_intent_mirror_matches_rust() {
+    let mirrors = [
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_IDLE",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Idle as u8,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_LOCOMOTION",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Locomotion as u8,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_STAGGER",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Stagger as u8,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_BALLISTIC",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Ballistic as u8,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_DEATH",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Death as u8,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_ATTACK",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Attack as u8,
+        },
+    ];
+    let mut failures = Vec::new();
+    for m in &mirrors {
+        match extract_const_int(HORDE_RENDER_ADAPTER_GD, m.local_name) {
+            Some(v) if v == m.canonical => {}
+            Some(v) => failures.push(format!(
+                "HordeRenderAdapter.gd: `const {}` = {}, expected {} (Rust AnimIntent)",
+                m.local_name, v, m.canonical
+            )),
+            None => failures.push(format!(
+                "HordeRenderAdapter.gd: `const {}` not found (expected {})",
+                m.local_name, m.canonical
+            )),
+        }
+    }
+    assert!(
+        failures.is_empty(),
+        "AnimIntent cross-language parity failures:\n  - {}",
+        failures.join("\n  - ")
+    );
+}

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/SkeletalHordeAdapter.gd b/apps/game-godot/SkeletalHordeAdapter.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..09ec199
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/SkeletalHordeAdapter.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,421 @@
+class_name SkeletalHordeAdapter
+extends RefCounted
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR4 — skeletal render-LOD-0 adapter (DARK / bench-only this PR).
+#
+# A pool of up to `cap` skinned character-female-a rigs (Skeleton3D +
+# AnimationPlayer), driven from the SAME render-state ABI the VAT horde consumes:
+#   - get_agent_buffer()             stride-8 Transform2D (position + heading)
+#   - get_agent_render_state_buffer() stride-4 [flags, health, hitstop, AnimIntent]
+# The clip set is the rig's OWN AnimationPlayer library (the full Kenney set),
+# resolved AnimIntent -> clip name by preference at setup. NEVER queries the sim
+# FFI itself — the bench (PR4) / sim_driver (PR5) feeds it both buffers.
+#
+# SCOPE (operator ruling 2026-06-22, dark/bench-only): this PR builds + measures
+# the mechanism. WHICH live agents bind here, and the VAT<->skeletal dedup, are
+# PR5's LODManager3D. In-game stays always-VAT until PR5; the forced-N bench is
+# the only consumer this PR. PR4's job: "can we instantiate, bind, animate, tint,
+# freeze, and MEASURE LOD-0 rigs safely?" -> pick the cap from the bench.
+#
+# Cohort impl-preflight folds (lc-codex / lc-agy / lc-gemini, 2026-06-22):
+#  - Shared per-slot clock via SlotClockBook (phase-matched swaps free in PR5).
+#  - Tint via instance uniform on EVERY skinned MeshInstance3D under the rig
+#    (the mini-rig is body + head — single-mesh tint leaves part untinted),
+#    through ONE shared ShaderMaterial (no per-rig material clone).
+#  - play() ONLY on bind / intent change (re-play every frame restarts the clip +
+#    breaks phase); speed_scale=0 freezes HitStop; seek from the shared clock on
+#    (re)bind so a PR5 VAT->skeletal promotion does not pop to frame 0.
+#  - Unbound pool rigs: visible=false + process_mode=DISABLED (an idle
+#    AnimationPlayer in the tree still burns skinning cycles).
+#  - Missing/short render-state record -> skip the slot (no ghost rig; do NOT
+#    inherit the VAT adapter's live-full-health default for an expensive rig).
+#  - Headless: instantiate ZERO rigs / Skeleton3D / AnimationPlayer; the pool +
+#    cap bookkeeping still run (determinism fixtures stay asset-free, crash-free).
+
+const RIG_GLB_PATH := "res://assets/mini-characters/character-female-a.glb"
+const COLORMAP_PATH := "res://assets/mini-characters/Textures/colormap.png"
+const SHADER_PATH := "res://shaders/skeletal_horde.gdshader"
+const AGENT_STRIDE := 8
+
+# render-state record layout — MIRROR of density_feedback.rs RS_* offsets
+# (same contract HordeRenderAdapter consumes).
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const RS_FLAGS := 0
+const RS_HEALTH := 1
+const RS_HITSTOP := 2
+const RS_INTENT := 3
+
+# AnimIntent — MIRROR of density_feedback.rs (locked by tests/anim_intent_parity.rs
+# against HordeRenderAdapter's identical consts; the wire contract).
+const ANIM_IDLE := 0
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const ANIM_STAGGER := 2
+const ANIM_BALLISTIC := 3
+const ANIM_DEATH := 4
+const ANIM_ATTACK := 5
+const ANIM_INTENT_COUNT := 6
+
+var _render_root: Node3D
+var _headless := false
+var _cap := 0
+var _clocks: SlotClockBook
+var _ready := false
+var _enabled := false
+var _colormap_enabled := true
+var _health_tint_enabled := false
+
+var _mat: ShaderMaterial
+
+# pool (parallel arrays, indexed by pool slot 0.._cap-1)
+var _holders: Array[Node3D] = []  # carry the per-agent sim transform
+var _players: Array[AnimationPlayer] = []
+var _meshes: Array = []  # Array[Array[MeshInstance3D]] — body + head per rig
+var _pool_slot := PackedInt32Array()  # bound BufferSlot, -1 = free
+var _pool_intent := PackedInt32Array()  # cached AnimIntent, -1 = none (force play)
+
+# AnimIntent -> clip NAME resolved from the rig's own AnimationPlayer library.
+var _intent_clip: Array[StringName] = []
+
+# tunables (parity with the VAT horde so a PR5 swap does not jump scale/facing/feet)
+var character_scale := 1.0
+var y_offset := 0.0
+var facing_offset := 0.0
+
+# perf / bench instrumentation
+var last_assembly_usec := 0
+var last_live := 0
+
+
+func _init(render_root: Node3D, headless: bool, cap: int, clocks: SlotClockBook = null) -> void:
+	_render_root = render_root
+	_headless = headless
+	_cap = maxi(cap, 0)
+	# Shared clock: PR5 injects ONE book into both this adapter and the VAT
+	# adapter so swaps phase-match. PR4 defaults to its own (no shared live slots
+	# yet — dark path).
+	_clocks = clocks if clocks != null else SlotClockBook.new()
+
+
+# This is RefCounted but parents up to `cap` holder Node3Ds into the SceneTree
+# (_render_root). Godot nodes are manually managed, so they are NOT freed when
+# this RefCounted's last reference drops — a replaced/reconstructed adapter would
+# orphan its whole pool in the tree. Free the pool on predelete (GCA #600; the
+# .gemini/styleguide.md "nodes are not GC'd — always queue_free()" rule).
+func _notification(what: int) -> void:
+	if what == NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE:
+		for holder in _holders:
+			if holder != null and is_instance_valid(holder):
+				holder.queue_free()
+
+
+# Build the shared material + the rig pool + the intent->clip table. Returns false
+# (no-op) when headless or on a load failure. Idempotent.
+func setup() -> bool:
+	if _ready:
+		return true
+	if _headless:
+		# Headless: no rigs, but size the bookkeeping arrays so update()'s cap math
+		# + slot accounting still run crash-free (no Skeleton3D/AnimationPlayer).
+		_init_pool_state()
+		_ready = true
+		return false
+
+	var shader := load(SHADER_PATH) as Shader
+	if shader == null:
+		push_error("SkeletalHordeAdapter: failed to load shader")
+		return false
+	_mat = ShaderMaterial.new()
+	_mat.shader = shader
+	var cm := load(COLORMAP_PATH) as Texture2D
+	if cm != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("colormap", cm)
+	else:
+		push_warning("SkeletalHordeAdapter: colormap missing at %s — flat tint" % COLORMAP_PATH)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", _colormap_enabled and cm != null)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_health_tint", _health_tint_enabled)
+
+	# Hoist the rig PackedScene load out of the per-rig loop (CR/Greptile #600):
+	# load() hits the resource cache after the first read, but the cast + dispatch
+	# still run N times. Load once, pass it into every _spawn_rig.
+	var rig_scene := load(RIG_GLB_PATH) as PackedScene
+	if rig_scene == null:
+		push_error("SkeletalHordeAdapter: failed to load rig scene at %s" % RIG_GLB_PATH)
+		return false
+	_init_pool_state()
+	for i in _cap:
+		if not _spawn_rig(i, rig_scene):
+			push_error("SkeletalHordeAdapter: failed to instantiate rig %d" % i)
+			return false
+	_build_intent_clip_table()
+	_ready = true
+	return true
+
+
+func _init_pool_state() -> void:
+	_pool_slot.resize(_cap)
+	_pool_slot.fill(-1)
+	_pool_intent.resize(_cap)
+	_pool_intent.fill(-1)
+	_holders.resize(_cap)
+	_players.resize(_cap)
+	_meshes.resize(_cap)
+
+
+# Instantiate one pooled rig: a holder Node3D (the sim-transform target) parenting
+# the rig .glb instance; cache its AnimationPlayer + every skinned MeshInstance3D;
+# assign the shared tint material to each mesh; start unbound (hidden + disabled).
+func _spawn_rig(i: int, packed: PackedScene) -> bool:
+	var rig := packed.instantiate() as Node3D
+	if rig == null:
+		return false
+	var holder := Node3D.new()
+	holder.name = "SkelRig%d" % i
+	if character_scale != 1.0:
+		rig.scale = Vector3(character_scale, character_scale, character_scale)
+	holder.add_child(rig)
+	_render_root.add_child(holder)
+
+	var player := _find_anim_player(rig)
+	var meshes: Array[MeshInstance3D] = []
+	_collect_meshes(rig, meshes)
+	# Tint every skinned surface (body + head) through the shared material — set on
+	# each mesh so no surface is left untinted (lc-codex). material_override
+	# replaces the GLB StandardMaterial, so the shader supplies the colormap.
+	for m in meshes:
+		m.material_override = _mat
+
+	_holders[i] = holder
+	_players[i] = player
+	_meshes[i] = meshes
+	holder.visible = false
+	holder.process_mode = Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED
+	return true
+
+
+# Resolve each AnimIntent to a clip NAME present in the rig's AnimationPlayer, by
+# preference (first match wins; else the first available clip; else ""). Mirrors
+# HordeRenderAdapter._build_intent_clip_table but resolves against the live
+# AnimationPlayer library (the full Kenney set), not the sparse VAT bake table.
+func _build_intent_clip_table() -> void:
+	_intent_clip.resize(ANIM_INTENT_COUNT)
+	for i in ANIM_INTENT_COUNT:
+		_intent_clip[i] = &""
+	var player: AnimationPlayer = _players[0] if _players.size() > 0 else null
+	if player == null:
+		push_warning("SkeletalHordeAdapter: rig has no AnimationPlayer — clips unavailable")
+		return
+	var names := player.get_animation_list()
+	if names.is_empty():
+		push_warning("SkeletalHordeAdapter: AnimationPlayer has no clips")
+		return
+	var fallback := names[0]
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_IDLE] = _first_clip(names, ["idle", "walk"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_LOCOMOTION] = _first_clip(
+		names, ["walk", "run", "sprint", "locomotion"], fallback
+	)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_STAGGER] = _first_clip(names, ["hit", "stagger", "walk"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_BALLISTIC] = _first_clip(names, ["fall", "flail", "walk"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_DEATH] = _first_clip(names, ["die", "death", "walk"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_ATTACK] = _first_clip(
+		names, ["attack", "attack-melee-right", "punch", "walk"], fallback
+	)
+
+
+# A clip name may be bare ("walk") or library-qualified ("Library/walk"); match
+# on the unqualified suffix so either import naming resolves.
+func _first_clip(names: PackedStringArray, prefs: Array, fallback: StringName) -> StringName:
+	for pref in prefs:
+		for nm in names:
+			var bare := nm.get_slice("/", nm.get_slice_count("/") - 1)
+			if bare == pref:
+				return nm
+	return fallback
+
+
+func set_active(enabled: bool) -> void:
+	_enabled = enabled
+	# Dark path: visibility of bound rigs is managed per-frame in update(); when
+	# disabled, unbind the whole pool so no rig lingers.
+	if not enabled and _ready and not _headless:
+		for i in _cap:
+			_unbind(i)
+
+
+func is_active() -> bool:
+	return _enabled and _ready and not _headless
+
+
+func set_colormap_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_colormap_enabled = on
+	if _mat != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", on)
+
+
+func set_health_tint_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_health_tint_enabled = on
+	if _mat != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_health_tint", on)
+
+
+# Per-frame: bind the first up-to-`cap` live agents to pool rigs (PR4's trivial
+# in-order selection — PR5's LODManager3D replaces it with a priority policy +
+# VAT dedup), drive each rig's transform / clip / freeze / tint, unbind the rest.
+func update(
+	agent_buf: PackedFloat32Array, render_state: PackedByteArray, count: int, delta: float
+) -> void:
+	if not _ready:
+		return
+	var t0 := Time.get_ticks_usec()
+	_clocks.ensure_capacity(count)
+	var max_agents := agent_buf.size() / AGENT_STRIDE
+	if count > max_agents:
+		count = max_agents
+	var rs_len := render_state.size()
+
+	var pool_i := 0
+	for s in count:
+		var bo := s * AGENT_STRIDE
+		var bxx := agent_buf[bo + 0]
+		var byx := agent_buf[bo + 1]
+		var ox := agent_buf[bo + 3]
+		var bxy := agent_buf[bo + 4]
+		var byy := agent_buf[bo + 5]
+		var oy := agent_buf[bo + 7]
+		# dead slots are zeroed by the snapshot writer -> all-zero basis
+		if absf(bxx) < 1e-9 and absf(bxy) < 1e-9 and absf(byx) < 1e-9 and absf(byy) < 1e-9:
+			_clocks.mark_dead(s)
+			continue
+		# Missing/short render-state record -> SKIP this slot (no ghost rig). An
+		# expensive skeletal rig must not render on a defaulted record, unlike the
+		# VAT adapter's cheap live-full-health fallback (lc-codex). Plain continue,
+		# NOT mark_dead: the slot is LIVE (it passed the zeroed-transform check),
+		# just record-less, so under a shared book (PR5) mark_dead would reset the
+		# clock that the VAT tier — which renders this slot via its fallback — is
+		# advancing, freezing/restarting its animation (GCA #600).
+		var ro := s * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		if ro + RENDER_STATE_STRIDE > rs_len:
+			continue
+		if pool_i >= _cap:
+			# Over the LOD-0 cap — the VAT tier renders these slots and owns their
+			# clock. Plain continue, NOT advance: under a shared book (PR5) the VAT
+			# tier already advances them, so advancing here too would double-speed
+			# their animation; on a later promotion the skeletal tier seeks from that
+			# shared clock, which VAT has kept current (GCA #600).
+			continue
+
+		var health := render_state[ro + RS_HEALTH]
+		var hitstop := render_state[ro + RS_HITSTOP]
+		var intent := render_state[ro + RS_INTENT]
+		var clk := _clocks.advance(s, delta, hitstop)
+		var heading := atan2(bxy, bxx)
+		_bind(pool_i, s, ox, oy, heading, intent, health, hitstop, clk)
+		pool_i += 1
+
+	# Unbind the unused tail of the pool.
+	for i in range(pool_i, _cap):
+		_unbind(i)
+	last_live = pool_i
+	last_assembly_usec = Time.get_ticks_usec() - t0
+
+
+func _bind(
+	pool_i: int,
+	slot: int,
+	ox: float,
+	oy: float,
+	heading: float,
+	intent: int,
+	health: int,
+	hitstop: int,
+	clk: float
+) -> void:
+	if _headless:
+		# bookkeeping only (no nodes headless)
+		_pool_slot[pool_i] = slot
+		_pool_intent[pool_i] = intent
+		return
+	var holder := _holders[pool_i]
+	if holder == null:
+		return
+	# sim (X,Y) -> Godot (X,Z), Y=0 ground (+ feet offset); yaw about +Y from the
+	# sim heading — IDENTICAL convention to HordeRenderAdapter so VAT/skeletal
+	# facing matches at a PR5 swap.
+	holder.global_position = Vector3(ox, y_offset, oy)
+	holder.rotation.y = -heading + facing_offset
+	if holder.process_mode == Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED:
+		holder.process_mode = Node.PROCESS_MODE_INHERIT
+		holder.visible = true
+
+	var player := _players[pool_i]
+	var clamped := clampi(intent, 0, ANIM_INTENT_COUNT - 1)
+	# play() ONLY on a (re)bind or intent change — never per frame (re-play
+	# restarts the clip). Seek to the shared clock so the clip phase-matches.
+	if player != null and (_pool_slot[pool_i] != slot or _pool_intent[pool_i] != clamped):
+		var clip := _intent_clip[clamped]
+		if clip != &"":
+			player.play(clip)
+			_seek_to_clock(player, clip, clk)
+	# Cache the intent OUTSIDE the player guard (CR/Greptile #600): a rig with no
+	# AnimationPlayer would otherwise never update the cache, keeping bind-state
+	# stale at -1 and re-entering the gate every frame. The clip table is static,
+	# so an unresolved (empty) clip can't appear later — no per-frame retry wanted.
+	_pool_intent[pool_i] = clamped
+	# HitStop = hold the pose (speed_scale 0); the shared clock is already frozen,
+	# so a later rebind seeks to the held phase.
+	if player != null:
+		player.speed_scale = 0.0 if hitstop > 0 else 1.0
+	# Per-instance health tint on EVERY skinned surface (body + head).
+	var h := float(health) / 255.0
+	for m in _meshes[pool_i]:
+		m.set_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac", h)
+	_pool_slot[pool_i] = slot
+
+
+func _unbind(pool_i: int) -> void:
+	if _pool_slot[pool_i] == -1:
+		return
+	_pool_slot[pool_i] = -1
+	_pool_intent[pool_i] = -1
+	if _headless:
+		return
+	var holder := _holders[pool_i]
+	if holder != null:
+		holder.visible = false
+		# DISABLED halts the AnimationPlayer tick + skinning for the idle rig
+		# (an in-tree AnimationPlayer still burns cycles otherwise — lc-agy).
+		holder.process_mode = Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED
+
+
+# Seek a (looping) clip to the shared slot clock so phase is continuous across a
+# (re)bind — non-looping clips clamp at the end (a finished death holds its pose).
+func _seek_to_clock(player: AnimationPlayer, clip: StringName, clk: float) -> void:
+	var anim := player.get_animation(clip)
+	if anim == null or anim.length <= 0.0:
+		return
+	var t := (
+		fmod(clk, anim.length) if anim.loop_mode != Animation.LOOP_NONE else minf(clk, anim.length)
+	)
+	player.seek(t, true)
+
+
+func _find_anim_player(n: Node) -> AnimationPlayer:
+	if n is AnimationPlayer:
+		return n
+	for c in n.get_children():
+		var r := _find_anim_player(c)
+		if r != null:
+			return r
+	return null
+
+
+func _collect_meshes(n: Node, out: Array[MeshInstance3D]) -> void:
+	if n is MeshInstance3D:
+		out.append(n)
+	for c in n.get_children():
+		_collect_meshes(c, out)
+
+
+func debug_status() -> String:
+	if not is_active():
+		return "Skel3D: off"
+	return "Skel3D: %d/%d rigs, asm %.2fms" % [last_live, _cap, last_assembly_usec / 1000.0]

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/SlotClockBook.gd b/apps/game-godot/SlotClockBook.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..b3e81cc
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/SlotClockBook.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+class_name SlotClockBook
+extends RefCounted
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR4 — the shared per-BufferSlot animation clock.
+#
+# Extracted from HordeRenderAdapter's private `_clock`/`_was_live` arrays so the
+# VAT (render-LOD-1) and skeletal (render-LOD-0) tiers read the SAME accumulated
+# time per slot. PR5's phase-matched VAT<->skeletal swap is then automatic: a
+# promoted/demoted agent keeps its gait phase because both adapters seek from
+# clock(slot) at the swap. This class IS the cohort impl-preflight fold #2
+# (lc-codex / lc-agy / lc-gemini, 2026-06-22 — all three: extract now, not PR5).
+#
+# Render-owned phase ONLY — never enters the Rust ABI. The clock is presentation
+# interpolation derived from slot lifetime + HitStop, not deterministic sim
+# state; pushing it into the FFI would couple render interpolation into the
+# deterministic sim surface for no reason (lc-codex guard).
+#
+# Slot lifecycle (the caller drives, once per frame per slot):
+#   ensure_capacity(count)                  # grow backing arrays to >= count
+#   mark_dead(slot)                         # zeroed-transform / absent record
+#   advance(slot, delta, hitstop_remaining) # rebirth-reset + HitStop-freeze + tick
+#   clock(slot) -> float                    # the seconds for CUSTOM_DATA.x / seek
+#
+# Semantics are byte-for-byte the PR3 HordeRenderAdapter behavior (so its
+# frozen-slot bench assertion is the regression guard): a slot that was dead last
+# frame resets to 0 AND advances on the same rebirth frame; HitStop (remaining >
+# 0) skips the advance so the clip holds its current frame.
+
+var _clock := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _was_live := PackedByteArray()
+
+
+func ensure_capacity(count: int) -> void:
+	if _clock.size() >= count:
+		return
+	_clock.resize(count)
+	_was_live.resize(count)
+
+
+# Advance a LIVE slot. On the rebirth frame (was_live edge 0->1) the clock resets
+# to 0 first, THEN advances this frame — matching PR3 exactly (a reborn slot does
+# not skip a frame). HitStop freezes: remaining > 0 -> the clock holds. Returns
+# the post-advance clock for the caller's CUSTOM_DATA.x / AnimationPlayer.seek.
+func advance(slot: int, delta: float, hitstop_remaining: int) -> float:
+	if slot < 0 or slot >= _clock.size():
+		return 0.0
+	if _was_live[slot] == 0:
+		_clock[slot] = 0.0
+		_was_live[slot] = 1
+	if hitstop_remaining == 0:
+		_clock[slot] += delta
+	return _clock[slot]
+
+
+# Mark a slot dead (zeroed transform or absent render-state). The clock is not
+# zeroed here — it resets on the next rebirth via advance()'s was_live edge, so a
+# recycled BufferSlot (PR3 respawn) starts a fresh gait.
+func mark_dead(slot: int) -> void:
+	if slot >= 0 and slot < _was_live.size():
+		_was_live[slot] = 0
+
+
+func clock(slot: int) -> float:
+	if slot < 0 or slot >= _clock.size():
+		return 0.0
+	return _clock[slot]
+
+
+func was_live(slot: int) -> bool:
+	return slot >= 0 and slot < _was_live.size() and _was_live[slot] == 1

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/shaders/skeletal_horde.gdshader b/apps/game-godot/shaders/skeletal_horde.gdshader
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..cda2f69
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/shaders/skeletal_horde.gdshader
@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+shader_type spatial;
+render_mode unshaded;
+
+// ADR-029 P2b PR4 — skeletal render-LOD-0 tint shader.
+//
+// The LOD-0 sibling of horde_render.gdshader (the VAT/LOD-1 shader). It does
+// NOT animate vertices — the skinned Skeleton3D + AnimationPlayer pose the mesh
+// (Godot's built-in skinning runs before this vertex(); we never touch the
+// vertex position, so skinning is transparent). This shader exists only to
+// reproduce the VAT tier's FRAGMENT look — Kenney colormap + the squared-curve
+// damage flash — so an agent does not visibly pop colour when PR5 swaps it
+// between the skeletal and VAT tiers.
+//
+// Health is a per-instance `instance uniform`, set via
+// set_instance_shader_parameter() on EVERY skinned MeshInstance3D under the rig
+// (the Kenney mini-rig is body + head — a single-mesh tint would leave part
+// untinted; lc-codex impl-preflight catch). `instance uniform` lets every pooled
+// rig share ONE ShaderMaterial with a distinct per-instance value — no material
+// clone (lc-agy / lc-gemini confirm).
+
+uniform vec3 base_color : source_color = vec3(0.30, 0.55, 0.30);
+uniform vec3 hurt_color : source_color = vec3(1.0, 0.05, 0.05);
+uniform bool use_colormap = true;  // ON by default — match PR3's "real Kenney colors"
+uniform sampler2D colormap : filter_nearest, source_color;  // Kenney palette (UV lookup)
+// OFF by default so the clean Kenney textures show for the artist preview; the
+// canonical sim_driver._apply_health_tint mutator flips it (parity with the VAT).
+uniform bool use_health_tint = false;
+
+// Per-instance health fraction [0,1] — set per pooled rig via
+// set_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac", ...). Default full health so an
+// unbound/uninitialised rig never renders pre-reddened.
+instance uniform float health_frac = 1.0;
+
+varying float v_health;
+
+void vertex() {
+	// No vertex displacement — the engine skins the mesh. Just carry health to
+	// the fragment stage (read the instance uniform once, vertex-rate).
+	v_health = clamp(health_frac, 0.0, 1.0);
+}
+
+void fragment() {
+	vec3 body = use_colormap ? texture(colormap, UV).rgb : base_color;
+	// Squared curve so damage reads EARLY — identical to horde_render.gdshader so
+	// the two tiers match: v_health*v_health maps 0.7 -> 0.49 (~half red).
+	if (use_health_tint) {
+		body = mix(hurt_color, body, v_health * v_health);
+	}
+	ALBEDO = body;
+}
