diff --git a/apps/game-godot/part_segmented_horde_bench.gd b/apps/game-godot/part_segmented_horde_bench.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..59a1f32
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/part_segmented_horde_bench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,483 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 gore epic mmnto-ai/liquid-city#565, PR1 (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#559) —
+# part-segmented render-LOD-0 horde bench (sibling of skeletal_horde_bench).
+# REAL GPU (headless instantiates zero rigs). PHASE A (gates exit): Godot
+# import-proof (6 parts resolve by NAME — the runtime contract, not glTF indices),
+# dismember hide/reset + pooled-recycle leak guard (lc-codex), bind count, HitStop
+# freeze, intent->clip, tint across all 6 parts, root planar-drift < eps. PHASE B
+# (reports): forced-N sweep {0,16,32,48,64} re-measures the cap (6 nodes/rig != the
+# skinned cost, lc-codex) + JSON + per-N PNG + a gameplay-camera perceptual baseline.
+# Run: <godot> --path apps/game-godot res://part_segmented_horde_bench.tscn
+#   0 = correctness clean, 1 = assertion failed, 2 = watchdog.
+
+const CAP := 64  # pool sized to the max swept N
+const SPACING := 2.0
+const OUT_DIR := "user://part_segmented_horde_bench/"
+
+# Phase A (correctness)
+const NC := 8  # synthetic agent count
+const FRAMES_C := 4  # fixed frames before asserting
+const DELTA_C := 0.1  # fixed delta so clock asserts are deterministic
+const DEAD_SLOT := 0
+const FROZEN_SLOT := 2  # hitstop > 0 -> clock holds, speed_scale == 0
+const CONTROL_SLOT := 3  # hitstop 0 -> clock advances, speed_scale == 1
+
+# Phase B (perf sweep)
+const N_SWEEP := [0, 16, 32, 48, 64]
+const WARMUP := 20
+const SAMPLE := 60
+const BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS := 2.0  # cap = highest N with p95 marginal <= this
+
+# render-state record layout (mirror of the ABI)
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const RS_HITSTOP := 2
+const LIVE_BIT := 0x04
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const ANIM_DEATH := 4
+# tier ids (ADR-029 P2b PR5) — bench plays LODManager3D: live agents SKEL, dead DEAD.
+const TIER_DEAD := 0
+const TIER_SKEL := 2
+const PART_COUNT := 6
+
+var _adapter: PartSegmentedHordeAdapter
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _xf := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _rs := PackedByteArray()
+# The adapter is a pure clock READER; this bench owns the tier mask + the shared
+# SlotClockBook (injected at construction; never reach into _adapter._clocks).
+var _tiers := PackedByteArray()
+var _book: SlotClockBook
+
+var _phase := 0  # 0 = correctness, 1 = perf sweep, 2 = perceptual, 3 = done
+var _frame := 0
+var _fails := 0
+var _watchdog := 0  # monotonic frame counter (hard anti-hang quit)
+
+# perf sweep state
+var _sweep_i := 0
+var _samples := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _results := []  # [{n, avg, p95, p99, max}]
+var _drift_start := Vector3.ZERO
+var _drift_rig := -1
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless":
+		# Headless instantiates zero rigs — verify the adapter no-ops, then quit clean.
+		var a := PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.new(self, true, CAP)
+		a.setup()
+		a.set_active(true)
+		var ok := not a.is_active() and a.last_live == 0
+		print("[part-bench] headless — adapter no-op %s; quit 0" % ("OK" if ok else "UNEXPECTED"))
+		get_tree().quit(0)
+		return
+
+	DisplayServer.window_set_vsync_mode(DisplayServer.VSYNC_DISABLED)
+	_cam = Camera3D.new()
+	_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_PERSPECTIVE
+	_cam.fov = 55.0
+	_cam.current = true
+	add_child(_cam)
+
+	_book = SlotClockBook.new()
+	_adapter = PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.new(self, false, CAP, _book)
+	if not _adapter.setup():
+		# setup() pushes an error naming any unresolved part (the runtime contract).
+		print("[part-bench] FAIL: setup returned false (see part-name push_error)")
+		get_tree().quit(1)
+		return
+	_adapter.set_active(true)
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(OUT_DIR))
+	print("[part-bench] start; cap=%d clips=%s" % [CAP, str(_adapter._intent_clip)])
+	_build_buffers(NC)
+	_frame_camera(NC)
+
+
+func _process(delta: float) -> void:
+	if _adapter == null:
+		return
+	# Hard watchdog: a self-quitting bench must never hang a window (~404 frames).
+	_watchdog += 1
+	if _watchdog > 1500:
+		push_error("[part-bench] watchdog tripped at %d frames — force quit" % _watchdog)
+		get_tree().quit(2)
+		return
+	if _phase == 0:
+		_process_correctness()
+	elif _phase == 1:
+		_process_perf(delta)
+	elif _phase == 2:
+		_process_perceptual(delta)
+
+
+func _process_correctness() -> void:
+	_advance_clocks(NC, DELTA_C)
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, NC, _tiers)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame < FRAMES_C:
+		return
+	_verify_correctness()
+	_phase = 1
+	_frame = 0
+	_sweep_i = 0
+	_samples = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_begin_sweep_n()
+
+
+func _verify_correctness() -> void:
+	var live_expected := NC - 1  # one dead slot
+	_check(
+		_adapter.last_live == live_expected,
+		"bind count == %d live" % live_expected,
+		"bound %d, expected %d" % [_adapter.last_live, live_expected]
+	)
+
+	var p1 := _pool_index_of(1)
+	_import_proof(p1)
+
+	# HitStop: frozen slot clock held at 0; control advanced FRAMES_C*DELTA_C.
+	var frozen_clk: float = _book.clock(FROZEN_SLOT)
+	var control_clk: float = _book.clock(CONTROL_SLOT)
+	_check(frozen_clk == 0.0, "hitstop clock frozen at 0", "frozen clock = %.3f" % frozen_clk)
+	var expect := float(FRAMES_C) * DELTA_C
+	_check(
+		absf(control_clk - expect) < 1e-4,
+		"control clock advanced to %.3f" % expect,
+		"control clock = %.3f" % control_clk
+	)
+
+	# speed_scale: frozen rig holds (0), control runs (1).
+	var fp := _pool_index_of(FROZEN_SLOT)
+	var cp := _pool_index_of(CONTROL_SLOT)
+	if fp >= 0 and cp >= 0:
+		var fpp: AnimationPlayer = _adapter._players[fp]
+		var cpp: AnimationPlayer = _adapter._players[cp]
+		if fpp != null and cpp != null:
+			_check(fpp.speed_scale == 0.0, "frozen speed_scale == 0", "= %.2f" % fpp.speed_scale)
+			_check(cpp.speed_scale == 1.0, "control speed_scale == 1", "= %.2f" % cpp.speed_scale)
+		else:
+			_check(false, "frozen + control rigs have AnimationPlayers", "player missing")
+	else:
+		_check(false, "frozen + control rigs bound", "pool index missing (f=%d c=%d)" % [fp, cp])
+
+	# intent -> clip table: locomotion + death resolved + differentiate.
+	var loco: StringName = _adapter._intent_clip[ANIM_LOCOMOTION]
+	var death: StringName = _adapter._intent_clip[ANIM_DEATH]
+	_check(
+		loco != &"" and death != &"",
+		"loco + death clips resolved",
+		"loco='%s' death='%s'" % [loco, death]
+	)
+	_check(loco != death, "intents differentiate (loco != death clip)", "both '%s'" % loco)
+
+	# per-instance tint across ALL 6 parts: first == last (none untinted) + distinct.
+	var m0 := _meshes_of(p1)
+	var m1 := _meshes_of(cp)
+	if p1 >= 0 and cp >= 0 and m0.size() > 0 and m1.size() > 0:
+		_check(
+			m0.size() == PART_COUNT, "rig has exactly 6 part surfaces", "surfaces = %d" % m0.size()
+		)
+		var h0_first: float = m0[0].get_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac")
+		var h0_last: float = m0[m0.size() - 1].get_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac")
+		var h1_first: float = m1[0].get_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac")
+		_check(
+			h0_first == h0_last,
+			"all 6 parts tinted (first == last)",
+			"first=%.3f last=%.3f" % [h0_first, h0_last]
+		)
+		_check(h0_first != h1_first, "tint distinct across rigs", "both = %.3f" % h0_first)
+	else:
+		_check(false, "tint-sample rigs bound with meshes", "missing meshes")
+
+	_verify_dismember_contract(p1)
+
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print("[part-bench] PHASE A: PASS (all correctness assertions)")
+	else:
+		print("[part-bench] PHASE A: FAIL (%d assertion(s))" % _fails)
+
+
+func _import_proof(pool_i: int) -> void:
+	if pool_i < 0:
+		_check(false, "import-proof rig bound", "no pool index")
+		return
+	var by_bit: Array = _adapter._part_by_bit[pool_i]
+	var all_live := by_bit.size() == PART_COUNT
+	for m in by_bit:
+		if m == null or not is_instance_valid(m):
+			all_live = false
+	_check(
+		all_live, "import-proof: all 6 bit->part nodes resolved + live", "by_bit=%s" % str(by_bit)
+	)
+	_check(_adapter._players[pool_i] != null, "rig has an AnimationPlayer", "player null")
+
+
+# Hide a subset via the mask, assert visibility; reset, assert all visible; then
+# RECYCLE the pool rig to a new agent slot and assert auto-reset — proving a hidden
+# limb cannot leak across pooled agents (lc-codex, load-bearing).
+func _verify_dismember_contract(pool_i: int) -> void:
+	if pool_i < 0:
+		_check(false, "dismember-contract rig bound", "no pool index")
+		return
+	var by_bit: Array = _adapter._part_by_bit[pool_i]
+	var mask := (1 << 0) | (1 << 1)  # hide head + arm-left
+	_adapter._apply_part_mask(pool_i, mask)
+	var hide_ok := true
+	for bit in PART_COUNT:
+		if by_bit[bit].visible != ((mask & (1 << bit)) == 0):
+			hide_ok = false
+	_check(hide_ok, "dismember: masked limbs hidden, others visible", "mask=%d mismatch" % mask)
+
+	_adapter.reset_parts_visible(pool_i)
+	var reset_ok := true
+	for bit in PART_COUNT:
+		if not by_bit[bit].visible:
+			reset_ok = false
+	_check(reset_ok, "reset_parts_visible restores all limbs", "a part stayed hidden")
+
+	# Re-hide, recycle to a fresh agent slot, assert the bind path restored visibility.
+	_adapter._apply_part_mask(pool_i, mask)
+	var new_slot: int = _adapter._pool_slot[pool_i] + 1000  # fresh id -> is_recycle
+	_adapter._bind(pool_i, new_slot, 5.0, 5.0, 0.0, ANIM_LOCOMOTION, 200, 0, 0.0)
+	var recycle_ok := true
+	for bit in PART_COUNT:
+		if not by_bit[bit].visible:
+			recycle_ok = false
+	_check(recycle_ok, "pooled recycle auto-resets limbs (no leak)", "a hidden limb leaked")
+
+
+func _begin_sweep_n() -> void:
+	var n: int = N_SWEEP[_sweep_i]
+	_build_buffers(n)
+	_frame_camera(maxi(n, 1))
+	_samples = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_frame = 0
+	_drift_rig = -1
+
+
+func _process_perf(delta: float) -> void:
+	var n: int = N_SWEEP[_sweep_i]
+	_advance_clocks(n, delta)
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, n, _tiers)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame <= WARMUP:
+		if _frame == WARMUP and n > 0:
+			_drift_rig = _pool_index_of(0)
+			if _drift_rig >= 0:
+				_drift_start = _rig_root_local(_drift_rig)
+		return
+	_samples.append(delta * 1000.0)  # ms
+	if _frame < WARMUP + SAMPLE:
+		return
+
+	_results.append(_summarise(n, _samples))
+	_capture_png(n)
+	if n > 0 and _drift_rig >= 0:
+		var drift := _rig_root_local(_drift_rig) - _drift_start
+		var horiz := Vector2(drift.x, drift.z).length()
+		_check(horiz < 0.05, "N=%d root drift < 0.05 (TRS clip)" % n, "drifted %.3f m" % horiz)
+
+	_sweep_i += 1
+	if _sweep_i < N_SWEEP.size():
+		_begin_sweep_n()
+	else:
+		_enter_perceptual()
+
+
+func _finalise() -> void:
+	var baseline: float = _results[0]["p95"]  # N=0
+	var cap := 0
+	print("[part-bench] PHASE B — isolation sweep (vsync off):")
+	for r in _results:
+		var marginal: float = r["p95"] - baseline
+		var within: bool = r["n"] == 0 or marginal <= BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS
+		if within and r["n"] > cap:
+			cap = r["n"]
+		print(
+			(
+				"[part-bench]   N=%2d  avg=%.3f  p95=%.3f  p99=%.3f  max=%.3f ms  (marg p95 +%.3f)%s"
+				% [
+					r["n"],
+					r["avg"],
+					r["p95"],
+					r["p99"],
+					r["max"],
+					marginal,
+					"" if within else "  OVER"
+				]
+			)
+		)
+	print(
+		(
+			"[part-bench] RECOMMENDED CAP = %d (p95 marginal <= %.1f ms over baseline)"
+			% [cap, BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS]
+		)
+	)
+	_write_json(cap, baseline)
+
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print("[part-bench] VERDICT: GO — mechanism verified; cap=%d" % cap)
+	else:
+		print("[part-bench] VERDICT: FAIL — %d correctness assertion(s)" % _fails)
+	_phase = 3
+	get_tree().quit(0 if _fails == 0 else 1)
+
+
+func _build_buffers(n: int) -> void:
+	_xf = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_rs = PackedByteArray()
+	_xf.resize(maxi(n, 1) * 8)
+	_rs.resize(maxi(n, 1) * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE)
+	var side := maxi(int(ceil(sqrt(float(maxi(n, 1))))), 1)
+	for i in n:
+		var gx := float(i % side) * SPACING
+		var gy := float(i / side) * SPACING
+		var ang := float(i) * 0.7
+		var cs := cos(ang)
+		var sn := sin(ang)
+		var o := i * 8
+		_xf[o + 0] = cs
+		_xf[o + 1] = -sn
+		_xf[o + 2] = 0.0
+		_xf[o + 3] = gx
+		_xf[o + 4] = sn
+		_xf[o + 5] = cs
+		_xf[o + 6] = 0.0
+		_xf[o + 7] = gy
+		var health := (i * 37) % 256
+		var hitstop := 6 if i == FROZEN_SLOT else 0
+		var ro := i * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		_rs[ro + 0] = LIVE_BIT
+		_rs[ro + 1] = health
+		_rs[ro + 2] = hitstop
+		_rs[ro + 3] = ANIM_LOCOMOTION
+	if n == NC:
+		for k in 8:
+			_xf[DEAD_SLOT * 8 + k] = 0.0
+		_rs[CONTROL_SLOT * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE + 3] = ANIM_DEATH
+	_tiers.resize(maxi(n, 1))
+	for i in _tiers.size():
+		_tiers[i] = TIER_SKEL
+	if n == NC:
+		_tiers[DEAD_SLOT] = TIER_DEAD
+
+
+# Advance the shared clock once per slot, exactly as LODManager3D does (the adapter
+# is a pure reader): mark DEAD slots, advance live slots HitStop-frozen.
+func _advance_clocks(n: int, delta: float) -> void:
+	_book.ensure_capacity(n)
+	for s in n:
+		if _tiers[s] == TIER_DEAD:
+			_book.mark_dead(s)
+		else:
+			_book.advance(s, delta, _rs[s * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE + RS_HITSTOP])
+
+
+func _frame_camera(n: int) -> void:
+	var side := maxi(int(ceil(sqrt(float(n)))), 1)
+	var extent := float(side) * SPACING
+	var c := extent * 0.5
+	_cam.position = Vector3(c, extent * 0.95, extent * 1.45)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(c, 0.5, c), Vector3.UP)
+
+
+# Perceptual baseline phase: reframe to a low, close, combat-distance camera, let a
+# few real frames render so the move propagates, then capture + finalise — the
+# gameplay-distance legibility frame the cohort asked for. (A force_draw captured the
+# sweep overview as a stale frame; a real rendered frame is correct.)
+func _enter_perceptual() -> void:
+	var n: int = N_SWEEP[N_SWEEP.size() - 1]
+	_build_buffers(n)
+	var c := float(maxi(int(ceil(sqrt(float(n)))), 1)) * SPACING * 0.5
+	_cam.position = Vector3(c, 6.0, c + 9.0)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(c, 0.8, c), Vector3.UP)
+	_phase = 2
+	_frame = 0
+
+
+func _process_perceptual(delta: float) -> void:
+	var n: int = N_SWEEP[N_SWEEP.size() - 1]
+	_advance_clocks(n, delta)
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, n, _tiers)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame < 4:
+		return
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img != null:
+		var path := OUT_DIR + "perceptual_gameplay_cam.png"
+		img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+		print("[part-bench] perceptual baseline capture %s" % path)
+	_finalise()
+
+
+func _summarise(n: int, s: PackedFloat32Array) -> Dictionary:
+	var arr := s.duplicate()
+	arr.sort()
+	var cnt := arr.size()
+	var sum := 0.0
+	for v in arr:
+		sum += v
+	return {
+		"n": n,
+		"avg": sum / maxf(float(cnt), 1.0),
+		"p95": arr[mini(int(0.95 * cnt), cnt - 1)] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+		"p99": arr[mini(int(0.99 * cnt), cnt - 1)] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+		"max": arr[cnt - 1] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+	}
+
+
+func _write_json(cap: int, baseline: float) -> void:
+	var doc := {
+		"schema": "part-segmented-cap-bench-v1",
+		"recommended_cap": cap,
+		"budget_marginal_ms": BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS,
+		"baseline_p95_ms": baseline,
+		"renderer": RenderingServer.get_video_adapter_name(),
+		"godot": Engine.get_version_info().get("string", "?"),
+		"viewport": [get_viewport().size.x, get_viewport().size.y],
+		"vsync": "disabled",
+		"sweep": _results,
+	}
+	var path := OUT_DIR + "part_segmented_cap.json"
+	var f := FileAccess.open(path, FileAccess.WRITE)
+	if f != null:
+		f.store_string(JSON.stringify(doc, "  "))
+		f.close()
+		print("[part-bench] wrote %s" % path)
+	print("[part-bench] JSON: %s" % JSON.stringify(doc))
+
+
+func _capture_png(n: int) -> void:
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img != null:
+		var path := OUT_DIR + "part_n%d.png" % n
+		img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+		print("[part-bench] capture %s" % path)
+
+
+func _check(ok: bool, label: String, fail_detail: String) -> void:
+	if ok:
+		print("[part-bench] PASS: %s" % label)
+	else:
+		_fails += 1
+		print("[part-bench] FAIL: %s — %s" % [label, fail_detail])
+
+
+func _pool_index_of(slot: int) -> int:
+	for i in _adapter._pool_slot.size():
+		if _adapter._pool_slot[i] == slot:
+			return i
+	return -1
+
+
+func _meshes_of(pool_i: int) -> Array:
+	return _adapter._meshes[pool_i] if pool_i >= 0 and pool_i < _adapter._meshes.size() else []
+
+
+func _rig_root_local(pool_i: int) -> Vector3:
+	var holder: Node3D = _adapter._holders[pool_i]
+	if holder == null or holder.get_child_count() == 0:
+		return Vector3.ZERO
+	var rig := holder.get_child(0) as Node3D
+	return rig.position if rig != null else Vector3.ZERO

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/GibDebrisRenderer.gd b/apps/game-godot/GibDebrisRenderer.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..5297344
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/GibDebrisRenderer.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,467 @@
+class_name GibDebrisRenderer
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 gore epic mmnto-ai/liquid-city#565, PR2 (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#560) —
+# RENDER-ONLY gib (gore) debris. When the Rust sim resolves a `Gib` kill (a car
+# pulping a zombie at extreme per-kg momentum) it appends a render-only GibEvent
+# to the FFI drain buffer; sim_driver drains it each frame via
+# `sim.get_gib_events()` and feeds the bytes to `ingest()`. This renderer then
+# spawns flying body-part debris + a blood-mist pop at the kill site.
+#
+# NO determinism concerns and NO physics bodies (the cohort decided this): the
+# Gib decision + the despawn already ran authoritatively in the resolver; THIS
+# is a pure one-shot visual feed (ADR-002 visual-physics checksum exclusion /
+# ADR-027 §4). Every chunk is KINEMATIC — a closed-form ballistic arc advanced
+# per-frame (pos += vel*dt; vel.y -= g*dt) with a tumble spin, then it fades +
+# retires. NO RigidBody3D, NO Skeleton3D — chunks are rigid part meshes lifted
+# from the SAME blocky kit the part-segmented horde renders (character-a.glb =
+# 6 separate meshes: head/torso/arm-left/arm-right/leg-left/leg-right).
+#
+# SCOPE: spawn the burst + the mist, advance + retire, draw via 6 MultiMesh (one
+# per part mesh, ~6 draw calls total). Determinism: ALL scatter randomness is
+# derived from the event `seed` (sim-computed, ADR-027 rule-2 — no Godot-side
+# hash drift) through a LOCAL RandomNumberGenerator, NEVER the global randf()/
+# randi(). NON-goals: ground decals (GoreSlick) defer to a later PR; dismember
+# (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561) is a sibling on the horde adapter, not here.
+
+# MIRROR of damage.rs GIB_* — keep in sync. Each record is GIB_EVENT_STRIDE
+# bytes, little-endian; read with bytes.decode_u32 / bytes.decode_float. The Rust
+# `gib_event_abi_layout_is_stable` test locks the producer side.
+const GIB_EVENT_STRIDE := 36
+const GIB_OFF_SLOT := 0  # u32 — stable replicated BufferSlot (unused here; debug)
+const GIB_OFF_TICK := 4  # u32 — sim tick of the kill (unused here; debug)
+const GIB_OFF_SEED := 8  # u32 — deterministic scatter seed; drives ALL randomness
+const GIB_OFF_POS_X := 12  # f32
+const GIB_OFF_POS_Y := 16  # f32
+const GIB_OFF_POS_Z := 20  # f32 — world height (z = floor*FLOOR_HEIGHT + altitude)
+const GIB_OFF_VEL_X := 24  # f32
+const GIB_OFF_VEL_Y := 28  # f32
+const GIB_OFF_VEL_Z := 32  # f32
+
+# Mesh source: the blocky kit's 6 rigid part meshes (SAME asset as the part-
+# segmented horde). Names match PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.BIT_TO_PART (Kenney
+# verbatim, case-insensitive) — resolved by node name on import, not glTF index.
+const RIG_GLB_PATH := "res://assets/blocky-characters/character-a.glb"
+const PART_NAMES: Array[StringName] = [
+	&"head", &"torso", &"arm-left", &"arm-right", &"leg-left", &"leg-right"
+]
+const PART_COUNT := 6
+
+# A 32-bit odd constant (golden-ratio bits) so per-chunk seed mixing de-
+# correlates the 6 chunks of one event (each part scatters independently from
+# the same event seed). Same magic the part-segment kit uses elsewhere.
+const CHUNK_SEED_MIX := 0x9E3779B9
+
+# Caps + fade. Oldest-first retirement when a new burst would overflow the pool.
+const MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS := 256
+# Blood-mist emitter pool — small; cycle the oldest on overflow (Gib is rare).
+const BLOOD_POOL_SIZE := 12
+# Last fraction of a chunk's TTL spent shrinking toward zero (visual fade-out).
+const FADE_FRACTION := 0.25
+
+# Over-the-top "Carmageddon dial" — crank these for more comically violent gore.
+@export var gib_loft_mult := 1.6  # upward kick added to scatter so limbs sail high
+@export var gib_chunk_ttl := 2.0  # seconds a chunk lives before it shrinks out
+@export var gib_gravity := 14.0  # Godot-Y downward accel on each chunk (m/s^2)
+@export var gib_scatter_speed := 6.0  # base scatter cone speed (m/s)
+@export var gib_blood_enabled := true  # red mist puff on each pulp
+
+var _headless := false
+var _ready_done := false
+
+# Per-part mesh resources (index parallel to PART_NAMES) + their draw nodes.
+var _part_meshes: Array[Mesh] = []
+var _part_scales: Array[Vector3] = []  # GLB import scale per part (lost if only .mesh is kept)
+var _multis: Array[MultiMeshInstance3D] = []
+
+# Chunk pool (parallel arrays — allocation-light per frame; no per-chunk objects).
+# A chunk is active iff `_chunk_age[i] >= 0.0`; -1.0 marks a free slot.
+var _chunk_part := PackedInt32Array()  # which part mesh (0..PART_COUNT-1)
+var _chunk_pos := PackedVector3Array()  # Godot world position
+var _chunk_vel := PackedVector3Array()  # Godot world velocity
+var _chunk_axis := PackedVector3Array()  # unit tumble axis
+var _chunk_spin := PackedFloat32Array()  # tumble rate (rad/s)
+var _chunk_angle := PackedFloat32Array()  # accumulated tumble angle (rad)
+var _chunk_age := PackedFloat32Array()  # seconds alive; -1.0 == free slot
+var _chunk_birth := PackedInt64Array()  # monotonic birth ticket (oldest-first)
+var _active_count := 0
+var _birth_counter := 0
+
+# Blood-mist emitter pool (round-robin; cycle the oldest on overflow).
+var _blood_pool: Array[GPUParticles3D] = []
+var _blood_next := 0
+
+# Scratch transform reused per-instance in _process so the per-frame buffer
+# rebuild stays allocation-light (no per-chunk Transform3D churn).
+var _scratch := Transform3D.IDENTITY
+
+# Per-part instance-transform lists, preallocated once + cleared each frame
+# (CR / Greptile: avoid the per-frame Array[Array] heap churn while active).
+var _per_part_xforms: Array[Array] = []
+
+# Single RNG reused across every burst (GCA): _rng.seed is reset per chunk in
+# _spawn_burst, so reuse is bit-identical to a fresh instance + allocation-free.
+var _rng := RandomNumberGenerator.new()
+
+
+# Build the 6 part meshes + 6 MultiMeshInstance3D children + the blood-mist pool.
+# Idempotent. Headless: no visual nodes, ingest()/_process() become no-ops (the
+# determinism/capture harness runs headless and must never touch the GPU).
+func setup(headless: bool) -> void:
+	if _ready_done:
+		return
+	_headless = headless
+	_init_pool_state()
+	if _headless:
+		_ready_done = true
+		return
+	if not _load_part_meshes():
+		push_error("GibDebrisRenderer: failed to load part meshes — gibs disabled")
+		# Stay un-ready so ingest()/_process() no-op; the kill still despawned sim-side.
+		return
+	_build_multimeshes()
+	if gib_blood_enabled:
+		_build_blood_pool()
+	_ready_done = true
+
+
+func _init_pool_state() -> void:
+	_chunk_part.resize(MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS)
+	_chunk_pos.resize(MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS)
+	_chunk_vel.resize(MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS)
+	_chunk_axis.resize(MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS)
+	_chunk_spin.resize(MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS)
+	_chunk_angle.resize(MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS)
+	_chunk_age.resize(MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS)
+	_chunk_age.fill(-1.0)
+	_chunk_birth.resize(MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS)
+	_active_count = 0
+
+
+# Load the GLB once, recursively collect its MeshInstance3D parts, keep each
+# part's `.mesh` resource by name, then free the temp instance. A missing part is
+# a graceful skip (push_warning) — its slot in _part_meshes stays null so the
+# spawn loop draws no chunk for it (the burst is just smaller).
+func _load_part_meshes() -> bool:
+	var scene := load(RIG_GLB_PATH) as PackedScene
+	if scene == null:
+		push_error("GibDebrisRenderer: failed to load rig scene at %s" % RIG_GLB_PATH)
+		return false
+	var rig := scene.instantiate() as Node3D
+	if rig == null:
+		push_error("GibDebrisRenderer: failed to instantiate rig from %s" % RIG_GLB_PATH)
+		return false
+	var meshes: Array[MeshInstance3D] = []
+	_collect_meshes(rig, meshes)
+	_part_meshes.resize(PART_COUNT)
+	_part_scales.resize(PART_COUNT)
+	_part_scales.fill(Vector3.ONE)
+	var found := 0
+	for p in PART_COUNT:
+		var inst := _find_part(meshes, PART_NAMES[p])
+		if inst == null or inst.mesh == null:
+			push_warning(
+				"GibDebrisRenderer: rig missing part '%s' — gibs skip that limb" % PART_NAMES[p]
+			)
+			_part_meshes[p] = null
+			continue
+		_part_meshes[p] = inst.mesh
+		_part_scales[p] = _cumulative_scale(inst, rig)
+		found += 1
+	# Free the temp rig (its mesh resources stay referenced via _part_meshes).
+	rig.queue_free()
+	return found > 0
+
+
+# Build one MultiMeshInstance3D per part mesh. Each MultiMesh is TRANSFORM_3D and
+# its instance_count + per-instance transforms are rebuilt every _process frame
+# from the active chunks for that part (~6 draw calls total). A null part mesh
+# gets a placeholder MultiMesh that simply never receives instances.
+func _build_multimeshes() -> void:
+	_multis.resize(PART_COUNT)
+	for p in PART_COUNT:
+		var mmi := MultiMeshInstance3D.new()
+		mmi.name = "GibPart%d" % p
+		var mm := MultiMesh.new()
+		mm.transform_format = MultiMesh.TRANSFORM_3D
+		mm.mesh = _part_meshes[p]
+		mm.instance_count = 0
+		mmi.multimesh = mm
+		add_child(mmi)
+		_multis[p] = mmi
+	_per_part_xforms.resize(PART_COUNT)
+	for pi in PART_COUNT:
+		_per_part_xforms[pi] = []
+
+
+# Build the blood-mist emitter pool: BLOOD_POOL_SIZE one-shot GPUParticles3D, each
+# a red expanding puff (initial velocity spread + gravity + short lifetime). On a
+# gib event we grab the next emitter (cycle oldest), move it to the kill pos, and
+# restart() it. Kept deliberately simple + robust.
+func _build_blood_pool() -> void:
+	var process_mat := _make_blood_process_material()
+	var draw_mat := _make_blood_draw_material()
+	var draw_mesh := SphereMesh.new()
+	draw_mesh.radius = 0.08
+	draw_mesh.height = 0.16
+	draw_mesh.radial_segments = 6
+	draw_mesh.rings = 3
+	draw_mesh.material = draw_mat
+	_blood_pool.resize(BLOOD_POOL_SIZE)
+	for i in BLOOD_POOL_SIZE:
+		var em := GPUParticles3D.new()
+		em.name = "GibBlood%d" % i
+		em.emitting = false
+		em.one_shot = true
+		em.amount = 24
+		em.lifetime = 0.6
+		em.explosiveness = 1.0  # all particles fire at once (a puff, not a stream)
+		em.process_material = process_mat
+		em.draw_pass_1 = draw_mesh
+		add_child(em)
+		_blood_pool[i] = em
+
+
+func _make_blood_process_material() -> ParticleProcessMaterial:
+	var mat := ParticleProcessMaterial.new()
+	mat.direction = Vector3(0.0, 1.0, 0.0)
+	mat.spread = 75.0  # wide cone — a splattering puff
+	mat.initial_velocity_min = 1.5
+	mat.initial_velocity_max = 4.5
+	mat.gravity = Vector3(0.0, -9.0, 0.0)
+	mat.scale_min = 0.5
+	mat.scale_max = 1.4
+	return mat
+
+
+func _make_blood_draw_material() -> StandardMaterial3D:
+	var mat := StandardMaterial3D.new()
+	mat.albedo_color = Color(0.55, 0.02, 0.02)  # dark arterial red
+	# Unshaded so the mist reads as a bright pop regardless of scene lighting
+	# (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#586 owns the lighting pass; gibs must not depend on it).
+	mat.shading_mode = BaseMaterial3D.SHADING_MODE_UNSHADED
+	return mat
+
+
+# Decode each 36-byte record + spawn a 6-chunk burst per event. Called each frame
+# by sim_driver with `sim.get_gib_events()`. Guard: not-ready / headless / empty
+# all early-return (the harness must never touch the GPU).
+func ingest(events: PackedByteArray) -> void:
+	if not _ready_done or _headless or events.is_empty():
+		return
+	var n := events.size() / GIB_EVENT_STRIDE
+	for e in n:
+		var base := e * GIB_EVENT_STRIDE
+		var seed := events.decode_u32(base + GIB_OFF_SEED)
+		# sim (x, y) ground plane + separate height z → Godot (x, z, y) with
+		# Godot-Y = UP (IDENTICAL convention to PartSegmentedHordeAdapter._bind).
+		var px := events.decode_float(base + GIB_OFF_POS_X)
+		var py := events.decode_float(base + GIB_OFF_POS_Y)
+		var pz := events.decode_float(base + GIB_OFF_POS_Z)
+		var vx := events.decode_float(base + GIB_OFF_VEL_X)
+		var vy := events.decode_float(base + GIB_OFF_VEL_Y)
+		var vz := events.decode_float(base + GIB_OFF_VEL_Z)
+		var kill_pos := Vector3(px, pz, py)
+		var spray_vel := Vector3(vx, vz, vy)
+		_spawn_burst(seed, kill_pos, spray_vel)
+		if gib_blood_enabled:
+			_pop_blood(kill_pos)
+
+
+# Spawn ONE chunk of each of the 6 parts from the kill position, with a seed-
+# driven scatter cone BIASED toward the (Godot-mapped) spray velocity, plus per-
+# chunk random speed, an upward loft (gib_loft_mult — limbs sail comically high),
+# and a random tumble. ALL randomness comes from a LOCAL RNG seeded off the event
+# seed mixed with the chunk index — reproducible per the event seed, NEVER the
+# global randf()/randi().
+func _spawn_burst(seed: int, kill_pos: Vector3, spray_vel: Vector3) -> void:
+	# Bias direction = the spray velocity (downrange the way the car was going);
+	# fall back to straight up when the kill carried no spray (degenerate impulse).
+	var spray_dir := spray_vel.normalized() if spray_vel.length() > 0.001 else Vector3.UP
+	# Reuse the class-level _rng (CR/GCA) - reset per chunk; determinism is
+	# unchanged since the seed is set fresh each iteration.
+	for chunk_index in PART_COUNT:
+		if _part_meshes[chunk_index] == null:
+			continue
+		# Mix the chunk index so the 6 parts of one kill scatter independently.
+		_rng.seed = seed ^ (chunk_index * CHUNK_SEED_MIX)
+		# Scatter cone: jitter the bias direction, then loft. randf_range draws are
+		# deterministic for a fixed _rng.seed (no global RNG touched).
+		var jitter := Vector3(
+			_rng.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0), _rng.randf_range(-0.2, 1.0), _rng.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0)
+		)
+		var dir := (spray_dir + jitter * 0.6).normalized()
+		var speed := gib_scatter_speed * _rng.randf_range(0.6, 1.4)
+		var loft := Vector3.UP * gib_scatter_speed * gib_loft_mult * _rng.randf_range(0.4, 1.0)
+		var vel := dir * speed + loft
+		var axis := Vector3(
+			_rng.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0), _rng.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0), _rng.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0)
+		)
+		axis = axis.normalized() if axis.length() > 0.001 else Vector3.UP
+		var spin := _rng.randf_range(6.0, 18.0)  # rad/s tumble
+		_add_chunk(chunk_index, kill_pos, vel, axis, spin)
+
+
+# Claim a pool slot for a new chunk. Oldest-first retirement when the pool is full
+# (a new burst would exceed MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS — the oldest active chunk is
+# overwritten so the freshest carnage always wins).
+func _add_chunk(part: int, pos: Vector3, vel: Vector3, axis: Vector3, spin: float) -> void:
+	var slot := _find_free_slot()
+	if slot < 0:
+		slot = _oldest_slot()
+	else:
+		_active_count += 1
+	_chunk_part[slot] = part
+	_chunk_pos[slot] = pos
+	_chunk_vel[slot] = vel
+	_chunk_axis[slot] = axis
+	_chunk_spin[slot] = spin
+	_chunk_angle[slot] = 0.0
+	_chunk_age[slot] = 0.0
+	_chunk_birth[slot] = _birth_counter
+	_birth_counter += 1
+
+
+func _find_free_slot() -> int:
+	for i in MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS:
+		if _chunk_age[i] < 0.0:
+			return i
+	return -1
+
+
+# The oldest active chunk by birth ticket (lowest _chunk_birth among active).
+# Only reached when the pool is full, so at least one active chunk exists.
+func _oldest_slot() -> int:
+	var best := -1
+	var best_birth := 0
+	for i in MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS:
+		if _chunk_age[i] >= 0.0 and (best < 0 or _chunk_birth[i] < best_birth):
+			best_birth = _chunk_birth[i]
+			best = i
+	if best < 0:
+		push_error("GibDebrisRenderer: _oldest_slot called with no active chunks")
+		return 0
+	return best
+
+
+# Cycle the next blood emitter (oldest-first round-robin), move it to the kill
+# position, ensure one_shot, and restart() the puff.
+func _pop_blood(kill_pos: Vector3) -> void:
+	if _blood_pool.is_empty():
+		return
+	var em := _blood_pool[_blood_next]
+	_blood_next = (_blood_next + 1) % _blood_pool.size()
+	if em == null:
+		return
+	em.global_position = kill_pos
+	em.one_shot = true
+	em.restart()
+
+
+# Per-frame: advance every active chunk along its closed-form ballistic arc
+# (pos += vel*dt; vel.y -= g*dt) + tumble, fade (shrink) in the last FADE_FRACTION
+# of its TTL, retire at end-of-life, then rebuild the 6 MultiMesh buffers from the
+# survivors. No-op when headless / not ready (the harness never touches the GPU).
+func _process(delta: float) -> void:
+	if not _ready_done or _headless:
+		return
+	if _active_count == 0:
+		return
+	var fade_start := gib_chunk_ttl * (1.0 - FADE_FRACTION)
+	# Per-part instance lists, preallocated at setup + cleared each frame (no
+	# per-frame heap churn while chunks are active - CR / Greptile).
+	for p in PART_COUNT:
+		_per_part_xforms[p].clear()
+
+	for i in MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS:
+		if _chunk_age[i] < 0.0:
+			continue
+		var age := _chunk_age[i] + delta
+		if age >= gib_chunk_ttl:
+			# Retire: free the slot.
+			_chunk_age[i] = -1.0
+			_active_count -= 1
+			continue
+		_chunk_age[i] = age
+		# Closed-form arc + tumble.
+		var vel := _chunk_vel[i]
+		vel.y -= gib_gravity * delta
+		_chunk_vel[i] = vel
+		_chunk_pos[i] += vel * delta
+		_chunk_angle[i] += _chunk_spin[i] * delta
+		# Fade: scale toward zero across the last FADE_FRACTION of the TTL so the
+		# chunk visually shrinks out instead of popping. (`chunk_scale`, not `scale`
+		# — `scale` is the inherited Node3D property; shadowing it trips gdlint.)
+		var chunk_scale := 1.0
+		if age > fade_start:
+			chunk_scale = maxf(0.0, 1.0 - (age - fade_start) / (gib_chunk_ttl - fade_start))
+		# Build the instance transform: tumble basis (scaled) at the world pos.
+		_scratch.basis = Basis(_chunk_axis[i], _chunk_angle[i]).scaled(
+			_part_scales[_chunk_part[i]] * chunk_scale
+		)
+		_scratch.origin = _chunk_pos[i]
+		(_per_part_xforms[_chunk_part[i]] as Array).append(_scratch)
+
+	# Push the rebuilt per-part instance lists into each MultiMesh.
+	for p in PART_COUNT:
+		var mm := _multis[p].multimesh
+		var xforms: Array = _per_part_xforms[p]
+		mm.instance_count = xforms.size()
+		for k in xforms.size():
+			mm.set_instance_transform(k, xforms[k])
+
+
+# Drop all active chunks (scene resets / benches). Leaves the pool + nodes intact;
+# the next frame's rebuild empties every MultiMesh.
+func clear_all() -> void:
+	if not _ready_done:
+		return
+	_chunk_age.fill(-1.0)
+	_active_count = 0
+	if _headless:
+		return
+	for p in PART_COUNT:
+		if p < _multis.size() and _multis[p] != null:
+			_multis[p].multimesh.instance_count = 0
+
+
+func debug_status() -> String:
+	if _headless or not _ready_done:
+		return "Gibs: off"
+	return "Gibs: %d chunks" % _active_count
+
+
+# Find a part MeshInstance3D by its (case-insensitive) node name among the rig's
+# collected meshes (Godot keeps glTF mesh-node names on import). IDENTICAL match
+# rule to PartSegmentedHordeAdapter._find_part so the same kit resolves the same
+# way in both renderers.
+func _find_part(meshes: Array, part_name: StringName) -> MeshInstance3D:
+	var want := String(part_name).to_lower()
+	for m in meshes:
+		if String(m.name).to_lower() == want:
+			return m
+	return null
+
+
+func _collect_meshes(n: Node, out: Array[MeshInstance3D]) -> void:
+	if n is MeshInstance3D:
+		out.append(n)
+	for c in n.get_children():
+		_collect_meshes(c, out)
+
+
+# The cumulative scale from the rig root down to a part node. The blocky kit
+# applies its real size via node transforms in the .glb; keeping only `.mesh`
+# (for the MultiMesh) drops that, so a gib chunk would render at the raw authored
+# size. Bake the scale into each chunk so debris matches the horde limb size.
+func _cumulative_scale(node: Node, root: Node) -> Vector3:
+	var s := Vector3.ONE
+	var n := node
+	while n != null:
+		if n is Node3D:
+			# .scale (not basis.get_scale()) keeps mirror/negative signs + skips the sqrt.
+			s *= (n as Node3D).scale
+		if n == root:
+			break
+		n = n.get_parent()
+	return s

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/gib_debris_bench.gd b/apps/game-godot/gib_debris_bench.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..7b8d42c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/gib_debris_bench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,218 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 gore epic mmnto-ai/liquid-city#565, PR2 (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#560) —
+# gib debris renderer bench. REAL GPU (headless asserts the no-op + quits clean).
+# PHASE A (gates exit): synthetic gib-event bursts spawn chunks, the pool cap holds
+# under a flood, clear_all empties. PHASE B (reports): a gameplay-camera "car-plow
+# line" perceptual capture — read the PNG (the cohort's acceptance gate; gibs are a
+# FEEL claim, evidenced at the player's frame, not theorized).
+#
+# Synthetic events stand in for sim.get_gib_events(): the Gib rung is tuning-
+# unreachable at v0 (operator's GIB_MOMENTUM_RATIO knob — spec mmnto-ai/liquid-city#506),
+# so the RENDER path is proven here from hand-built ABI bytes, and the sim->event
+# path is proven by the damage.rs cargo tests. Same 36-byte ABI as the producer.
+# Run: <godot> --path apps/game-godot res://gib_debris_bench.tscn
+#   0 = correctness clean, 1 = assertion failed, 2 = watchdog.
+
+const GIB_EVENT_STRIDE := 36
+const OUT_DIR := "user://gib_debris_bench/"
+const PLOW_N := 12  # zombies in the car-plow line
+const SPACING := 2.4
+const PART_CHUNKS := 6  # one chunk per body part per kill (head/torso/2 arms/2 legs)
+const CAPTURE_AFTER_S := 0.7  # let debris arc into the air before the photo
+
+var _renderer: GibDebrisRenderer
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _phase := 0
+var _frame := 0
+var _elapsed := 0.0
+var _fails := 0
+var _watchdog := 0
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless":
+		# Headless: the renderer builds no GPU nodes and ingest() no-ops.
+		var r := GibDebrisRenderer.new()
+		add_child(r)
+		r.setup(true)
+		r.ingest(_plow_bytes())
+		var ok := r.debug_status() == "Gibs: off"
+		print("[gib-bench] headless — renderer no-op %s; quit 0" % ("OK" if ok else "UNEXPECTED"))
+		get_tree().quit(0)
+		return
+
+	DisplayServer.window_set_vsync_mode(DisplayServer.VSYNC_DISABLED)
+	Engine.max_fps = 144  # cap fps so the time-based capture stays well under the watchdog
+	_cam = Camera3D.new()
+	_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_PERSPECTIVE
+	_cam.fov = 60.0
+	_cam.current = true
+	add_child(_cam)
+	# Gameplay-distance framing of the plow line (sim x:0..N*SPACING at z=0 maps to
+	# Godot x,0,0 in the renderer): low + close, looking down the line.
+	var mid := float(PLOW_N) * SPACING * 0.5
+	_cam.position = Vector3(mid, 6.5, mid + 15.0)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(mid, 2.5, 4.0), Vector3.UP)
+	_add_scene()
+
+	_renderer = GibDebrisRenderer.new()
+	add_child(_renderer)
+	_renderer.setup(false)
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(OUT_DIR))
+	print("[gib-bench] start; renderer=%s" % _renderer.debug_status())
+
+
+func _process(delta: float) -> void:
+	if _renderer == null:
+		return
+	# Hard watchdog: a self-quitting bench must never hang a window.
+	_watchdog += 1
+	if _watchdog > 1200:
+		push_error("[gib-bench] watchdog tripped — force quit")
+		get_tree().quit(2)
+		return
+	if _phase == 0:
+		_process_correctness()
+	elif _phase == 1:
+		_process_perceptual(delta)
+
+
+func _process_correctness() -> void:
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame == 1:
+		# One plow burst: PLOW_N kills x 6 part-chunks each.
+		_renderer.ingest(_plow_bytes())
+		var expect := PLOW_N * PART_CHUNKS
+		_check(
+			_renderer._active_count == expect,
+			"burst spawns %d chunks" % expect,
+			"got %d" % _renderer._active_count
+		)
+		# Cap: a flood far past the cap must clamp (oldest-first retirement).
+		_renderer.ingest(_flood_bytes(200))  # 200 * 6 = 1200 >> 256
+		_check(
+			_renderer._active_count <= GibDebrisRenderer.MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS,
+			"pool cap holds (<= %d)" % GibDebrisRenderer.MAX_ACTIVE_GIB_CHUNKS,
+			"got %d" % _renderer._active_count
+		)
+		return
+	if _frame < 6:
+		return  # let a few frames advance the arcs (proves _process is stable)
+	_renderer.clear_all()
+	_check(
+		_renderer._active_count == 0,
+		"clear_all empties the pool",
+		"got %d" % _renderer._active_count
+	)
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print("[gib-bench] PHASE A: PASS")
+	else:
+		print("[gib-bench] PHASE A: FAIL (%d assertion(s))" % _fails)
+	# Seed the carnage to photograph + reframe to the perceptual phase.
+	_renderer.ingest(_plow_bytes())
+	_phase = 1
+	_elapsed = 0.0
+
+
+func _process_perceptual(delta: float) -> void:
+	# Let the debris arc up for CAPTURE_AFTER_S so the chunks read as airborne
+	# carnage (frame 0 would catch them still at the kill points). Elapsed time,
+	# not a frame count, so it is robust under vsync-off.
+	_elapsed += delta
+	if _elapsed < CAPTURE_AFTER_S:
+		return
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img != null:
+		var path := OUT_DIR + "perceptual_carplow_gibs.png"
+		img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+		print(
+			(
+				"[gib-bench] perceptual capture %s (%d chunks airborne)"
+				% [path, _renderer._active_count]
+			)
+		)
+	_finalise()
+
+
+func _finalise() -> void:
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print("[gib-bench] VERDICT: GO — gib render path verified; perceptual PNG captured")
+	else:
+		print("[gib-bench] VERDICT: FAIL — %d assertion(s)" % _fails)
+	get_tree().quit(0 if _fails == 0 else 1)
+
+
+# Synthetic gib-event bytes for a car-plow line: PLOW_N kills along sim-x at z=0,
+# each thrown downrange (+x) + lofted (+ sim-height z). Mirrors the damage.rs ABI.
+func _plow_bytes() -> PackedByteArray:
+	var evs := []
+	for i in PLOW_N:
+		var ev := {
+			"slot": i,
+			"tick": 0,
+			"seed": (0x51ED + i * 0x9E3779B9) & 0xFFFFFFFF,
+			"pos": Vector3(float(i) * SPACING, 0.0, 0.0),  # sim x, y, height z
+			"vel": Vector3(7.0, 0.0, 4.0),  # downrange +x, no lateral, +loft
+		}
+		evs.append(ev)
+	return _encode(evs)
+
+
+func _flood_bytes(n: int) -> PackedByteArray:
+	var evs := []
+	for i in n:
+		var ev := {
+			"slot": 1000 + i,
+			"tick": 0,
+			"seed": (i * 0x9E3779B9) & 0xFFFFFFFF,
+			"pos": Vector3(fmod(float(i), 10.0) * SPACING, 0.0, 0.0),
+			"vel": Vector3(6.0, 0.0, 5.0),
+		}
+		evs.append(ev)
+	return _encode(evs)
+
+
+func _encode(evs: Array) -> PackedByteArray:
+	var b := PackedByteArray()
+	b.resize(evs.size() * GIB_EVENT_STRIDE)
+	for i in evs.size():
+		var e: Dictionary = evs[i]
+		var o := i * GIB_EVENT_STRIDE
+		b.encode_u32(o + 0, int(e["slot"]) & 0xFFFFFFFF)
+		b.encode_u32(o + 4, int(e["tick"]) & 0xFFFFFFFF)
+		b.encode_u32(o + 8, int(e["seed"]) & 0xFFFFFFFF)
+		var p: Vector3 = e["pos"]
+		var v: Vector3 = e["vel"]
+		b.encode_float(o + 12, p.x)
+		b.encode_float(o + 16, p.y)
+		b.encode_float(o + 20, p.z)
+		b.encode_float(o + 24, v.x)
+		b.encode_float(o + 28, v.y)
+		b.encode_float(o + 32, v.z)
+	return b
+
+
+# A dim ground plane + a sun so airborne debris reads against the floor in the
+# perceptual capture (this is a bench prop, NOT the game's lighting — that is
+# mmnto-ai/liquid-city#586).
+func _add_scene() -> void:
+	var ground := MeshInstance3D.new()
+	var pm := PlaneMesh.new()
+	pm.size = Vector2(60.0, 60.0)
+	ground.mesh = pm
+	var mat := StandardMaterial3D.new()
+	mat.albedo_color = Color(0.16, 0.17, 0.19)
+	ground.material_override = mat
+	add_child(ground)
+	var sun := DirectionalLight3D.new()
+	sun.rotation_degrees = Vector3(-55.0, -35.0, 0.0)
+	add_child(sun)
+
+
+func _check(ok: bool, label: String, detail: String) -> void:
+	if ok:
+		print("[gib-bench] PASS: %s" % label)
+	else:
+		_fails += 1
+		print("[gib-bench] FAIL: %s — %s" % [label, detail])

+/// Bytes per packed [`GibEvent`] record in the `get_gib_events` FFI buffer.

+pub const GIB_OFF_TICK: usize = 4;
+pub const GIB_OFF_SEED: usize = 8;

+pub const GIB_OFF_POS_Y: usize = 16;
+pub const GIB_OFF_POS_Z: usize = 20;

+pub const GIB_OFF_VEL_Y: usize = 28;

+/// One `Gib` pulping, emitted by `damage_resolution_system` on the
+/// `DispositionRung::Gib` terminal branch and drained once per Godot frame
+/// via `get_gib_events`. RENDER-ONLY (ADR-002 visual-physics checksum
+/// exclusion / ADR-027 §4): the Gib *decision* + the despawn are authoritative
+/// sim state (in the region checksum); THIS event is a one-shot render feed
+/// that never re-enters a sim system. `slot` + `tick` are exact ints so the
+/// render can reproduce the scatter; `seed` is computed sim-side (no
+/// Godot-side hash drift — lc-agy).
+#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
+pub struct GibEvent {
+    /// Stable, replicated [`BufferSlot`] of the pulped agent — NEVER recycled
+    /// on despawn, so it's the deterministic key (NOT a Bevy `Entity`, whose
+    /// bits depend on spawn/despawn order — lc-codex).
+    pub slot: u32,
+    /// Sim tick of the kill (truncated from the `SimTickCount` u64 — exact for
+    /// the seed + debugging; a u32 tick is ~2.2 years at 60 Hz).
+    pub tick: u32,
+    /// `gib_seed(slot, tick)` — the deterministic scatter seed (ADR-027 rule-2:
+    /// hashed sim facts, never a per-tick RNG draw).
+    pub seed: u32,
+    /// Absolute world kill position `(x, y, z)`, `z = floor*FLOOR_HEIGHT +
+    /// altitude`.
+    pub pos: [f32; 3],

+impl GibEvent {

+    /// `GIB_OFF_*` offsets. The GDScript renderer reads them back with
+    /// `PackedByteArray.decode_u32` / `decode_float`.
+    pub fn write_bytes(&self, out: &mut Vec<u8>) {
+        out.extend_from_slice(&self.slot.to_le_bytes());
+        out.extend_from_slice(&self.tick.to_le_bytes());
+        out.extend_from_slice(&self.seed.to_le_bytes());
+        out.extend_from_slice(&self.pos[0].to_le_bytes());
+        out.extend_from_slice(&self.pos[1].to_le_bytes());
+        out.extend_from_slice(&self.pos[2].to_le_bytes());
+        out.extend_from_slice(&self.vel[0].to_le_bytes());
+        out.extend_from_slice(&self.vel[1].to_le_bytes());
+        out.extend_from_slice(&self.vel[2].to_le_bytes());
+    }

+/// Deterministic per-gib scatter seed from stable sim facts (ADR-027 rule-2):
+/// a fixed integer hash, no RNG, no wall-clock. Two same-seed replays of the
+/// same kill produce the same `seed`, so the render-only debris scatter is
+/// reproducible without entering the ADR-013 region checksum.
+#[inline]
+pub fn gib_seed(slot: u32, tick: u32) -> u32 {

+    // adjacent (slot, tick) pairs decorrelate (neighboring gibs don't scatter

+    let mut z = (slot ^ tick.rotate_left(16)).wrapping_add(0x9E37_79B9);
+    z = (z ^ (z >> 16)).wrapping_mul(0x21F0_AAAD);
+    z = (z ^ (z >> 15)).wrapping_mul(0x735A_2D97);

+/// `get_gib_events` drain calls this; extracted free so the publish order +
+/// ABI layout are cargo-testable without the gdext singleton.
+pub fn pack_gib_events_sorted(mut events: Vec<GibEvent>) -> Vec<u8> {
+    events.sort_unstable_by_key(|e| (e.tick, e.slot));
+    let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(events.len() * GIB_EVENT_STRIDE);

diff --git a/.totem/specs/561.md b/.totem/specs/561.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..7573a54
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.totem/specs/561.md
@@ -0,0 +1,90 @@
+# Spec: Survivable dismemberment — ActiveStatuses substrate + per-limb hide + SeveredLimb debris + tactical locomotion, rev 2
+
+**Issue:** mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561 · **Epic:** mmnto-ai/liquid-city#565 (gore) · **Builds on:** mmnto-ai/liquid-city#559 (part-segmented LOD-0 mesh, shipped) + mmnto-ai/liquid-city#560 (gib-explode + `GibDebrisRenderer`, shipped) · **ADR:** ADR-027 §1 (`ActiveStatuses`/`Dismembered`, Accepted), §3 (legibility contract), Totem-rules 3 (insert-once-never-remove) + 7 (Rust-exported bit-constant parity) · ADR-029 P2b (render-state ABI, the documented stride-bump seam) · ADR-013 (determinism) · ADR-027 Totem-rule-2 (seeded, no per-tick RNG).
+
+> **Status — rev 2 (2026-06-23).** Scope set by operator (single-agent, "modify don't multiply"). **Cohort blind design-review folded** — lc-codex (`0818Z`), lc-gemini (`0815Z`), lc-agy (`0820Z`): unanimous ACCEPT of the settled forks (1 stride-bump, 2 minimal-substrate, 4 reuse-`GibDebrisRenderer`, 5 `SeveredLimbEvent`, 6 2-PR cut). Sharpenings folded into rev 2: **stride-bump touches ALL render-state consumers** (codex+agy 🔴), **torso excluded from survivable selection** (codex+agy 🔴), **`ActiveStatuses` default-at-spawn** (codex+agy), **mass-scaled severity not raw impulse** (codex), **one-part-per-tick + precedence + separate event + zero-fill test** (codex). **Adjudicated 2:1 OUT of PR-A:** agy's sharp-vs-blunt cause-multiplier (needs `DamageSourceKind` / a `DamageEvent` schema change this slice rules out) and full left/right directional locality (needs deliberate sim-state) → § Parked/Open. Line numbers verified at source; re-verify at impl time.
+
+## The design in one line
+A hit can take a **limb** instead of a life. The agent **keeps living** with the piece gone, and the missing piece **changes the threat** — sever the legs and a charging zombie drops to a slow ankle-grabbing crawl; take the head and it shambles blind (loses its target); take an arm and it can't grapple. Funny *and* tactical: the player reads the silhouette and makes a call. ADR-027's "make the damage state legible" applied to the player's eyes.
+
+## Design invariant (the budget guarantee): MODIFY, DON'T MULTIPLY
+Dismemberment **never adds an agent.** It mutates the existing agent's status + locomotion and spawns only **render-only, capped** debris. Agent count — the scarce frame resource for the multi-faction city sim — is **unchanged** by any amount of dismemberment. The "separated walking-legs / crawling-torso-half as their own creatures" gag (which *would* multiply agents) is **explicitly out** (§ Parked).
+
+## Problem
+The part-segmented rig (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#559) and the kinematic-MultiMesh debris system (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#560) shipped, and the Godot dismember seam is **in place but dormant**: `PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.gd` has `BIT_TO_PART`, `_apply_part_mask(pool_i, mask)` (a SET bit hides that limb), `reset_parts_visible()` — but production `_bind` only ever applies mask 0, because **the sim publishes no dismemberment state.** ADR-027 §1 ratified the substrate (`ActiveStatuses`/`Dismembered{parts:u8}`) but it does not exist in code. This slice fills that gap.
+
+## Verified grounding
+- **Render-state ABI** (`density_feedback.rs:595-621`): `RENDER_STATE_STRIDE = 4`; byte `[0]` flags (bit2 `LIVE_BIT`, bit4 `BALLISTIC_BIT`; bits 0/1/7 unused, **bits 3/5/6 reserved coarse gore flags**), `[1]` health, `[2]` hitstop, `[3]` intent. Doc-anticipated: *"Extending the record (e.g. a gore-segment byte) is a coordinated Rust+GDScript stride bump guarded by the buffer's parity test."*
+- **`RENDER_STATE_STRIDE` is mirrored across MANY GDScript consumers** (cohort 🔴): `HordeRenderAdapter.gd`, `PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.gd`, `SkeletalHordeAdapter.gd`, `LODManager3D.gd` + benches `horde_render_bench.gd`, `lod_manager_bench.gd`, `part_segmented_horde_bench.gd`, `skeletal_horde_bench.gd`. Each computes `ro = slot * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE` — **miss one and slot 1+ records misalign catastrophically.**
+- **`BIT_TO_PART`** (`PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.gd:67-69`, mirrors ADR-027 source-of-truth): `bit0 head, bit1 arm-left, bit2 arm-right, bit3 leg-left, bit4 leg-right, bit5 torso` (bits 6-7 reserved).
+- **Parity precedent:** `tests/anim_intent_parity.rs` + `tests/bit_protocol_parity.rs` read the GDScript consts via `include_str!` and assert they match Rust — the Totem-rule-7 mechanism.
+- **Dismemberment is NOT a `DispositionRung`** (`damage.rs` `select_disposition` selects lethality). It's a separate post-lattice rule → can apply to a *surviving* agent.
+- **No `DamageSourceKind` exists** — live producers all emit `DamageType::Physical`; `source: Entity` is attribution, not a content profile. Cause-gating (sharp vs blunt) is NOT expressible without a `DamageEvent` schema change → out of PR-A (§ Parked).
+- **Reuse target:** `GibDebrisRenderer` (capped 256-chunk kinematic MultiMesh + fade) — inherits the `_cumulative_scale` head-scale fix for free (agy).
+
+## Scope
+
+### PR-A — substrate + visible dismemberment (satisfies ADR-027 §3 *visual* legibility)
+**WILL**
+1. **Status substrate, default-installed at spawn** (`components.rs`): `Dismembered { parts: u8 }` + `ActiveStatuses { active_mask: u16, dismembered: Dismembered }` per ADR-027 §1, both `Copy`. **`ActiveStatuses::default()` is installed at agent SPAWN** (not lazily on first sever — lazy insert = archetype churn on the impact frame, and forces `Option<&mut>`/insert-and-mutate-same-tick; cohort). The resolver mutates a plain `&mut ActiveStatuses`. `ACTIVE_STATUS_DISMEMBERED_BIT = 1 << 4`; `Dismembered.parts` is meaningful only when that bit is set; once `parts` is nonzero the bit is set and the component is **never structurally removed** (Totem-rule-3). `Dismembered.parts` bit layout = Rust **single source of truth** (Totem-rule-7).
+2. **Cause→limb rule, resolver-side** (`damage.rs`) — a deterministic, post-lattice rule, **mass-scaled** (cohort/codex — a raw impulse threshold would make heavier agents easier to dismember, inverting the structural-resistance model):
+   ```text
+   specific_dv = sqrt(total_impulse_ns_sq) / target_mass_kg
+   severity    = specific_dv / BALLISTIC_REFERENCE_DELTA_V
+   eligible if: health.current > 0 (post-damage)  &&  !forced_terminal_despawn
+             && rung != Gib  &&  DISMEMBER_MIN_RATIO <= severity < GIB_MOMENTUM_RATIO
+   ```
+   (squared mass-scaled compares fine — the point is mass-scaling.) **Precedence** (codex): forced-despawn > lethal Gib > other-lethal death-routing > **survivable dismember** — a target is never both dead and survivably-dismembered the same tick. **One newly-severed part per target per tick** (the resolver already aggregates same-tick damage; one limb-pop per agent/tick is legible + event-testable). **v0 may be tuning-unreachable + forced-path-tested** (mirroring Gib); the operator's knob enables it. NO `DamageEvent` schema change; NO cause-kind inference from `source` entity types (would couple the pure resolver to producer ECS facts).
+3. **Limb selection — deterministic, weighted, degradation-aware, NON-TORSO** (`damage.rs`):
+   - **Eligible parts = head, arm-left, arm-right, leg-left, leg-right. TORSO IS EXCLUDED** (cohort 🔴 — hiding the torso on a living agent = floating head/limbs = a render-bug look + breaks legibility; bit5 stays published + parity-tested but is selected only by terminal/Gib paths, never survivable).
+   - Filter already-severed parts (`!Dismembered.parts`); if exhausted, no-op (no duplicate event for an absent part).
+   - Base weights: head rare (~0.05), arms/legs the bulk. A **light verticality bias** from `impulse.z` is allowed (high-loft → legs, flat → arms). **No left/right locality in PR-A** (2:1 — needs a deliberate target-facing basis; render heading is wrong to pull into the resolver, `Velocity` has a zero-vel fallback problem; deferred — § Open).
+   - Deterministic roll: `dismember_seed(slot, tick)` SplitMix (no per-tick RNG; Totem-rule-2) → scale across the summed weights of *remaining* eligible parts → pick.
+4. **ABI stride bump 4→5 — ALL consumers** (`density_feedback.rs` + every GDScript mirror listed in § Verified grounding): add byte `[4]` = `Dismembered.parts`, published each tick from the authoritative status; **keep the existing whole-buffer zero-fill before write** so recycled/dead slots can't retain a stale sever mask. **Every adapter/manager/bench that computes a record offset moves to 5** — a single missed mirror drifts all slots. **Parity test** (`tests/render_state_parity.rs`) scans **ALL production consumers** (not one adapter) — assert `RENDER_STATE_STRIDE`, the `RS_*` offsets, and `BIT_TO_PART` match Rust↔GDScript (Totem-rule-7). *(Strongly consider centralizing the GDScript ABI consts in one file so the mirrors can't diverge — codex.)*
+5. **Wire the dormant seam** (`PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.gd`): `_bind` reads byte `[4]` → `_apply_part_mask(pool_i, parts)`; `reset_parts_visible` stays the recycle leak-guard.
+6. **`SeveredLimb` debris (render-only) — reuse `GibDebrisRenderer` MACHINERY, NOT `GibEvent` semantics** (codex): a **separate** one-shot `SeveredLimbEvent { slot, tick, seed, pos, vel, part: u8 }` (exact-int packed, sorted by `(tick, slot, part)`, drain-on-read) → a renderer path that spawns **ONE** part chunk (not a 6-part burst; don't overload the 36-byte `GibEvent`, which means "pulp, all parts"). Ballistic arc from the sever impulse, capped + time-faded. Shares the 256-chunk pool (or a small dedicated sub-cap, e.g. 64); oldest-first global retirement is fine (render-only). **Excluded from checksum.**
+7. **Verify:** cargo tests — mass-scaled eligibility (heavier agent ≠ easier), deterministic limb selection + same-seed reproducibility, **torso never selected for survivable**, ABI/parity across all consumers, **byte[4] zero-fill on recycle/gap**, `ActiveStatuses` default-at-spawn + in-place mutation (no insert), one-part-per-tick, precedence (kill-plane/Gib/lethal unaffected), drain-on-read, event order stable. Forced-path Godot bench + a **viewed PNG** of a de-limbed, correctly-hidden agent + one flying `SeveredLimb`.
+
+### PR-B — tactical locomotion modes (the "mean it" layer, rides PR-A)
+**WILL** map the parts mask → a behavior mode (deterministic, in-checksum; `SpeedMultiplier` exists from mmnto-ai/liquid-city#557):
+
+| Missing | Mode | Tactical read |
+|---|---|---|
+| both legs | **crawl** — speed-floored, low, still ankle-grabs | charging threat → slow + low; step over / kite |
+| head | **blind shamble** — loses/never-acquires target; wanders | de-aggro tool; headless-still-coming gag |
+| arm(s) | **can't grapple/attack** that side; still shambles | defanged; safe to pass |
+| one leg | **hobble** — speed-reduced | easy to outrun |
+
+Armless-still-shambles/bites is free (a mask-read in the attack gate). **WILL verify** determinism (two same-seed runs bit-identical) + a perceptual capture per mode.
+
+### WILL NOT (either PR)
+- **Spawn separated partial-agents** (walking legs / crawling torso-halves) — violates modify-don't-multiply; § Parked.
+- Select the **torso** for survivable dismemberment; build the full `ActiveStatuses` (`fire/frost/poison/crippled`); add `DamageSourceKind` / cause-kind gating; change `DamageEvent` schema, the Gib/kill-plane/corpse-arc branches, or non-vehicle/non-melee producers.
+- Per-tick RNG, wall-clock, or any non-carried-state input (ADR-013 / Totem-rule-2).
+
+## Determinism & legibility (cohort-confirmed)
+- **Authoritative (in-checksum):** `active_mask`, `Dismembered.parts`, the seeded mass-scaled selection, PR-B locomotion. Seed = `dismember_seed(slot, tick)` SplitMix.
+- **Render-only (excluded):** the `SeveredLimbEvent` bytes + the flying-limb/hidden-node visual. Absent FFI resource must not change sim state; two same-seed worlds match with/without the render event buffer (the `GibEvent` lessons: exact-int packing, sorted publish order, drain-on-read, no Godot hash drift).
+- **Legibility (ADR-027 §3):** PR-A satisfies **visual** legibility (the missing-limb silhouette + flying `SeveredLimb` *are* the readable state); **PR-B is where the status becomes tactically honest** — keep that distinction crisp in the PR-A body (codex).
+
+## Performance budget (the sanity anchor)
+- **Zero new agents** → city-sim agent budget untouched.
+- Substrate: `ActiveStatuses` `Copy`, default-installed at spawn → no runtime archetype migration.
+- ABI: +1 byte/slot (stride 4→5) on a small CPU buffer → frame-noise.
+- Locomotion: one mask-read branch per dismembered agent/tick → noise vs flocking.
+- Debris: shares the capped 256-chunk `GibDebrisRenderer` pool (or a 64-chunk sub-cap) → fixed, agent-count-independent.
+
+## Parked — captured, fenced, NOT this slice
+- **Separated walking-legs / crawling-torso-halves as independent creatures** — the only version that *multiplies* agents. Revisit only if it earns the slots.
+- **Sharp-vs-blunt cause-multiplier** (agy: blades sever readily, vehicle bumps rarely, status ticks never) — a genuinely nice *feel* knob, but needs a `DamageSourceKind` / damage-source profile (a `DamageEvent` schema change). Gated on damage-profiles + enemy-variety mmnto-ai/liquid-city#562 / mmnto-ai/liquid-city#563 / mmnto-ai/liquid-city#564. **Flagged to operator** as a future feel choice.
+- **Full left/right directional locality** — needs a deliberate target-facing basis as sim state; PR-A uses verticality-biased seeded selection only.
+- **Throw-spare-parts / picks-up-spare-parts zombies** — special archetypes, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#603 / mmnto-ai/liquid-city#604.
+- **Crawl/walk comedy at depth** — mmnto-ai/liquid-city#604. **Body piles / persistent limbs** — mmnto-ai/liquid-city#464.
+
+## Open (post-cohort; minor)
+- **Eligibility band tuning** (`DISMEMBER_MIN_RATIO`): a usable vehicle band exists at current constants without a balance retune (codex — top-speed is lethal, moderately-high speed is sub-lethal but above Launch); v0 stays forced-path-verified unless the operator opts to tune live.
+- **Debris cap policy:** shared 256 pool vs a dedicated `SeveredLimb` sub-cap (~64).
+- **GDScript ABI-const centralization** (one consts file vs per-adapter mirrors guarded by the parity scan).
+
+## PR cut
+Two PRs: **PR-A** (substrate + ABI-all-consumers + mass-scaled cause-rule + non-torso selection + hide + one-part `SeveredLimb` debris — the visible slice; satisfies *visual* legibility; mirrors the gib PR shape) → **PR-B** (tactical locomotion — the *tactical* layer). Each independently reviewable; PR-A shippable alone.

+const SEVERED_LIMB_EVENT_STRIDE := 37
+const SL_OFF_SLOT := 0  # u32 — replicated BufferSlot (unused here; debug)
+const SL_OFF_TICK := 4  # u32 — sim tick of the sever (unused here; debug)

@@ -73,6 +97,11 @@ var _ready_done := false

+# per event — a survivable dismemberment (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561), NOT the 6-part
+# gib pulp. Called each frame by sim_driver with sim.get_severed_limb_events().
+# Same not-ready / headless / empty guards as ingest() (the harness never touches

+func ingest_severed(events: PackedByteArray) -> void:

-		)

+# Seed-driven ballistic scatter for ONE chunk: jitter the bias direction, loft it,
+# pick a tumble, and add the chunk to the pool. `chunk_seed` FULLY determines the
+# scatter via a LOCAL RNG (never the global randf()/randi()). Shared by the gib
+# burst (6 chunks, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#560) and a single severed limb
+# (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561) so both use identical arc math.
+func _scatter_chunk(chunk_seed: int, part: int, pos: Vector3, spray_dir: Vector3) -> void:
+	_rng.seed = chunk_seed
+	# Scatter cone: jitter the bias direction, then loft. randf_range draws are
+	# deterministic for a fixed _rng.seed (no global RNG touched).
+	var jitter := Vector3(
+		_rng.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0), _rng.randf_range(-0.2, 1.0), _rng.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0)
+	)
+	var dir := (spray_dir + jitter * 0.6).normalized()

+	var loft := Vector3.UP * gib_scatter_speed * gib_loft_mult * _rng.randf_range(0.4, 1.0)
+	var vel := dir * speed + loft
+	var axis := Vector3(
+		_rng.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0), _rng.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0), _rng.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0)
+	)
+	axis = axis.normalized() if axis.length() > 0.001 else Vector3.UP
+	var spin := _rng.randf_range(6.0, 18.0)  # rad/s tumble
+	_add_chunk(part, pos, vel, axis, spin)
+
+
+# Spawn ONE flying limb chunk for a survivable dismemberment
+# (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561). Unlike a gib (the whole body pulps into 6 chunks), a
+# dismemberment loses a SINGLE limb — the trailing `part` byte (an ADR-027
+# Dismembered.parts BIT) selects which, resolved to a mesh via _bit_to_mesh. Same
+# seed-driven ballistic arc as a gib chunk, biased downrange by the sever impulse.
+func _spawn_severed_limb(seed: int, sever_pos: Vector3, sever_vel: Vector3, part_bit: int) -> void:
+	# part_bit is a decode_u8 (0..255), so only the upper bound can exclude — the
+	# reserved bits 6-7 fall through here (CR mmnto-ai/liquid-city#609).
+	if part_bit >= PART_COUNT:
+		return
+	var mesh_idx := _bit_to_mesh[part_bit]
+	# Skip if this kit doesn't carry that limb mesh (e.g. a future accessory set).
+	if mesh_idx < 0 or _part_meshes[mesh_idx] == null:
+		return
+	var spray_dir := sever_vel.normalized() if sever_vel.length() > 0.001 else Vector3.UP
+	_scatter_chunk(seed, mesh_idx, sever_pos, spray_dir)

 func _begin_sweep_n() -> void:
diff --git a/apps/game-godot/severed_limb_bench.gd b/apps/game-godot/severed_limb_bench.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..a6c3259
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/severed_limb_bench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,332 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 gore epic mmnto-ai/liquid-city#565, PR-A (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561) —
+# survivable-dismemberment render bench. REAL GPU (headless asserts the no-op + quits
+# clean). PHASE A (gates exit): the production byte[4] Dismembered.parts path hides
+# ONLY the severed limb on a rendered PartSegmentedHordeAdapter rig; one synthetic
+# SeveredLimbEvent spawns exactly ONE GibDebrisRenderer chunk (NOT the 6-part gib
+# burst); the bit->mesh map resolves by name (arm-left bit -> the arm-left mesh); an
+# out-of-range part spawns nothing. PHASE B (reports): a gameplay-camera PNG of a
+# de-limbed agent + its airborne limb chunk — read the PNG (the cohort's acceptance
+# gate; legibility is a FEEL claim, evidenced at the player's frame, not theorized;
+# PR2 caught a 10x head-scale bug only by VIEWING).
+#
+# Synthetic bytes stand in for sim.get_severed_limb_events(): the dismember band is
+# largely forced-path at v0 (operator's DISMEMBER_MIN_RATIO knob), so the RENDER path
+# is proven here from hand-built ABI bytes and the sim->event path by the damage.rs
+# cargo tests. Same 37-byte ABI as the producer.
+# Run: <godot> --path apps/game-godot res://severed_limb_bench.tscn
+#   0 = correctness clean, 1 = assertion failed, 2 = watchdog.
+
+# SeveredLimbEvent ABI mirror (damage.rs SL_*; severed_limb_event_parity.rs locks it).
+const SEVERED_LIMB_EVENT_STRIDE := 37
+const SL_OFF_SLOT := 0
+const SL_OFF_TICK := 4
+const SL_OFF_SEED := 8
+const SL_OFF_POS_X := 12
+const SL_OFF_POS_Y := 16
+const SL_OFF_POS_Z := 20
+const SL_OFF_VEL_X := 24
+const SL_OFF_VEL_Y := 28
+const SL_OFF_VEL_Z := 32
+const SL_OFF_PART := 36
+
+# render-state record layout (mirror of the ABI; render_state_parity.rs locks it).
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 5
+const RS_FLAGS := 0
+const RS_HEALTH := 1
+const RS_HITSTOP := 2
+const RS_INTENT := 3
+const RS_PARTS := 4
+const LIVE_BIT := 0x04
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const TIER_SKEL := 2
+const PART_COUNT := 6
+# ADR-027 Dismembered.parts bit the hero loses (bit0 head .. bit5 torso).
+const DISMEMBER_BIT_ARM_LEFT := 1
+
+const OUT_DIR := "user://severed_limb_bench/"
+const NC := 3  # rendered agents
+const HERO := 1  # the de-limbed agent (center)
+const CAP := 4  # adapter pool (>= NC)
+const SPACING := 2.6
+const FRAMES_C := 4  # frames before the correctness asserts
+const DELTA_C := 0.1
+const CAPTURE_AFTER_S := 0.32  # catch the limb mid-arc (airborne, not yet too high)
+
+var _adapter: PartSegmentedHordeAdapter
+var _gibs: GibDebrisRenderer
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _book: SlotClockBook
+var _xf := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _rs := PackedByteArray()
+var _tiers := PackedByteArray()
+
+var _phase := 0  # 0 = correctness, 1 = perceptual
+var _frame := 0
+var _elapsed := 0.0
+var _fails := 0
+var _watchdog := 0
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless":
+		# Headless: both renderers build no GPU nodes and their ingest paths no-op.
+		var book := SlotClockBook.new()
+		var a := PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.new(self, true, CAP, book)
+		a.setup()
+		a.set_active(true)
+		var g := GibDebrisRenderer.new()
+		add_child(g)
+		g.setup(true)
+		g.ingest_severed(
+			_severed_bytes(0, 0x1234, Vector3(0, 0, 1), Vector3(0, 0, 5), DISMEMBER_BIT_ARM_LEFT)
+		)
+		var ok := not a.is_active() and a.last_live == 0 and g.debug_status() == "Gibs: off"
+		print(
+			"[sever-bench] headless — renderers no-op %s; quit 0" % ("OK" if ok else "UNEXPECTED")
+		)
+		get_tree().quit(0)
+		return
+
+	DisplayServer.window_set_vsync_mode(DisplayServer.VSYNC_DISABLED)
+	Engine.max_fps = 144  # cap fps so the time-based capture stays well under the watchdog
+	_cam = Camera3D.new()
+	_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_PERSPECTIVE
+	_cam.fov = 55.0
+	_cam.current = true
+	add_child(_cam)
+	_add_scene()
+
+	_book = SlotClockBook.new()
+	_adapter = PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.new(self, false, CAP, _book)
+	if not _adapter.setup():
+		print("[sever-bench] FAIL: adapter setup returned false (see part-name push_error)")
+		get_tree().quit(1)
+		return
+	_adapter.set_active(true)
+	_gibs = GibDebrisRenderer.new()
+	add_child(_gibs)
+	_gibs.setup(false)
+	# Suppress blood during the correctness severs (several fire) — re-enabled for ONE
+	# clean puff at the photo seed, so the capture isn't a blood cloud (in-game a single
+	# sever pops one puff; the bench's _verify fires three).
+	_gibs.gib_blood_enabled = false
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(OUT_DIR))
+	_build_buffers()
+	_frame_camera()
+	print(
+		"[sever-bench] start; adapter=%s gibs=%s" % [_adapter.debug_status(), _gibs.debug_status()]
+	)
+
+
+func _process(delta: float) -> void:
+	if _adapter == null:
+		return
+	# Hard watchdog: a self-quitting bench must never hang a window.
+	_watchdog += 1
+	if _watchdog > 1200:
+		push_error("[sever-bench] watchdog tripped — force quit")
+		get_tree().quit(2)
+		return
+	if _phase == 0:
+		_process_correctness()
+	elif _phase == 1:
+		_process_perceptual(delta)
+
+
+func _process_correctness() -> void:
+	_advance_clocks(DELTA_C)
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, NC, _tiers)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame < FRAMES_C:
+		return
+	_verify()
+	# Seed the carnage to photograph: ONE clean airborne limb + one blood puff. Arc it
+	# up + toward the camera (sim +y -> Godot +z) + slightly left so the severed arm
+	# separates from the figure cluster into open space and reads clearly.
+	_gibs.clear_all()
+	_gibs.gib_blood_enabled = true
+	var hero_pos := Vector3(float(HERO) * SPACING, 0.0, 1.2)  # sim x, y, height
+	_gibs.ingest_severed(
+		_severed_bytes(HERO, 0xA17C, hero_pos, Vector3(-1.5, 3.0, 4.0), DISMEMBER_BIT_ARM_LEFT)
+	)
+	_phase = 1
+	_elapsed = 0.0
+
+
+func _verify() -> void:
+	# 1) Production hide path: the hero's byte[4] arm-left bit, applied by update() ->
+	#    _apply_part_mask, hides ONLY that limb on the rendered rig.
+	var hp := _pool_index_of(HERO)
+	if hp >= 0:
+		var by_bit: Array = _adapter._part_by_bit[hp]
+		var hidden := 1 << DISMEMBER_BIT_ARM_LEFT
+		var hide_ok := true
+		for bit in PART_COUNT:
+			if by_bit[bit].visible != ((hidden & (1 << bit)) == 0):
+				hide_ok = false
+		_check(hide_ok, "byte[4] hides ONLY arm-left on the rendered hero rig", "mask mismatch")
+	else:
+		_check(false, "hero rig bound", "no pool index for hero")
+
+	# 2) One SeveredLimbEvent -> exactly ONE chunk (NOT the 6-part gib burst).
+	_gibs.clear_all()
+	var hero_pos := Vector3(float(HERO) * SPACING, 0.0, 1.1)
+	_gibs.ingest_severed(
+		_severed_bytes(HERO, 0xBEEF, hero_pos, Vector3(1.5, 0.0, 5.0), DISMEMBER_BIT_ARM_LEFT)
+	)
+	_check(
+		_gibs._active_count == 1, "one sever -> ONE chunk (not 6)", "got %d" % _gibs._active_count
+	)
+
+	# 3) bit -> mesh resolves by name: the chunk draws the arm-left mesh.
+	var slot := _active_chunk_slot()
+	var want: int = _gibs._bit_to_mesh[DISMEMBER_BIT_ARM_LEFT]
+	_check(want >= 0, "arm-left bit resolves to a mesh slot", "bit_to_mesh = %d" % want)
+	if slot >= 0:
+		_check(
+			_gibs._chunk_part[slot] == want,
+			"severed chunk uses the arm-left mesh",
+			"chunk part=%d want=%d" % [_gibs._chunk_part[slot], want]
+		)
+	else:
+		_check(false, "severed chunk is active", "no active chunk slot")
+
+	# 4) Out-of-range part bit -> no chunk (the guard holds).
+	_gibs.ingest_severed(_severed_bytes(HERO, 0x1, hero_pos, Vector3(1, 0, 5), 7))
+	_check(
+		_gibs._active_count == 1, "invalid part bit spawns no chunk", "got %d" % _gibs._active_count
+	)
+
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print("[sever-bench] PHASE A: PASS")
+	else:
+		print("[sever-bench] PHASE A: FAIL (%d assertion(s))" % _fails)
+
+
+func _process_perceptual(delta: float) -> void:
+	# Keep rendering the de-limbed rig; let the limb chunk arc up for CAPTURE_AFTER_S
+	# (elapsed time, robust under vsync-off) so it reads as airborne carnage.
+	_advance_clocks(delta)
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, NC, _tiers)
+	_elapsed += delta
+	if _elapsed < CAPTURE_AFTER_S:
+		return
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img != null:
+		var path := OUT_DIR + "perceptual_dismember.png"
+		img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+		print(
+			"[sever-bench] perceptual capture %s (%d chunk airborne)" % [path, _gibs._active_count]
+		)
+	_finalise()
+
+
+func _finalise() -> void:
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print("[sever-bench] VERDICT: GO — dismember render path verified; perceptual PNG captured")
+	else:
+		print("[sever-bench] VERDICT: FAIL — %d assertion(s)" % _fails)
+	get_tree().quit(0 if _fails == 0 else 1)
+
+
+# NC live agents in a short row along sim-x; the hero (center) carries the arm-left
+# Dismembered.parts bit in byte[4] so the production update() path hides that limb.
+func _build_buffers() -> void:
+	_xf = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_rs = PackedByteArray()
+	_xf.resize(NC * 8)
+	_rs.resize(NC * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE)
+	for i in NC:
+		var o := i * 8
+		# Identity basis (heading 0) -> rigs face their default forward; framed 3/4.
+		_xf[o + 0] = 1.0
+		_xf[o + 3] = float(i) * SPACING  # sim x
+		_xf[o + 5] = 1.0
+		_xf[o + 7] = 0.0  # sim y
+		var ro := i * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		_rs[ro + RS_FLAGS] = LIVE_BIT
+		_rs[ro + RS_HEALTH] = 220
+		_rs[ro + RS_HITSTOP] = 0
+		_rs[ro + RS_INTENT] = ANIM_LOCOMOTION
+		_rs[ro + RS_PARTS] = 0
+	_rs[HERO * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE + RS_PARTS] = 1 << DISMEMBER_BIT_ARM_LEFT
+	_tiers.resize(NC)
+	for i in NC:
+		_tiers[i] = TIER_SKEL
+
+
+# Advance the shared clock once per live slot, exactly as LODManager3D does (the
+# adapter is a pure reader).
+func _advance_clocks(delta: float) -> void:
+	_book.ensure_capacity(NC)
+	for s in NC:
+		_book.advance(s, delta, _rs[s * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE + RS_HITSTOP])
+
+
+# Gameplay-distance 3/4 front framing of the hero + its airborne limb.
+func _frame_camera() -> void:
+	var mid := float(HERO) * SPACING
+	# Pulled back + raised, aimed up at the arc so the figures sit low and the airborne
+	# limb has clear sky above the hero.
+	_cam.position = Vector3(mid + 1.0, 3.2, 7.5)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(mid - 0.6, 2.0, 0.0), Vector3.UP)
+
+
+# One SeveredLimbEvent record (37 LE bytes), mirroring the damage.rs producer. pos/vel
+# are sim (x, y, height-z); the renderer maps to Godot (x, z, y).
+func _severed_bytes(
+	slot: int, seed_v: int, pos: Vector3, vel: Vector3, part_bit: int
+) -> PackedByteArray:
+	var b := PackedByteArray()
+	b.resize(SEVERED_LIMB_EVENT_STRIDE)
+	b.encode_u32(SL_OFF_SLOT, slot & 0xFFFFFFFF)
+	b.encode_u32(SL_OFF_TICK, 0)
+	b.encode_u32(SL_OFF_SEED, seed_v & 0xFFFFFFFF)
+	b.encode_float(SL_OFF_POS_X, pos.x)
+	b.encode_float(SL_OFF_POS_Y, pos.y)
+	b.encode_float(SL_OFF_POS_Z, pos.z)
+	b.encode_float(SL_OFF_VEL_X, vel.x)
+	b.encode_float(SL_OFF_VEL_Y, vel.y)
+	b.encode_float(SL_OFF_VEL_Z, vel.z)
+	b.encode_u8(SL_OFF_PART, part_bit & 0xFF)
+	return b
+
+
+# A dim ground plane + a sun so the rigs + airborne limb read against the floor in the
+# perceptual capture (bench prop, NOT the game's lighting — that is mmnto-ai/liquid-city#586).
+func _add_scene() -> void:
+	var ground := MeshInstance3D.new()
+	var pm := PlaneMesh.new()
+	pm.size = Vector2(40.0, 40.0)
+	ground.mesh = pm
+	var mat := StandardMaterial3D.new()
+	mat.albedo_color = Color(0.16, 0.17, 0.19)
+	ground.material_override = mat
+	add_child(ground)
+	var sun := DirectionalLight3D.new()
+	sun.rotation_degrees = Vector3(-55.0, -35.0, 0.0)
+	add_child(sun)
+
+
+func _check(ok: bool, label: String, detail: String) -> void:
+	if ok:
+		print("[sever-bench] PASS: %s" % label)
+	else:
+		_fails += 1
+		print("[sever-bench] FAIL: %s — %s" % [label, detail])
+
+
+func _pool_index_of(slot: int) -> int:
+	for i in _adapter._pool_slot.size():
+		if _adapter._pool_slot[i] == slot:
+			return i
+	return -1
+
+
+func _active_chunk_slot() -> int:
+	for i in _gibs._chunk_age.size():
+		if _gibs._chunk_age[i] >= 0.0:
+			return i
+	return -1

+/// Pick a limb to sever on a SURVIVING, sub-Gib, high-energy hit (ADR-027 §1,

+/// and Gib never both fire. **TORSO is never selected** on a living agent (a
+/// floating head/limbs silhouette reads as a render bug — cohort). Deterministic:
+/// a seeded weighted pick over the present limbs, head deliberately rare, biased
+/// toward legs on a high-loft hit and arms on a flat one.
+fn select_dismember_part(
+    total_impulse_ns_sq: f32,
+    target_mass_kg: f32,
+    current_parts: u8,
+    seed: u32,
+    total_impulse_ns: [f32; 3],

+    if !total_impulse_ns_sq.is_finite() || !target_mass_kg.is_finite() || target_mass_kg <= 0.0 {
+        return None;
+    }
+    let band_lo = target_mass_kg * BALLISTIC_REFERENCE_DELTA_V * DISMEMBER_MIN_RATIO;
+    let gib_bound = target_mass_kg * BALLISTIC_REFERENCE_DELTA_V * GIB_MOMENTUM_RATIO;
+    if total_impulse_ns_sq < band_lo * band_lo || total_impulse_ns_sq >= gib_bound * gib_bound {
+        return None;
+    }
+    // Verticality bias from the hit direction (a high-loft hood-clip favors legs;
+    // a flat hit favors arms). Cheap, deterministic, no left/right locality (v0).
+    let mag = total_impulse_ns_sq.sqrt();

+    let arm_mult = if vert < 0.3 { 3 } else { 2 };

+    let candidates: [(u8, u32); 5] = [

+        (DISMEMBER_BIT_ARM_LEFT, 3 * arm_mult),
+        (DISMEMBER_BIT_ARM_RIGHT, 3 * arm_mult),
+        (DISMEMBER_BIT_LEG_LEFT, 3 * leg_mult),
+        (DISMEMBER_BIT_LEG_RIGHT, 3 * leg_mult),
