+    use crate::components::{
+        BallisticMode, DeadTag, FactionTag, Velocity, WorldPosition, DISMEMBER_BIT_TORSO,
+    };
     use crate::tuning_stub::{
         BALLISTIC_THRESHOLD, DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG, DEFAULT_HEALTH_MAX, DEFAULT_POISE_MAX, GRAVITY,
         VEHICLE_AGENT_KNOCKBACK_DELTA_V_AT_MAX_SPEED, VEHICLE_LOFT_ARCHETYPE_MULT,
@@ -2852,4 +3095,251 @@ mod tests {
         };
         assert_eq!(run(), run());
     }
+
+    // ----- Survivable dismemberment (ADR-027 §1, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561) -----
+
+    /// `new_damage_world` + the render-only `SeveredLimbEventBuffer` so the
+    /// resolver's `Option<Res<SeveredLimbEventBuffer>>` append path is exercised.
+    fn new_dismember_world() -> World {
+        let mut world = new_damage_world();
+        world.init_resource::<SeveredLimbEventBuffer>();
+        world
+    }
+
+    /// A dismemberable agent: a slot-carrying LOD-1 agent with a default
+    /// `ActiveStatuses` (the resolver mutates it in place) at the given mass.
+    fn spawn_dismemberable_agent(world: &mut World, slot: u32, mass_kg: f32) -> Entity {
+        let e = spawn_lod1_agent_with_slot(world, slot);
+        world
+            .entity_mut(e)
+            .insert((ActiveStatuses::default(), Mass::new(mass_kg)));
+        e
+    }
+
+    /// Clone of the severed-limb events the resolver appended this run.
+    fn severed_events(world: &World) -> Vec<SeveredLimbEvent> {
+        world
+            .resource::<SeveredLimbEventBuffer>()
+            .0
+            .read()
+            .expect("severed buffer lock")
+            .clone()
+    }
+
+    /// A mid-band per-kg severity (ratio 4.0 ∈ `[DISMEMBER_MIN_RATIO=3, GIB=5)`).
+    fn dismember_mid_band_ns(mass_kg: f32) -> f32 {
+        mass_kg * BALLISTIC_REFERENCE_DELTA_V * 4.0
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn surviving_in_band_hit_severs_one_nontorso_limb_and_emits_event() {
+        let mut world = new_dismember_world();
+        let agent = spawn_dismemberable_agent(&mut world, 4, DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG);
+        // Low damage (survives) + an in-band impulse.
+        send_event(
+            &mut world,
+            event_with_impulse(
+                agent,
+                1.0,
+                dismember_mid_band_ns(DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG),
+                [1.0, 0.0, 0.0],
+            ),
+        );
+        run_resolver(&mut world);
+        assert!(
+            world.get_entity(agent).is_ok(),
+            "in-band non-lethal hit must NOT despawn"
+        );
+        let status = *world.get::<ActiveStatuses>(agent).expect("status present");
+        assert_ne!(
+            status.active_mask & ACTIVE_STATUS_DISMEMBERED_BIT,
+            0,
+            "dismembered bit set"
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            status.dismembered.parts.count_ones(),
+            1,
+            "exactly one limb severed"
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            status.dismembered.parts & (1 << DISMEMBER_BIT_TORSO),
+            0,
+            "torso never severed on a living agent"
+        );
+        let events = severed_events(&world);
+        assert_eq!(events.len(), 1, "one severed-limb event");
+        assert_eq!(events[0].slot, 4);
+        assert_ne!(
+            status.dismembered.parts & (1 << events[0].part),
+            0,
+            "event part matches the set status bit"
+        );
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn dismember_severity_is_mass_scaled_heavier_is_harder() {
+        // codex: a RAW impulse threshold would make heavier agents EASIER to
+        // dismember. With the mass-scaled band, the SAME absolute impulse that
+        // dismembers a default-mass agent (severity 4.0) is BELOW the band for a
+        // 2x agent (severity 2.0 < DISMEMBER_MIN_RATIO=3) → no dismember.
+        let imp = dismember_mid_band_ns(DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG);
+        let mut world = new_dismember_world();
+        let light = spawn_dismemberable_agent(&mut world, 1, DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG);
+        let heavy = spawn_dismemberable_agent(&mut world, 2, DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG * 2.0);
+        send_event(
+            &mut world,
+            event_with_impulse(light, 1.0, imp, [1.0, 0.0, 0.0]),
+        );
+        send_event(
+            &mut world,
+            event_with_impulse(heavy, 1.0, imp, [1.0, 0.0, 0.0]),
+        );
+        run_resolver(&mut world);
+        assert_ne!(
+            world
+                .get::<ActiveStatuses>(light)
+                .unwrap()
+                .dismembered
+                .parts,
+            0,
+            "default-mass agent (severity 4.0) dismembers"
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            world
+                .get::<ActiveStatuses>(heavy)
+                .unwrap()
+                .dismembered
+                .parts,
+            0,
+            "2x-mass agent (severity 2.0) is below the band — heavier is harder, not easier"
+        );
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn dismember_part_is_deterministic_for_a_fixed_seed() {
+        let pick = |slot: u32| -> u8 {
+            let mut world = new_dismember_world();
+            let a = spawn_dismemberable_agent(&mut world, slot, DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG);
+            send_event(
+                &mut world,
+                event_with_impulse(
+                    a,
+                    1.0,
+                    dismember_mid_band_ns(DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG),
+                    [1.0, 0.0, 0.0],
+                ),
+            );
+            run_resolver(&mut world);
+            severed_events(&world)[0].part
+        };
+        assert_eq!(pick(7), pick(7), "same (slot, tick) → same limb");
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn torso_is_never_selected_for_survivable_dismember() {
+        // Sweep many seeds (slots) with a high-loft hit — torso must never win.
+        for slot in 0..64u32 {
+            let mut world = new_dismember_world();
+            let a = spawn_dismemberable_agent(&mut world, slot, DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG);
+            send_event(
+                &mut world,
+                event_with_impulse(
+                    a,
+                    1.0,
+                    dismember_mid_band_ns(DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG),
+                    [0.3, 0.0, 0.95],
+                ),
+            );
+            run_resolver(&mut world);
+            let parts = world.get::<ActiveStatuses>(a).unwrap().dismembered.parts;
+            assert_eq!(
+                parts & (1 << DISMEMBER_BIT_TORSO),
+                0,
+                "slot {slot}: torso never severed"
+            );
+        }
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn below_band_hit_does_not_dismember() {
+        let mut world = new_dismember_world();
+        let a = spawn_dismemberable_agent(&mut world, 5, DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG);
+        // ratio 2.0 < DISMEMBER_MIN_RATIO=3.0.
+        let below = DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG * BALLISTIC_REFERENCE_DELTA_V * 2.0;
+        send_event(
+            &mut world,
+            event_with_impulse(a, 1.0, below, [1.0, 0.0, 0.0]),
+        );
+        run_resolver(&mut world);
+        assert_eq!(
+            world.get::<ActiveStatuses>(a).unwrap().dismembered.parts,
+            0,
+            "below-band hit does not sever"
+        );
+        assert!(severed_events(&world).is_empty(), "no event below the band");
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn lethal_gib_takes_precedence_over_survivable_dismember() {
+        // A LETHAL Gib-rung hit pulps + despawns; it must NOT also emit a
+        // survivable-dismember event (precedence: dead → no survivable sever).
+        let mut world = new_dismember_world();
+        world.init_resource::<GibEventBuffer>();
+        let a = spawn_dismemberable_agent(&mut world, 6, DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG);
+        let gib_mag = gib_bound_ns(DEFAULT_AGENT_MASS_KG) * 1.1;
+        send_event(
+            &mut world,
+            event_with_impulse(a, 999.0, gib_mag, [1.0, 0.0, 0.0]),
+        );
+        run_resolver(&mut world);
+        assert!(world.get_entity(a).is_err(), "lethal Gib despawns");
+        assert!(
+            severed_events(&world).is_empty(),
+            "no severed-limb event on a Gib kill"
+        );
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn dismember_seed_is_deterministic_and_domain_separated_from_gib() {
+        assert_eq!(dismember_seed(5, 9), dismember_seed(5, 9), "deterministic");
+        assert_ne!(dismember_seed(5, 9), dismember_seed(6, 9), "varies by slot");
+        assert_ne!(
+            dismember_seed(5, 9),
+            dismember_seed(5, 10),
+            "varies by tick"
+        );
+        assert_ne!(
+            dismember_seed(5, 9),
+            gib_seed(5, 9),
+            "domain-separated from gib_seed"
+        );
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn severed_limb_event_abi_layout_is_stable() {
+        let e = SeveredLimbEvent {
+            slot: 0x0102_0304,
+            tick: 7,
+            seed: 0xAABB_CCDD,
+            pos: [1.0, 2.0, 3.0],
+            vel: [4.0, 5.0, 6.0],
+            part: DISMEMBER_BIT_LEG_LEFT,
+        };
+        let bytes = pack_severed_limb_events_sorted(vec![e]);
+        assert_eq!(bytes.len(), SEVERED_LIMB_EVENT_STRIDE);
+        assert_eq!(
+            &bytes[SL_OFF_SLOT..SL_OFF_SLOT + 4],
+            &0x0102_0304u32.to_le_bytes()
+        );
+        assert_eq!(&bytes[SL_OFF_TICK..SL_OFF_TICK + 4], &7u32.to_le_bytes());
+        assert_eq!(
+            &bytes[SL_OFF_POS_X..SL_OFF_POS_X + 4],
+            &1.0f32.to_le_bytes()
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            &bytes[SL_OFF_VEL_Z..SL_OFF_VEL_Z + 4],
+            &6.0f32.to_le_bytes()
+        );
+        assert_eq!(bytes[SL_OFF_PART], DISMEMBER_BIT_LEG_LEFT);
+    }
 }

diff --git a/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/render_state_parity.rs b/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/render_state_parity.rs
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..584035a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/render_state_parity.rs
@@ -0,0 +1,209 @@
+//! Cross-language parity for the per-agent render-state ABI (ADR-029 P2b PR3 +
+//! the survivable-dismemberment stride bump, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561).
+//!
+//! `RENDER_STATE_STRIDE` + the `RS_*` byte offsets are canonical in
+//! `density_feedback.rs` as `pub const`. EVERY GDScript consumer that computes a
+//! per-slot record offset (`slot * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE`) mirrors the stride
+//! locally — and a SINGLE missed mirror silently misaligns every slot after 0
+//! (the cohort's "all-or-nothing" catch on the 4→5 bump). This test reads each
+//! consumer via `include_str!` and asserts the mirrors match the Rust canonical,
+//! the same discipline `bit_protocol_parity.rs` / `anim_intent_parity.rs` /
+//! `gib_event_parity.rs` apply.
+//!
+//! NOTE: `extract_const_usize` is duplicated from `gib_event_parity.rs` — the
+//! parity-test extractor DRY (a shared `tests/common`) is a tracked test-infra
+//! cleanup to be done holistically across all four parity tests (Greptile,
+//! mmnto-ai/liquid-city#607), not folded into this feature PR.
+//!
+//! Bumping the stride (adding a render-state byte):
+//!   1. Bump `RENDER_STATE_STRIDE` + add the `RS_*` offset in `density_feedback.rs`.
+//!   2. Bump `RENDER_STATE_STRIDE` in EVERY consumer below (+ the new `RS_*`
+//!      where it is read).
+//!   3. This test fails until every mirror matches.
+
+use zomboid_sim::density_feedback::{
+    RENDER_STATE_STRIDE, RS_FLAGS, RS_HEALTH, RS_HITSTOP, RS_INTENT, RS_PARTS,
+};
+
+const HORDE_RENDER_ADAPTER_GD: &str =
+    include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd");
+const LOD_MANAGER_3D_GD: &str = include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/LODManager3D.gd");
+const PART_SEGMENTED_GD: &str =
+    include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.gd");
+const SKELETAL_GD: &str = include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/SkeletalHordeAdapter.gd");
+const HORDE_RENDER_BENCH_GD: &str = include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/horde_render_bench.gd");
+const LOD_MANAGER_BENCH_GD: &str = include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/lod_manager_bench.gd");
+const PART_SEGMENTED_BENCH_GD: &str =
+    include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/part_segmented_horde_bench.gd");
+const SKELETAL_BENCH_GD: &str = include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/skeletal_horde_bench.gd");
+const SEVERED_LIMB_BENCH_GD: &str = include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/severed_limb_bench.gd");
+
+/// Scan a GDScript file for `const NAME := <decimal>` (or `const NAME: int =
+/// <decimal>`) and return the parsed `usize`. Word-boundary matched; tolerant of
+/// `:=`/`: int =` and trailing comments/commas.
+fn extract_const_usize(content: &str, name: &str) -> Option<usize> {
+    let prefix = format!("const {name}");
+    for line in content.lines() {
+        let trimmed = line.trim_start();
+        if !trimmed.starts_with(&prefix) {
+            continue;
+        }
+        let after_name = &trimmed[prefix.len()..];
+        match after_name.chars().next() {
+            Some(c) if c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_' => continue,
+            _ => {}
+        }
+        let Some(eq_pos) = trimmed.find('=') else {
+            continue;
+        };
+        let after_eq = trimmed[eq_pos + 1..].trim_start();
+        let Some(token) = after_eq.split_whitespace().next() else {
+            continue;
+        };
+        let token = token.trim_end_matches(',');
+        if let Ok(val) = token.parse::<usize>() {
+            return Some(val);
+        }
+    }
+    None
+}
+
+/// Every consumer that strides the render-state buffer. A missed bump in any of
+/// these is the catastrophic per-slot-misalignment case the cohort flagged.
+const STRIDE_CONSUMERS: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
+    ("HordeRenderAdapter.gd", HORDE_RENDER_ADAPTER_GD),
+    ("LODManager3D.gd", LOD_MANAGER_3D_GD),
+    ("PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.gd", PART_SEGMENTED_GD),
+    ("SkeletalHordeAdapter.gd", SKELETAL_GD),
+    ("horde_render_bench.gd", HORDE_RENDER_BENCH_GD),
+    ("lod_manager_bench.gd", LOD_MANAGER_BENCH_GD),
+    ("part_segmented_horde_bench.gd", PART_SEGMENTED_BENCH_GD),
+    ("skeletal_horde_bench.gd", SKELETAL_BENCH_GD),
+    ("severed_limb_bench.gd", SEVERED_LIMB_BENCH_GD),
+];
+
+#[test]
+fn all_consumers_agree_on_render_state_stride() {
+    let mut failures = Vec::new();
+    for (label, content) in STRIDE_CONSUMERS {
+        match extract_const_usize(content, "RENDER_STATE_STRIDE") {
+            Some(v) if v == RENDER_STATE_STRIDE => {}
+            Some(v) => failures.push(format!(
+                "{label}: RENDER_STATE_STRIDE = {v}, expected {RENDER_STATE_STRIDE} (Rust canonical)"
+            )),
+            None => failures.push(format!(
+                "{label}: RENDER_STATE_STRIDE not found (expected {RENDER_STATE_STRIDE})"
+            )),
+        }
+    }
+    assert!(
+        failures.is_empty(),
+        "render-state stride parity failures (a missed mirror misaligns every slot):\n  - {}",
+        failures.join("\n  - ")
+    );
+}
+
+/// (file label, file content, &[(const name, canonical value)]) — one per
+/// consumer's asymmetric offset table.
+type OffsetTable = (&'static str, &'static str, &'static [(&'static str, usize)]);
+
+#[test]
+fn consumers_agree_on_declared_rs_offsets() {
+    // Per-file: each consumer mirrors only the offsets it actually reads
+    // (intentionally asymmetric, like `bit_protocol_parity.rs`). `RS_PARTS` (the
+    // dismember mask, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561) is read by the production
+    // `PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.gd` and exercised by `severed_limb_bench.gd`.
+    let tables: &[OffsetTable] = &[
+        (
+            "HordeRenderAdapter.gd",
+            HORDE_RENDER_ADAPTER_GD,
+            &[
+                ("RS_FLAGS", RS_FLAGS),
+                ("RS_HEALTH", RS_HEALTH),
+                ("RS_HITSTOP", RS_HITSTOP),
+                ("RS_INTENT", RS_INTENT),
+            ],
+        ),
+        (
+            "LODManager3D.gd",
+            LOD_MANAGER_3D_GD,
+            &[
+                ("RS_HEALTH", RS_HEALTH),
+                ("RS_HITSTOP", RS_HITSTOP),
+                ("RS_INTENT", RS_INTENT),
+            ],
+        ),
+        (
+            "PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.gd",
+            PART_SEGMENTED_GD,
+            &[
+                ("RS_FLAGS", RS_FLAGS),
+                ("RS_HEALTH", RS_HEALTH),
+                ("RS_HITSTOP", RS_HITSTOP),
+                ("RS_INTENT", RS_INTENT),
+                ("RS_PARTS", RS_PARTS),
+            ],
+        ),
+        (
+            "SkeletalHordeAdapter.gd",
+            SKELETAL_GD,
+            &[
+                ("RS_FLAGS", RS_FLAGS),
+                ("RS_HEALTH", RS_HEALTH),
+                ("RS_HITSTOP", RS_HITSTOP),
+                ("RS_INTENT", RS_INTENT),
+            ],
+        ),
+        (
+            "horde_render_bench.gd",
+            HORDE_RENDER_BENCH_GD,
+            &[("RS_HITSTOP", RS_HITSTOP)],
+        ),
+        (
+            "lod_manager_bench.gd",
+            LOD_MANAGER_BENCH_GD,
+            &[
+                ("RS_FLAGS", RS_FLAGS),
+                ("RS_HEALTH", RS_HEALTH),
+                ("RS_HITSTOP", RS_HITSTOP),
+                ("RS_INTENT", RS_INTENT),
+            ],
+        ),
+        (
+            "part_segmented_horde_bench.gd",
+            PART_SEGMENTED_BENCH_GD,
+            &[("RS_HITSTOP", RS_HITSTOP)],
+        ),
+        (
+            "skeletal_horde_bench.gd",
+            SKELETAL_BENCH_GD,
+            &[("RS_HITSTOP", RS_HITSTOP)],
+        ),
+        (
+            "severed_limb_bench.gd",
+            SEVERED_LIMB_BENCH_GD,
+            &[
+                ("RS_FLAGS", RS_FLAGS),
+                ("RS_HEALTH", RS_HEALTH),
+                ("RS_HITSTOP", RS_HITSTOP),
+                ("RS_INTENT", RS_INTENT),
+                ("RS_PARTS", RS_PARTS),
+            ],
+        ),
+    ];
+    let mut failures = Vec::new();
+    for (label, content, offsets) in tables {
+        for (name, canonical) in *offsets {
+            match extract_const_usize(content, name) {
+                Some(v) if v == *canonical => {}
+                Some(v) => failures.push(format!("{label}: {name} = {v}, expected {canonical}")),
+                None => failures.push(format!("{label}: {name} not found (expected {canonical})")),
+            }
+        }
+    }
+    assert!(
+        failures.is_empty(),
+        "render-state offset parity failures:\n  - {}",
+        failures.join("\n  - ")
+    );
+}
diff --git a/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/severed_limb_event_parity.rs b/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/severed_limb_event_parity.rs
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..413d18b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/severed_limb_event_parity.rs
@@ -0,0 +1,171 @@
+//! Cross-language parity for the severed-limb FFI ABI (survivable dismemberment,
+//! mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561).
+//!
+//! The `SeveredLimbEvent` wire record is canonical in `damage.rs` as `pub const`
+//! `SEVERED_LIMB_EVENT_STRIDE` + the `SL_OFF_*` byte offsets. It is a SEPARATE
+//! event from `GibEvent` (a Gib pulps the whole body -> a 6-part burst; a sever
+//! loses ONE limb -> one chunk), so it carries its own stride + its own parity
+//! test rather than overloading the 36-byte gib record. The Godot renderer
+//! `GibDebrisRenderer.gd` mirrors the consts as `const SL_* := N` to decode the
+//! drained FFI buffer; this test reads the GDScript via `include_str!` and asserts
+//! the mirror matches the Rust canonical — the same discipline `gib_event_parity.rs`
+//! / `bit_protocol_parity.rs` / `anim_intent_parity.rs` apply.
+//!
+//! NOTE: `extract_const_usize` is duplicated from `gib_event_parity.rs` — the
+//! parity-test extractor DRY (a shared `tests/common`) is a tracked test-infra
+//! cleanup to be done holistically across all parity tests (Greptile,
+//! mmnto-ai/liquid-city#607), not folded into this feature PR.
+//!
+//! Adding/moving a field:
+//!   1. Edit the `SL_*` consts in `damage.rs`.
+//!   2. Mirror the `const SL_* := N` in `GibDebrisRenderer.gd`.
+//!   3. Extend the mirror table below.
+
+use zomboid_sim::damage::{
+    SEVERED_LIMB_EVENT_STRIDE, SL_OFF_PART, SL_OFF_POS_X, SL_OFF_POS_Y, SL_OFF_POS_Z, SL_OFF_SEED,
+    SL_OFF_SLOT, SL_OFF_TICK, SL_OFF_VEL_X, SL_OFF_VEL_Y, SL_OFF_VEL_Z,
+};
+
+const GIB_DEBRIS_RENDERER_GD: &str = include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/GibDebrisRenderer.gd");
+const SEVERED_LIMB_BENCH_GD: &str = include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/severed_limb_bench.gd");
+
+/// Every GDScript file that mirrors the `SL_*` ABI: the production renderer AND the
+/// bench, which builds synthetic ABI bytes from its OWN copy of the constants — a
+/// silent drift there would feed `ingest_severed` wrong-but-valid-looking bytes with
+/// no test failure (Greptile, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#609).
+const SL_MIRROR_FILES: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
+    ("GibDebrisRenderer.gd", GIB_DEBRIS_RENDERER_GD),
+    ("severed_limb_bench.gd", SEVERED_LIMB_BENCH_GD),
+];
+
+/// Scan a GDScript file for `const NAME := <decimal>` (or `const NAME: int =
+/// <decimal>`) and return the parsed `usize`. Word-boundary matched; tolerant of
+/// `:=`/`: int =` and trailing comments/commas. Duplicated from
+/// `gib_event_parity.rs` pending the shared-extractor cleanup (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#607).
+fn extract_const_usize(content: &str, name: &str) -> Option<usize> {
+    let prefix = format!("const {name}");
+    for line in content.lines() {
+        let trimmed = line.trim_start();
+        if !trimmed.starts_with(&prefix) {
+            continue;
+        }
+        // Word-boundary: the char after the name must not extend an identifier, so
+        // a query for `SL_OFF_POS_X` does not match `const SL_OFF_POS_XY`.
+        let after_name = &trimmed[prefix.len()..];
+        match after_name.chars().next() {
+            Some(c) if c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_' => continue,
+            _ => {}
+        }
+        let Some(eq_pos) = trimmed.find('=') else {
+            continue;
+        };
+        let after_eq = trimmed[eq_pos + 1..].trim_start();
+        let Some(token) = after_eq.split_whitespace().next() else {
+            continue;
+        };
+        let token = token.trim_end_matches(',');
+        if let Ok(val) = token.parse::<usize>() {
+            return Some(val);
+        }
+    }
+    None
+}
+
+struct Mirror {
+    local_name: &'static str,
+    canonical: usize,
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn gdscript_severed_abi_mirrors_match_rust() {
+    let mirrors = [
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "SEVERED_LIMB_EVENT_STRIDE",
+            canonical: SEVERED_LIMB_EVENT_STRIDE,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "SL_OFF_SLOT",
+            canonical: SL_OFF_SLOT,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "SL_OFF_TICK",
+            canonical: SL_OFF_TICK,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "SL_OFF_SEED",
+            canonical: SL_OFF_SEED,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "SL_OFF_POS_X",
+            canonical: SL_OFF_POS_X,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "SL_OFF_POS_Y",
+            canonical: SL_OFF_POS_Y,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "SL_OFF_POS_Z",
+            canonical: SL_OFF_POS_Z,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "SL_OFF_VEL_X",
+            canonical: SL_OFF_VEL_X,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "SL_OFF_VEL_Y",
+            canonical: SL_OFF_VEL_Y,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "SL_OFF_VEL_Z",
+            canonical: SL_OFF_VEL_Z,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "SL_OFF_PART",
+            canonical: SL_OFF_PART,
+        },
+    ];
+    let mut failures = Vec::new();
+    for (label, content) in SL_MIRROR_FILES {
+        for m in &mirrors {
+            match extract_const_usize(content, m.local_name) {
+                Some(v) if v == m.canonical => {}
+                Some(v) => failures.push(format!(
+                    "{label}: `const {}` = {}, expected {} (Rust damage.rs)",
+                    m.local_name, v, m.canonical
+                )),
+                None => failures.push(format!(
+                    "{label}: `const {}` not found (expected {})",
+                    m.local_name, m.canonical
+                )),
+            }
+        }
+    }
+    assert!(
+        failures.is_empty(),
+        "severed-limb ABI cross-language parity failures:\n  - {}",
+        failures.join("\n  - ")
+    );
+}
+
+/// The record is 3×u32 (slot/tick/seed) + 6×f32 (pos xyz, vel xyz) + 1×u8 (part).
+/// Every 4-byte field sits exactly 4 past the previous; `part` is a single trailing
+/// byte at `VEL_Z + 4`, and the stride is `PART + 1`. Asserting the FULL sequential
+/// chain (not just the terminal gap) catches an internal offset shift even if the
+/// stride were bumped to compensate — e.g. `POS_X` → 13 with all later offsets + the
+/// stride each +1 would pass a terminal-only check (Greptile, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#607).
+/// Independent of the GDScript mirror.
+#[test]
+fn severed_limb_offsets_tile_the_stride() {
+    assert_eq!(SL_OFF_SLOT, 0);
+    assert_eq!(SL_OFF_TICK, SL_OFF_SLOT + 4);
+    assert_eq!(SL_OFF_SEED, SL_OFF_TICK + 4);
+    assert_eq!(SL_OFF_POS_X, SL_OFF_SEED + 4);
+    assert_eq!(SL_OFF_POS_Y, SL_OFF_POS_X + 4);
+    assert_eq!(SL_OFF_POS_Z, SL_OFF_POS_Y + 4);
+    assert_eq!(SL_OFF_VEL_X, SL_OFF_POS_Z + 4);
+    assert_eq!(SL_OFF_VEL_Y, SL_OFF_VEL_X + 4);
+    assert_eq!(SL_OFF_VEL_Z, SL_OFF_VEL_Y + 4);
+    // `part` is a single byte flush after vel.z; the stride is one past it.
+    assert_eq!(SL_OFF_PART, SL_OFF_VEL_Z + 4);
+    assert_eq!(SL_OFF_PART + 1, SEVERED_LIMB_EVENT_STRIDE);
+}

index 7573a54..0eaef42 100644
--- a/.totem/specs/561.md
+++ b/.totem/specs/561.md
@@ -87,4 +87,43 @@ Armless-still-shambles/bites is free (a mask-read in the attack gate). **WILL ve
 - **GDScript ABI-const centralization** (one consts file vs per-adapter mirrors guarded by the parity scan).
 
 ## PR cut
-Two PRs: **PR-A** (substrate + ABI-all-consumers + mass-scaled cause-rule + non-torso selection + hide + one-part `SeveredLimb` debris — the visible slice; satisfies *visual* legibility; mirrors the gib PR shape) → **PR-B** (tactical locomotion — the *tactical* layer). Each independently reviewable; PR-A shippable alone.
+Two PRs: **PR-A** (substrate + ABI-all-consumers + mass-scaled cause-rule + non-torso selection + hide + one-part `SeveredLimb` debris — the visible slice; satisfies *visual* legibility; mirrors the gib PR shape) → **PR-B** (tactical locomotion — the *tactical* layer). Each independently reviewable; PR-A shippable alone. **PR-A MERGED** (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#609 → `main` a4d8708).
+
+## Implementation Design — PR-B (tactical locomotion), rev 1 (cohort-folded 2026-06-23)
+**Cohort BLIND impl-preflight folded** — lc-codex (`2210Z`), lc-gemini (`2159Z`), lc-agy (`2200Z`): unanimous on forks 1/2/3/4/5/7 + the arm-mode deferral; 2:1 on fork 6 (render), ruled below. Source-grounded by me (re-verify at impl time).
+
+### Scope
+PR-B makes the sim **read** `Dismembered.parts` to change locomotion: both-legs → crawl, one-leg → hobble, head → blind-wander; armless-still-shambles is free. WILL NOT add the "arm → can't grapple" defang — **no agent→player threat exists** (`combat.rs` is player→agent only; `AnimIntent::Attack` is RESERVED/unused), so the flag would guard a non-existent mechanic (deferred to a future zombie-threat slice). WILL NOT add a new `AnimIntent`, spawn agents, or touch PR-A's substrate/ABI.
+
+### Data model deltas
+- **No new component.** Crawl/hobble read PR-A's `ActiveStatuses.dismembered.parts`; blind is stateless (fork 2a).
+- **New system `dismember_locomotion_system`** (sim, in-checksum): `par_iter_mut` over `(&ActiveStatuses, &mut Velocity), Without<BallisticMode>`; disjoint planar write (`dx/dy`); ordered `.after(speed_variation_system).before(damage_resolution_system)` (codex — both write `Velocity`, don't let Bevy infer). both-legs → ×`CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE`; one-leg → ×`HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE`; no severed legs → identity fast path (skip the multiplies, like `Vagrant`).
+- **Blind branch in `flow_field_sample_system`**: query gains `Option<&ActiveStatuses>` + `Option<&BufferSlot>` (codex caveat 2 — don't narrow the query; non-agent/test `WorldPosition+Velocity` entities keep flowing). Placed AFTER the existing `flow_fields.field(pos.floor)` read/assert (codex caveat 1 — preserve the floor-invariant fail-loud). If the head bit is set: substitute the seeded wander vector and **gate OUT the close-range direct-pursuit override** (fork 3).
+- **Wander vector = NO trig** (codex+agy convergent — libm cross-platform desync under ADR-013): a fixed precomputed unit-vector **direction table** (8 dirs) indexed by `hash(slot, tick / WANDER_PERIOD)` (SplitMix, `dismember_seed`-style). Bit-exact, no `sin/cos`, no normalize zero-edge.
+- **New tuning consts (`tuning_stub.rs`):** `CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE` (~0.25), `HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE` (~0.55), `WANDER_PERIOD` (ticks). Compile-time assert `0.0 < CRAWL < HOBBLE < 1.0` (mirrors `WALL_SPEED_SCALE > 0` deadlock guard).
+
+### State lifecycle
+- `dismember_locomotion_system`: per-frame read of `ActiveStatuses`, per-frame `Velocity` write; zero persistent new state.
+- Blind heading: derived per-frame from `(slot, tick-bucket)`; zero persistent state. `WANDER_PERIOD` buckets ticks so the heading changes slowly (shamble, not jitter).
+- Constants: compile-time / server-lifetime.
+
+### Failure modes
+| Failure | Category | Surface | Recovery |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `CRAWL`/`HOBBLE` misordered or 0 | init/config | compile-time assert | `0 < CRAWL < HOBBLE < 1` blocks the build |
+| Wander vector non-finite | runtime | `debug_assert` (mirror `speed_variation_system`) | finite by construction (table = unit vectors) |
+| Agent with default `ActiveStatuses` | runtime | identity fast path | no-op, velocity untouched |
+| Blind agent on invalid floor | runtime | floor read/assert kept before blind branch (codex) | fails loud as today |
+| Test/non-agent entity w/o `BufferSlot` | runtime | `Option<&BufferSlot>` → normal flow | doesn't drop out of sampling |
+
+### Invariants to lock in via tests (codex's list)
+- both-legs velocity = ×`CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE` (and > 0); one-leg = ×`HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE` — after the FULL chain (`SpeedMultiplier` + density still stack).
+- no-sever agent velocity unchanged (identity).
+- blind agent near the active entity with LOS does NOT direct-pursue; velocity = the deterministic blind heading (close-range branch gated).
+- modes compose: head + both-legs = blind direction + crawl magnitude in the production schedule.
+- two same-seed worlds → identical `Velocity.to_bits()`/position bits for blind agents across `WANDER_PERIOD` boundaries.
+- crawling publishes `AnimIntent::Locomotion` + nonzero `RS_PARTS` (no new intent).
+- armless agent locomotion unchanged.
+
+### Open questions (ruled)
+- **Fork 6 (render) — RULED: reuse `AnimIntent::Locomotion`** (codex+gemini, over agy's add-`Crawl`). agy's levitation-bug concern (legless rig bobbing on the walk clip) is real, but the fix lives RENDER-SIDE off the parts mask the adapter already reads (PR-A) — when both leg bits are set the adapter can lay-flat / suppress the vertical bob as a cheap stopgap, no new sim intent / FFI surface, until Ruben's crawl clip lands. **VIEW the perceptual capture** to decide whether the stopgap is needed for PR-B or a fast-follow ([[feedback_diagnostic_first_observability]]).

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/dismember_locomotion_bench.gd b/apps/game-godot/dismember_locomotion_bench.gd

index 0000000..e9433e2
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/dismember_locomotion_bench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,234 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 gore epic mmnto-ai/liquid-city#565, PR-B (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561) —
+# tactical-locomotion render bench. The SIM side (crawl/hobble/blind velocity) is
+# proven by the zomboid-sim cargo tests; this bench is the PERCEPTUAL proof that a
+# dismembered rig reads correctly while playing its Locomotion clip — specifically
+# the fork-6 question (lc-agy): does a LEGLESS rig running the upright walk cycle
+# "levitate"/bob? VIEW the PNG to decide whether a render-side lay-flat stopgap is
+# warranted or Locomotion-as-is reads fine. (PR2 caught a 10x head-scale bug only by
+# VIEWING — legibility is evidenced at the player's frame, not theorized.)
+#
+# Three rigs in a row, all Locomotion: intact (baseline) | both-legs-gone (CRAWL) |
+# head-gone (BLIND). PHASE A (gates exit): the production byte[4] path hides exactly
+# the severed limbs. PHASE B: a gameplay-camera PNG.
+# Run: <godot> --path apps/game-godot res://dismember_locomotion_bench.tscn
+#   0 = clean, 1 = assertion failed, 2 = watchdog.
+
+# render-state record layout (mirror of the ABI; render_state_parity.rs locks it).
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 5
+const RS_FLAGS := 0
+const RS_HEALTH := 1
+const RS_HITSTOP := 2
+const RS_INTENT := 3
+const RS_PARTS := 4
+const LIVE_BIT := 0x04
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const TIER_SKEL := 2
+const PART_COUNT := 6
+
+# ADR-027 Dismembered.parts bits (mirror of components.rs DISMEMBER_BIT_*).
+const BIT_HEAD := 0
+const BIT_LEG_LEFT := 3
+const BIT_LEG_RIGHT := 4
+const PARTS_CRAWL := (1 << BIT_LEG_LEFT) | (1 << BIT_LEG_RIGHT)
+const PARTS_BLIND := 1 << BIT_HEAD
+
+const OUT_DIR := "user://dismember_locomotion_bench/"
+const NC := 3  # rendered agents
+const A_INTACT := 0
+const A_CRAWL := 1
+const A_BLIND := 2
+const CAP := 4  # adapter pool (>= NC)
+const SPACING := 2.6
+const FRAMES_C := 6  # let the walk clip advance before asserting
+const DELTA_C := 0.1
+const CAPTURE_AT_FRAME := 18  # mid walk-cycle, well under the watchdog
+
+var _adapter: PartSegmentedHordeAdapter
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _book: SlotClockBook
+var _xf := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _rs := PackedByteArray()
+var _tiers := PackedByteArray()
+
+var _phase := 0  # 0 = correctness, 1 = perceptual
+var _frame := 0
+var _fails := 0
+var _watchdog := 0
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless":
+		# Headless: the adapter builds no GPU nodes and stays inert.
+		var book := SlotClockBook.new()
+		var a := PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.new(self, true, CAP, book)
+		a.setup()
+		a.set_active(true)
+		var ok := not a.is_active() and a.last_live == 0
+		print("[loco-bench] headless — adapter no-op %s; quit 0" % ("OK" if ok else "UNEXPECTED"))
+		get_tree().quit(0)
+		return
+
+	DisplayServer.window_set_vsync_mode(DisplayServer.VSYNC_DISABLED)
+	Engine.max_fps = 144  # cap fps so the frame-counted capture stays under the watchdog
+	_cam = Camera3D.new()
+	_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_PERSPECTIVE
+	_cam.fov = 55.0
+	_cam.current = true
+	add_child(_cam)
+	_add_scene()
+
+	_book = SlotClockBook.new()
+	_adapter = PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.new(self, false, CAP, _book)
+	if not _adapter.setup():
+		print("[loco-bench] FAIL: adapter setup returned false (see part-name push_error)")
+		get_tree().quit(1)
+		return
+	_adapter.set_active(true)
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(OUT_DIR))
+	_build_buffers()
+	_frame_camera()
+	print("[loco-bench] start; adapter=%s" % _adapter.debug_status())
+
+
+func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
+	if _adapter == null:
+		return
+	# Hard watchdog: a self-quitting bench must never hang a window.
+	_watchdog += 1
+	if _watchdog > 1200:
+		push_error("[loco-bench] watchdog tripped — force quit")
+		get_tree().quit(2)
+		return
+	# Advance the shared clock + run the production update() so the rigs play their
+	# Locomotion clip (the legless bob, if any, shows here).
+	_advance_clocks(DELTA_C)
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, NC, _tiers)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _phase == 0:
+		if _frame >= FRAMES_C:
+			_verify()
+			_phase = 1
+		return
+	if _frame >= CAPTURE_AT_FRAME:
+		_capture()
+		_finalise()
+
+
+func _verify() -> void:
+	# Production hide path: byte[4] applied by update() -> _apply_part_mask hides
+	# EXACTLY the severed limbs on each rendered rig.
+	_check_hide(A_CRAWL, PARTS_CRAWL, "both legs hidden on the crawl rig")
+	_check_hide(A_BLIND, PARTS_BLIND, "head hidden on the blind rig")
+	_check_hide(A_INTACT, 0, "intact rig keeps all parts visible")
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print("[loco-bench] PHASE A: PASS")
+	else:
+		print("[loco-bench] PHASE A: FAIL (%d assertion(s))" % _fails)
+
+
+func _check_hide(slot: int, mask: int, label: String) -> void:
+	var pi := _pool_index_of(slot)
+	if pi < 0:
+		_check(false, label, "no pool index for slot %d" % slot)
+		return
+	var by_bit: Array = _adapter._part_by_bit[pi]
+	var ok := true
+	for bit in PART_COUNT:
+		var should_be_visible := (mask & (1 << bit)) == 0
+		if by_bit[bit].visible != should_be_visible:
+			ok = false
+	_check(ok, label, "mask mismatch")
+
+
+func _capture() -> void:
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img != null:
+		var path := OUT_DIR + "perceptual_locomotion.png"
+		img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+		print("[loco-bench] perceptual capture %s" % path)
+
+
+func _finalise() -> void:
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print(
+			"[loco-bench] VERDICT: GO — dismember-locomotion render legible; perceptual PNG captured"
+		)
+	else:
+		print("[loco-bench] VERDICT: FAIL — %d assertion(s)" % _fails)
+	get_tree().quit(0 if _fails == 0 else 1)
+
+
+# NC live agents in a short row along sim-x: intact, both-legs-gone (crawl),
+# head-gone (blind). Each carries its Dismembered.parts mask in byte[4] so the
+# production update() path hides the right limbs; all play Locomotion.
+func _build_buffers() -> void:
+	_xf = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_rs = PackedByteArray()
+	_xf.resize(NC * 8)
+	_rs.resize(NC * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE)
+	var masks := [0, PARTS_CRAWL, PARTS_BLIND]
+	for i in NC:
+		var o := i * 8
+		# AGENT_STRIDE=8 transform pack; the adapter consumes [0]=basis-xx +
+		# [4]=basis-xy (heading via atan2), [3]=ox, [7]=oy. [0]=1 / [4]=0 -> heading 0
+		# (face forward). Indices 1/2/5/6 are inert padding the adapter never reads.
+		_xf[o + 0] = 1.0
+		_xf[o + 3] = float(i) * SPACING  # sim x
+		_xf[o + 7] = 0.0  # sim y
+		var ro := i * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		_rs[ro + RS_FLAGS] = LIVE_BIT
+		_rs[ro + RS_HEALTH] = 220
+		_rs[ro + RS_HITSTOP] = 0
+		_rs[ro + RS_INTENT] = ANIM_LOCOMOTION
+		_rs[ro + RS_PARTS] = masks[i]
+	_tiers.resize(NC)
+	for i in NC:
+		_tiers[i] = TIER_SKEL
+
+
+# Advance the shared clock once per live slot, exactly as LODManager3D does (the
+# adapter is a pure reader).
+func _advance_clocks(delta: float) -> void:
+	_book.ensure_capacity(NC)
+	for s in NC:
+		_book.advance(s, delta, _rs[s * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE + RS_HITSTOP])
+
+
+# Gameplay-distance 3/4 front framing centered on the middle (crawl) rig.
+func _frame_camera() -> void:
+	var mid := float(A_CRAWL) * SPACING
+	_cam.position = Vector3(mid + 0.6, 2.4, 7.0)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(mid, 1.0, 0.0), Vector3.UP)
+
+
+# A dim ground plane + a sun so the rigs read against the floor (bench prop, NOT the
+# game's lighting — that is mmnto-ai/liquid-city#586).
+func _add_scene() -> void:
+	var ground := MeshInstance3D.new()
+	var pm := PlaneMesh.new()
+	pm.size = Vector2(40.0, 40.0)
+	ground.mesh = pm
+	var mat := StandardMaterial3D.new()
+	mat.albedo_color = Color(0.16, 0.17, 0.19)
+	ground.material_override = mat
+	add_child(ground)
+	var sun := DirectionalLight3D.new()
+	sun.rotation_degrees = Vector3(-55.0, -35.0, 0.0)
+	add_child(sun)
+
+
+func _check(ok: bool, label: String, detail: String) -> void:
+	if ok:
+		print("[loco-bench] PASS: %s" % label)
+	else:
+		_fails += 1
+		print("[loco-bench] FAIL: %s — %s" % [label, detail])
+
+
+func _pool_index_of(slot: int) -> int:
+	for i in _adapter._pool_slot.size():
+		if _adapter._pool_slot[i] == slot:
+			return i
+	return -1

diff --git a/packages/zomboid-sim/src/dismember_locomotion.rs b/packages/zomboid-sim/src/dismember_locomotion.rs
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..a6ece56
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/zomboid-sim/src/dismember_locomotion.rs
@@ -0,0 +1,298 @@
+//! Tactical locomotion from survivable dismemberment (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561
+//! PR-B). The first SIM-side *reader* of `ActiveStatuses.dismembered.parts` —
+//! PR-A (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#609) only *published* it to the render ABI; here
+//! the missing limb changes the threat.
+//!
+//! Two hooks, per the cohort impl-preflight (lc-codex/lc-gemini/lc-agy,
+//! 2026-06-23). Direction and magnitude live at structurally different stages
+//! of the locomotion chain, so they cannot collapse into one system:
+//!
+//! - **Magnitude — here:** [`dismember_locomotion_system`] scales planar
+//!   velocity for missing legs (both → crawl, one → hobble). A post-clamp
+//!   per-agent scalar, the exact shape of [`speed_variation_system`][sv]:
+//!   `par_iter_mut`, disjoint `Velocity` write, `Without<BallisticMode>`,
+//!   slotted `.after(speed_variation_system).before(damage_resolution_system)`.
+//! - **Direction — `systems/flow.rs`:** a headless agent loses the player and
+//!   shambles. [`blind_wander_dir`] supplies a seeded wander heading that
+//!   `flow_field_sample_system` substitutes for the flow gradient + the
+//!   close-range pursuit override.
+//!
+//! All effects are **authoritative / in-checksum** (ADR-013) — they move the
+//! agent. No per-tick RNG, no wall-clock: the wander is a pure SplitMix hash of
+//! `(BufferSlot, coarse tick bucket)`, and the leg scalars are pure functions of
+//! the authoritative `Dismembered.parts`. Reading `parts` directly is sound: PR-A
+//! sets a part bit only alongside `ACTIVE_STATUS_DISMEMBERED_BIT` and never
+//! clears it, so `parts != 0` already implies the status is live.
+//!
+//! [sv]: crate::speed_variation::speed_variation_system
+
+use bevy_ecs::prelude::*;
+
+use crate::components::{
+    ActiveStatuses, BallisticMode, Velocity, DISMEMBER_BIT_HEAD, DISMEMBER_BIT_LEG_LEFT,
+    DISMEMBER_BIT_LEG_RIGHT,
+};
+use crate::tuning_stub::{CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE, HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE, WANDER_PERIOD};
+
+/// The two leg bits of `Dismembered.parts` (ADR-027 §1 layout: bit3 leg-left,
+/// bit4 leg-right). Shared by the scalar mapping and its boundary tests.
+pub(crate) const LEG_MASK: u8 = (1 << DISMEMBER_BIT_LEG_LEFT) | (1 << DISMEMBER_BIT_LEG_RIGHT);
+
+/// Planar speed multiplier for an agent's *leg*-dismemberment state — pure
+/// classification, unit-testable without an ECS world. `None` = identity (the
+/// common case: both legs intact), so the caller skips the multiplies entirely
+/// (mirrors `horde_speed_modulation_system`'s Vagrant fast path and avoids a
+/// `clippy::float_cmp` against `1.0`). Both legs gone → crawl; exactly one →
+/// hobble. Head/arm/torso bits do not affect speed.
+#[inline]
+pub fn leg_locomotion_scale(parts: u8) -> Option<f32> {
+    match parts & LEG_MASK {
+        0 => None,
+        LEG_MASK => Some(CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE),
+        _ => Some(HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE),
+    }
+}
+
+/// `true` when the agent has lost its head (`Dismembered.parts` bit0). A blind
+/// agent steers by [`blind_wander_dir`] instead of the player-seeking flow.
+#[inline]
+pub(crate) fn is_blind(parts: u8) -> bool {
+    parts & (1 << DISMEMBER_BIT_HEAD) != 0
+}
+
+/// Eight compass unit vectors — the blind-wander heading table. A fixed
+/// pre-baked table (NOT `angle.sin()/cos()`) because transcendental libm
+/// results differ across platforms and would desync the ADR-013 lockstep
+/// (cohort convergent catch, lc-codex + lc-agy). `R = 1/√2` is the exact f32
+/// std constant, so every entry is bit-identical on every target.
+const R: f32 = std::f32::consts::FRAC_1_SQRT_2;
+const WANDER_DIRS: [(f32, f32); 8] = [
+    (1.0, 0.0),
+    (R, R),
+    (0.0, 1.0),
+    (-R, R),
+    (-1.0, 0.0),
+    (-R, -R),
+    (0.0, -1.0),
+    (R, -R),
+];
+
+/// SplitMix32 finalizer for the blind-wander heading. Same shape as
+/// [`crate::damage::dismember_seed`] / `gib_seed`, domain-separated by a
+/// distinct additive constant so an agent's wander stream never correlates with
+/// its debris-seed stream. Pure `wrapping`/xorshift — bit-identical everywhere.
+#[inline]
+fn wander_hash(slot: u32, bucket: u32) -> u32 {
+    let mut z = (slot ^ bucket.rotate_left(16)).wrapping_add(0xC2B2_AE35);
+    z = (z ^ (z >> 16)).wrapping_mul(0x21F0_AAAD);
+    z = (z ^ (z >> 15)).wrapping_mul(0x735A_2D97);
+    z ^ (z >> 15)
+}
+
+/// Deterministic blind-wander heading (unit vector) for `(slot, tick)`. The
+/// tick is bucketed by `WANDER_PERIOD` so the heading *holds* for that many
+/// ticks — a slow shamble-drift, not a per-tick jitter. Pure: no RNG, no
+/// wall-clock, no trig → bit-exact across the lockstep cohort.
+#[inline]
+pub(crate) fn blind_wander_dir(slot: u32, tick: u32) -> (f32, f32) {
+    let bucket = tick / WANDER_PERIOD;
+    let idx = (wander_hash(slot, bucket) % WANDER_DIRS.len() as u32) as usize;
+    WANDER_DIRS[idx]
+}
+
+/// Scale each non-ballistic agent's planar velocity by its leg-dismemberment
+/// locomotion mode (crawl / hobble). The magnitude tail of the locomotion
+/// chain — slotted `.after(speed_variation_system).before(damage_resolution_system)`
+/// so it composes on top of the `SpeedMultiplier` + density scalers and feeds
+/// the modulated velocity into advection. Ballistic agents are excluded (their
+/// `Velocity` is launch state, not walk locomotion), matching
+/// `speed_variation_system` / `flocking_system`.
+///
+/// Parallel-safe (`systems.rs` rule 1): `par_iter_mut` with a disjoint
+/// per-entity `Velocity` write, no cross-entity reads, no reduction. A release
+/// `assert!` inside a Rayon closure is unreliable across the FFI boundary, so
+/// the finiteness tripwire is a `debug_assert!` — the scale is a finite
+/// positive constant and the incoming velocity is finite (flocking clamp), so
+/// it cannot fire in practice.
+pub fn dismember_locomotion_system(
+    mut q: Query<(&ActiveStatuses, &mut Velocity), Without<BallisticMode>>,
+) {
+    q.par_iter_mut().for_each(|(status, mut vel)| {
+        let Some(scale) = leg_locomotion_scale(status.dismembered.parts) else {
+            return; // identity fast path — no severed legs (the common case)
+        };
+        vel.dx *= scale;
+        vel.dy *= scale;
+        debug_assert!(
+            vel.dx.is_finite() && vel.dy.is_finite(),
+            "dismember_locomotion_system: non-finite velocity after scaling \
+             (scale={}, dx={}, dy={})",
+            scale,
+            vel.dx,
+            vel.dy
+        );
+    });
+}
+
+#[cfg(test)]
+mod tests {
+    use super::*;
+
+    fn legs(left: bool, right: bool) -> u8 {
+        let mut p = 0;
+        if left {
+            p |= 1 << DISMEMBER_BIT_LEG_LEFT;
+        }
+        if right {
+            p |= 1 << DISMEMBER_BIT_LEG_RIGHT;
+        }
+        p
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn intact_legs_are_identity_none() {
+        assert_eq!(leg_locomotion_scale(0), None);
+        // Head/arm/torso loss alone does not slow locomotion.
+        assert_eq!(leg_locomotion_scale(1 << DISMEMBER_BIT_HEAD), None);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn both_legs_gone_is_crawl() {
+        assert_eq!(
+            leg_locomotion_scale(legs(true, true)),
+            Some(CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE)
+        );
+        assert_eq!(leg_locomotion_scale(LEG_MASK), Some(CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE));
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn one_leg_gone_is_hobble() {
+        assert_eq!(
+            leg_locomotion_scale(legs(true, false)),
+            Some(HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE)
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            leg_locomotion_scale(legs(false, true)),
+            Some(HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE)
+        );
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn crawl_is_slower_than_hobble_and_both_positive() {
+        // The deadlock-floor + ordering invariant the tuning asserts at compile
+        // time, restated as a runtime check on the mapping.
+        let crawl = leg_locomotion_scale(LEG_MASK).unwrap();
+        let hobble = leg_locomotion_scale(legs(true, false)).unwrap();
+        assert!(0.0 < crawl && crawl < hobble && hobble < 1.0);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn is_blind_reads_only_the_head_bit() {
+        assert!(is_blind(1 << DISMEMBER_BIT_HEAD));
+        assert!(!is_blind(0));
+        assert!(!is_blind(LEG_MASK), "leg loss is not blindness");
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn wander_dir_is_unit_length_and_finite() {
+        for slot in 0..64u32 {
+            for tick in (0..600u32).step_by(7) {
+                let (x, y) = blind_wander_dir(slot, tick);
+                assert!(x.is_finite() && y.is_finite());
+                let mag_sq = x * x + y * y;
+                assert!((mag_sq - 1.0).abs() < 1e-6, "non-unit heading: {mag_sq}");
+            }
+        }
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn wander_dir_is_deterministic_and_holds_within_a_period() {
+        // Same (slot, tick) → same heading (lockstep determinism).
+        assert_eq!(blind_wander_dir(5, 90), blind_wander_dir(5, 90));
+        // Ticks inside one WANDER_PERIOD bucket share a heading (shamble, not
+        // jitter); a bucket boundary may change it.
+        let base = (WANDER_PERIOD) * 3;
+        assert_eq!(
+            blind_wander_dir(5, base),
+            blind_wander_dir(5, base + WANDER_PERIOD - 1),
+            "heading must hold across a wander period"
+        );
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn wander_dir_varies_by_slot() {
+        // Not all slots share a heading at a given tick (decorrelated by slot).
+        let t = WANDER_PERIOD * 2;
+        let headings: std::collections::HashSet<_> = (0..32u32)
+            .map(|s| {
+                let (x, y) = blind_wander_dir(s, t);
+                (x.to_bits(), y.to_bits())
+            })
+            .collect();
+        assert!(headings.len() > 1, "all slots wandered identically");
+    }
+
+    fn status_with(parts: u8) -> ActiveStatuses {
+        ActiveStatuses {
+            active_mask: crate::components::ACTIVE_STATUS_DISMEMBERED_BIT,
+            dismembered: crate::components::Dismembered { parts },
+        }
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn system_scales_leg_severed_and_leaves_intact_untouched() {
+        bevy_tasks::ComputeTaskPool::get_or_init(bevy_tasks::TaskPool::default);
+        let mut world = World::new();
+        let v = || Velocity {
+            dx: 1.0,
+            dy: 0.0,
+            dz: 0.0,
+        };
+        let intact = world.spawn((ActiveStatuses::default(), v())).id();
+        let hobble = world.spawn((status_with(legs(true, false)), v())).id();
+        let crawl = world.spawn((status_with(LEG_MASK), v())).id();
+
+        let mut sched = Schedule::default();
+        sched.add_systems(dismember_locomotion_system);
+        sched.run(&mut world);
+
+        assert_eq!(world.get::<Velocity>(intact).unwrap().dx, 1.0);
+        assert_eq!(
+            world.get::<Velocity>(hobble).unwrap().dx,
+            HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE
+        );
+        assert_eq!(world.get::<Velocity>(crawl).unwrap().dx, CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn system_skips_ballistic_agents() {
+        // A leg-severed agent that is mid-flight keeps its launch velocity —
+        // `Without<BallisticMode>`, matching `speed_variation_system`.
+        bevy_tasks::ComputeTaskPool::get_or_init(bevy_tasks::TaskPool::default);
+        let mut world = World::new();
+        let e = world
+            .spawn((
+                status_with(LEG_MASK),
+                Velocity {
+                    dx: 1.0,
+                    dy: 0.0,
+                    dz: 0.0,
+                },
+                BallisticMode {
+                    velocity: [5.0, 0.0, 2.0],
+                    spawned_at_tick: 0,
+                },
+            ))
+            .id();
+
+        let mut sched = Schedule::default();
+        sched.add_systems(dismember_locomotion_system);
+        sched.run(&mut world);
+
+        assert_eq!(
+            world.get::<Velocity>(e).unwrap().dx,
+            1.0,
+            "ballistic agents are excluded from leg-locomotion scaling"
+        );
+    }
+}
diff --git a/packages/zomboid-sim/src/lib.rs b/packages/zomboid-sim/src/lib.rs
index fdac050..642cd5a 100644
--- a/packages/zomboid-sim/src/lib.rs
+++ b/packages/zomboid-sim/src/lib.rs
@@ -23,6 +23,7 @@ pub mod damage;
 pub mod density;
 pub mod density_feedback;

+        // crawl. The blind branch (flow sample) sets DIRECTION; the post-clamp

+    }

@@ -125,6 +125,36 @@ const _: () = assert!(
      MAX_AGENT_SPEED stays a hard ceiling (exceeding it risks wall tunnelling)",
 );
 
+/// Tactical-locomotion speed scales for survivable dismemberment
+/// (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561 PR-B). Planar-velocity multipliers applied by
+/// `dismember_locomotion_system` when an agent has lost legs: both legs →
+/// `CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE` (drag the torso, still ankle-grabs); one leg →
+/// `HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE` (easy to outrun). A drive-time feel knob, like the
+/// speed-multiplier spread; graduate to the runtime `FlockingTuning` resource
+/// if live tuning is wanted.
+pub const CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE: f32 = 0.25;
+pub const HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE: f32 = 0.55;
+
+/// Blind-wander heading hold, in ticks, for a headless agent
+/// (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561 PR-B). `blind_wander_dir` buckets the sim tick by
+/// this period so the seeded heading holds `WANDER_PERIOD × FIXED_DT` ≈ 0.5 s —
+/// a shamble drift, not a per-tick jitter. Must be strictly > 0 (it divides the
+/// tick).
+pub const WANDER_PERIOD: u32 = 30;
+
+// Crawl/hobble deadlock-floor + ordering invariant, mirroring the
+// `WALL_SPEED_SCALE > 0` guard in `horde_speed.rs`: both scales strictly
+// positive (a zeroed crawl freezes the agent into a hidden-death look),
+// crawl strictly slower than hobble, both below 1.0 (dismemberment only
+// slows). `WANDER_PERIOD` strictly positive so the bucket divide is safe.
+const _: () = assert!(
+    0.0 < CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE
+        && CRAWL_SPEED_SCALE < HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE
+        && HOBBLE_SPEED_SCALE < 1.0
+        && WANDER_PERIOD > 0,
+    "dismember locomotion: 0 < CRAWL < HOBBLE < 1 and WANDER_PERIOD > 0",
+);
+
 /// Player on-foot movement speed in m/s. 5.0 ≈ jog pace; placeholder for
 /// the 0.3.5 Driveable Slice on-foot avatar. Replaced by per-vehicle speed
 /// curves once ADR-017 vehicle physics lands.
